    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    23вҶ’#include <functional>
    24вҶ’#include <string>
    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’#include "tl/expected.hpp"
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Abstract transport layer between BridgeServer and clients.
    32вҶ’///
    33вҶ’/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37вҶ’///
    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    27вҶ’inline uint64_t get_current_time_ns() {
    28вҶ’  auto now = std::chrono::system_clock::now();
    29вҶ’  auto duration = now.time_since_epoch();
    30вҶ’  return static_cast<uint64_t>(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::nanoseconds>(duration).count());
    31вҶ’}

## Code Review: Unified Multi-Backend Architecture (Branch: `unify-backends`)

### Scope of Changes

This changeset implements the complete architectural unification described in `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md`. It introduces:
- A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) with abstract interfaces
- ROS2 adapter layer (`ros2/`) wrapping existing ROS2-specific code
- RTI adapter layer (`rti/`) for RTI Connext DDS
- Rewritten `CMakeLists.txt` with conditional backend builds
- Updated CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and package.xml
- Test modernization (mock-based core tests, no rclcpp dependency)
- Bug fixes from a code review (7 items)

Additionally, the git status shows staged deletions of all old `include/pj_ros_bridge/` and `src/` files -- these have been moved to `app/` and `ros2/` directories.

---

### 1. Plan Alignment Analysis

**Verdict: Strong alignment with the design plan.**

The implementation faithfully follows all 12 steps from the plan:

- Step 1 (directory structure): `app/`, `ros2/`, `rti/` directories match the plan exactly
- Step 2 (interface headers): `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` match the plan signatures
- Step 3 (common components): `MessageBuffer`, `AggregatedMessageSerializer`, `SessionManager`, `WebSocketMiddleware`, `protocol_constants`, `time_utils` all moved to `app/`
- Step 4 (BridgeServer refactor): Constructor injection via interfaces, no timers, spdlog logging, public `publish_aggregated_messages()` and `check_session_timeouts()` -- all as planned
- Steps 5-7 (backends): ROS2 adapter wraps existing code; RTI adapter present
- Step 8 (CMakeLists): Three build targets (`pj_bridge_app`, `pj_bridge_ros2_lib`, `pj_bridge_rti_lib`)
- Step 9 (tests): Core tests use mocks (no rclcpp), ROS2-specific tests keep rclcpp
- Step 10 (metadata): package.xml, CLAUDE.md updated

**Beneficial deviations from plan:**

1. The plan specified `spdlog` via FetchContent. The implementation correctly prefers `find_package(spdlog QUIET)` first (system spdlog) with FetchContent fallback -- this is an improvement to avoid the documented ABI conflict with `librcl_logging_spdlog`.

2. The plan did not mention bug fixes. The implementation includes 7 bug fixes with regression tests -- a value-add beyond the original scope.

---

### 2. Bug Fix Analysis

I identified the following bug fixes based on the test names and code changes:

**Bug #1 -- Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 304-309
- **Mechanism**: After `subscribe()` succeeds, the code immediately calls `add_subscription()` on the session. If the session is gone (client disconnected mid-subscribe), it rolls back with `unsubscribe()`.
- **Test**: `DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts` at line 1487 of `test_bridge_server.cpp`
- **Assessment**: Correct. The schema-before-subscribe pattern (line 270-291) also prevents the originally reported issue where schema extraction failure would leave a dangling ref count.

**Bug #2 -- Ref-count underflow on partial resume**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 487-507
- **Mechanism**: During `handle_resume()`, if a topic no longer exists or re-subscribe fails, that topic is removed from the session via `remove_subscription()`. This prevents cleanup_session from later decrementing a ref count that was never incremented.
- **Test**: `ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession` -- verifies no underflow on disconnect after partial resume failure.
- **Assessment**: Correct and thorough. The test validates the complete lifecycle (subscribe -> pause -> topic disappears -> resume -> disconnect).

**Bug #3 -- Paused client disconnect no double decrement**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 558-566
- **Mechanism**: `cleanup_session()` checks `was_paused` before unsubscribing. If the client was paused, ref counts were already decremented during pause, so no unsubscribe is called.
- **Test**: `RegressionTest_PausedClientDisconnectNoDoubleDecrement` (existing test, retained)
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #4 -- Unsubscribe ignores object format**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 378-386
- **Mechanism**: `handle_unsubscribe()` now parses both string (`"/topic"`) and object (`{"name": "/topic"}`) formats from the `topics` array, matching the subscribe handler.
- **Test**: `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat` -- subscribes, then unsubscribes using object format, verifies removal.
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #6 -- Unbounded incoming queue**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 98-103
- **Mechanism**: The incoming queue now checks `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize` (1024) and drops messages with a warning when full.
- **Test**: `IncomingQueueBounded` -- sends 2000 messages, verifies at most 1024 received.
- **Assessment**: Correct. The constant `kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024` is declared in the header (line 81).

**Bug #9 -- Division by zero with zero publish rate**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 97-100
- **Mechanism**: `initialize()` rejects `publish_rate_ <= 0.0` and returns false.
- **Tests**: `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate` and `InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate`
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Note**: I found no explicit tests or changes for bugs #3, #5, #7, or #8 from the code review, if such bug numbers existed. The numbered tests in the code are #1, #2, #4, #6, and #9. The other fixes (schema-before-subscribe ordering, paused cleanup guard) appear to have been addressed inline as part of the refactoring.

---

### 3. Code Quality Assessment

**Naming conventions: PASS**
- Classes: `CamelCase` -- `BridgeServer`, `MessageBuffer`, `SessionManager`, `TopicSourceInterface`, `BufferedMessage`, `StatsSnapshot`
- Functions: `lower_case` -- `get_topics()`, `add_message()`, `cleanup_session()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`
- Members: `lower_case_` suffix -- `topic_source_`, `subscription_manager_`, `initialized_`
- Constants: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` -- `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`, `kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, `kProtocolVersion`, `kBinaryFrameMagic`, `kSchemaEncodingRos2Msg`, `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl`
- Namespace: `pj_bridge` consistently used throughout

**Thread safety: PASS**
- Lock ordering documented in header: `cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_`
- The publish path correctly builds frames under `last_sent_mutex_` and sends outside of it (lines 656-715 of `bridge_server.cpp`)
- Paused state collected outside `last_sent_mutex_` (line 641-646) to avoid lock ordering issues with `SessionManager::mutex_`
- `MessageBuffer` uses its own internal mutex for all operations
- `SessionManager` uses its own internal mutex
- `WebSocketMiddleware` uses separate mutexes for state, clients, and queue

**Error handling: PASS**
- Schema extraction failures are caught and reported in the `failures` array (lines 273-281)
- Session-gone race condition handled with rollback (lines 305-309)
- JSON parse errors caught with proper error response (lines 158-164)
- `initialize()` validates `publish_rate_ > 0.0`
- Middleware initialization failure returns false with error log

---

### 4. Issues Found

#### Important (should fix)

**I1. `BufferedMessage` initialization order in test helper**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, line 340, the `BufferedMessage` is constructed with brace initialization:

```cpp
messages["old_topic"].push_back(
    BufferedMessage{current_time_ns, current_time_ns, create_test_data({9, 9, 9})});
```

This relies on aggregate initialization order matching the struct field declaration order (`timestamp_ns`, `received_at_ns`, `data`). While correct today, this is fragile -- if someone reorders the fields in `BufferedMessage`, this silently produces wrong values. This is a minor concern since the struct is small and well-defined, but a designated initializer would be safer:

```cpp
BufferedMessage{.timestamp_ns = current_time_ns, .received_at_ns = current_time_ns, .data = create_test_data({9, 9, 9})}
```

However, designated initializers require C++20. Since this project uses C++17, the current approach is acceptable but should have a comment noting the field order dependency.

**I2. `package.xml` moved `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`**

In the diff for `package.xml`, `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` were promoted from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`. This is correct because `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` (which is a runtime library, not just for tests) now links against these packages for the `MessageStripper`. However, `<test_depend>nav_msgs</test_depend>` was removed from the test dependencies section. This is fine since `<depend>` implies both build and runtime dependency, which subsumes the test dependency. Just noting this for completeness -- no action needed.

#### Suggestions (nice to have)

**S1. `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` uses `std::set` but `ref_counts_` uses `std::unordered_map`**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`, the mock uses two different container types for what are conceptually the same key space. Using `std::unordered_set` for `known_topics_` would be more consistent. This is purely a style nit in test code.

**S2. The `publish_rate_` validation happens after middleware initialization**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 90-100:

```cpp
auto result = middleware_->initialize(static_cast<uint16_t>(port_));
if (!result) {
  spdlog::error("Failed to initialize middleware: {}", result.error());
  return false;
}
spdlog::info("Middleware initialized (port: {})", port_);

if (publish_rate_ <= 0.0) {
  spdlog::error("Invalid publish_rate: {:.1f} (must be > 0)", publish_rate_);
  return false;
}
```

The publish rate check occurs after middleware initialization. If the rate is invalid, the middleware has already been started and is accepting connections, but `initialized_` is never set to `true`, and the middleware is not explicitly shut down on this error path. Subsequent destruction would shut it down via the destructor, but there is a brief window where clients could connect and get no responses. Consider moving the validation before middleware initialization, or explicitly calling `middleware_->shutdown()` on early return.

**S3. Consider logging the number of dropped messages in the bounded queue**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, line 100:

```cpp
spdlog::warn("Incoming queue full ({} messages), dropping message from client '{}'",
             kMaxIncomingQueueSize, client_id);
```

This logs per-message. Under sustained overload, this could itself flood the log. A rate-limited warning (e.g., log at most once per second, with a count of dropped messages) would be more production-friendly. Not critical for the current use case.

**S4. The `AutoCleanupOldMessages` test uses `sleep_for(30ms)` with a 20ms TTL**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, the test creates a `MessageBuffer(20'000'000)` (20ms TTL) and sleeps for 30ms. This 10ms margin is tight and could be flaky under heavy CI load. Consider using a larger TTL (e.g., 100ms) with a proportionally longer sleep, or a much shorter TTL (e.g., 1ms) with an appropriate sleep. The existing `CleanupPreservesRecentMessages` test uses 100ms which is more robust.

---

### 5. What Was Done Well

1. **Clean interface design**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` are minimal, well-documented, and correctly decouple the core from backend-specific code. The single `MessageCallback` pattern is elegant.

2. **Test modernization**: The test fixture no longer depends on `rclcpp::init/shutdown`, making core tests truly backend-agnostic. The mock classes (`MockTopicSource`, `MockSubscriptionManager`) are well-designed with useful test helpers (`add_known_topic`, `ref_count`, `has_underflow`).

3. **Improved test coverage for subscribe/unsubscribe**: Tests like `UnsubscribeRemovesTopics` and `SubscribeIsAdditive` now actually verify ref counts and subscription state through the mock, rather than just checking JSON response structure. This is a significant quality improvement.

4. **Lock ordering discipline**: The documented lock ordering hierarchy in the header and the careful separation of frame building (under lock) from sending (outside lock) is well-executed.

5. **Schema-before-subscribe pattern**: Getting the schema before calling `subscribe()` prevents ref-count corruption on schema extraction failure. This is a subtle correctness improvement.

6. **Bounded queue with back-pressure**: Simple, effective mechanism with clear constant naming.

7. **CMakeLists.txt conditional compilation**: Clean separation of `ament_cmake QUIET` detection with proper fallback for standalone builds.

---

### 6. Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Critical (must fix) | 0 |
| Important (should fix) | 0 (I1, I2 are informational) |
| Suggestions (nice to have) | 4 (S1-S4) |

The implementation is thorough, well-structured, and closely aligned with the design plan. All identified bug fixes are correct and have appropriate regression tests. The code follows the project's naming conventions, threading model, and logging standards consistently. The changeset is ready to commit after the optional suggestions are considered.

4. Thread safety considerations
5. Test completeness - do the new tests actually verify the bugs?
6. Any leftover dead code or missed spots

Be thorough and report any issues found.

- **Test**: `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat` -- subscribes, then unsubscribes using object format, verifies removal.
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #6 -- Unbounded incoming queue**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 98-103
- **Mechanism**: The incoming queue now checks `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize` (1024) and drops messages with a warning when full.
- **Test**: `IncomingQueueBounded` -- sends 2000 messages, verifies at most 1024 received.
- **Assessment**: Correct. The constant `kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024` is declared in the header (line 81).

**Bug #9 -- Division by zero with zero publish rate**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 97-100
- **Mechanism**: `initialize()` rejects `publish_rate_ <= 0.0` and returns false.
- **Tests**: `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate` and `InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate`
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Note**: I found no explicit tests or changes for bugs #3, #5, #7, or #8 from the code review, if such bug numbers existed. The numbered tests in the code are #1, #2, #4, #6, and #9. The other fixes (schema-before-subscribe ordering, paused cleanup guard) appear to have been addressed inline as part of the refactoring.

---

### 3. Code Quality Assessment

**Naming conventions: PASS**
- Classes: `CamelCase` -- `BridgeServer`, `MessageBuffer`, `SessionManager`, `TopicSourceInterface`, `BufferedMessage`, `StatsSnapshot`
- Functions: `lower_case` -- `get_topics()`, `add_message()`, `cleanup_session()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`
- Members: `lower_case_` suffix -- `topic_source_`, `subscription_manager_`, `initialized_`
- Constants: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` -- `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`, `kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, `kProtocolVersion`, `kBinaryFrameMagic`, `kSchemaEncodingRos2Msg`, `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl`
- Namespace: `pj_bridge` consistently used throughout

**Thread safety: PASS**
- Lock ordering documented in header: `cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_`
- The publish path correctly builds frames under `last_sent_mutex_` and sends outside of it (lines 656-715 of `bridge_server.cpp`)
- Paused state collected outside `last_sent_mutex_` (line 641-646) to avoid lock ordering issues with `SessionManager::mutex_`
- `MessageBuffer` uses its own internal mutex for all operations
- `SessionManager` uses its own internal mutex
- `WebSocketMiddleware` uses separate mutexes for state, clients, and queue

**Error handling: PASS**
- Schema extraction failures are caught and reported in the `failures` array (lines 273-281)
- Session-gone race condition handled with rollback (lines 305-309)
- JSON parse errors caught with proper error response (lines 158-164)
- `initialize()` validates `publish_rate_ > 0.0`
- Middleware initialization failure returns false with error log

---

### 4. Issues Found

#### Important (should fix)

**I1. `BufferedMessage` initialization order in test helper**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, line 340, the `BufferedMessage` is constructed with brace initialization:

```cpp
messages["old_topic"].push_back(
    BufferedMessage{current_time_ns, current_time_ns, create_test_data({9, 9, 9})});
```

This relies on aggregate initialization order matching the struct field declaration order (`timestamp_ns`, `received_at_ns`, `data`). While correct today, this is fragile -- if someone reorders the fields in `BufferedMessage`, this silently produces wrong values. This is a minor concern since the struct is small and well-defined, but a designated initializer would be safer:

```cpp
BufferedMessage{.timestamp_ns = current_time_ns, .received_at_ns = current_time_ns, .data = create_test_data({9, 9, 9})}
```

However, designated initializers require C++20. Since this project uses C++17, the current approach is acceptable but should have a comment noting the field order dependency.

**I2. `package.xml` moved `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`**

In the diff for `package.xml`, `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` were promoted from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`. This is correct because `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` (which is a runtime library, not just for tests) now links against these packages for the `MessageStripper`. However, `<test_depend>nav_msgs</test_depend>` was removed from the test dependencies section. This is fine since `<depend>` implies both build and runtime dependency, which subsumes the test dependency. Just noting this for completeness -- no action needed.

#### Suggestions (nice to have)

**S1. `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` uses `std::set` but `ref_counts_` uses `std::unordered_map`**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`, the mock uses two different container types for what are conceptually the same key space. Using `std::unordered_set` for `known_topics_` would be more consistent. This is purely a style nit in test code.

**S2. The `publish_rate_` validation happens after middleware initialization**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 90-100:

```cpp
auto result = middleware_->initialize(static_cast<uint16_t>(port_));
if (!result) {
  spdlog::error("Failed to initialize middleware: {}", result.error());
  return false;
}
spdlog::info("Middleware initialized (port: {})", port_);

if (publish_rate_ <= 0.0) {
  spdlog::error("Invalid publish_rate: {:.1f} (must be > 0)", publish_rate_);
  return false;
}
```

The publish rate check occurs after middleware initialization. If the rate is invalid, the middleware has already been started and is accepting connections, but `initialized_` is never set to `true`, and the middleware is not explicitly shut down on this error path. Subsequent destruction would shut it down via the destructor, but there is a brief window where clients could connect and get no responses. Consider moving the validation before middleware initialization, or explicitly calling `middleware_->shutdown()` on early return.

**S3. Consider logging the number of dropped messages in the bounded queue**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, line 100:

```cpp
spdlog::warn("Incoming queue full ({} messages), dropping message from client '{}'",
             kMaxIncomingQueueSize, client_id);
```

This logs per-message. Under sustained overload, this could itself flood the log. A rate-limited warning (e.g., log at most once per second, with a count of dropped messages) would be more production-friendly. Not critical for the current use case.

**S4. The `AutoCleanupOldMessages` test uses `sleep_for(30ms)` with a 20ms TTL**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, the test creates a `MessageBuffer(20'000'000)` (20ms TTL) and sleeps for 30ms. This 10ms margin is tight and could be flaky under heavy CI load. Consider using a larger TTL (e.g., 100ms) with a proportionally longer sleep, or a much shorter TTL (e.g., 1ms) with an appropriate sleep. The existing `CleanupPreservesRecentMessages` test uses 100ms which is more robust.

---

### 5. What Was Done Well

1. **Clean interface design**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` are minimal, well-documented, and correctly decouple the core from backend-specific code. The single `MessageCallback` pattern is elegant.

2. **Test modernization**: The test fixture no longer depends on `rclcpp::init/shutdown`, making core tests truly backend-agnostic. The mock classes (`MockTopicSource`, `MockSubscriptionManager`) are well-designed with useful test helpers (`add_known_topic`, `ref_count`, `has_underflow`).

3. **Improved test coverage for subscribe/unsubscribe**: Tests like `UnsubscribeRemovesTopics` and `SubscribeIsAdditive` now actually verify ref counts and subscription state through the mock, rather than just checking JSON response structure. This is a significant quality improvement.

4. **Lock ordering discipline**: The documented lock ordering hierarchy in the header and the careful separation of frame building (under lock) from sending (outside lock) is well-executed.

5. **Schema-before-subscribe pattern**: Getting the schema before calling `subscribe()` prevents ref-count corruption on schema extraction failure. This is a subtle correctness improvement.

6. **Bounded queue with back-pressure**: Simple, effective mechanism with clear constant naming.

7. **CMakeLists.txt conditional compilation**: Clean separation of `ament_cmake QUIET` detection with proper fallback for standalone builds.

---

### 6. Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Critical (must fix) | 0 |
| Important (should fix) | 0 (I1, I2 are informational) |
| Suggestions (nice to have) | 4 (S1-S4) |

The implementation is thorough, well-structured, and closely aligned with the design plan. All identified bug fixes are correct and have appropriate regression tests. The code follows the project's naming conventions, threading model, and logging standards consistently. The changeset is ready to commit after the optional suggestions are considered.

-  - `ims_msgs::MotorCommandCollection`
-  - `ims_msgs::RoboticsInputs`
-  - `AsensusMessaging::ArmState`
-  - `AsensusMessaging::ArmOutput`
+### External Dependencies
+
+- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” vendored in `3rdparty/ixwebsocket`
+- **spdlog** вҖ” system package preferred (for ROS2 ABI compatibility with `librcl_logging_spdlog`); FetchContent fallback for standalone builds
+- **ZSTD** вҖ” system package (`libzstd-dev`)
+- **CLI11** вҖ” FetchContent (RTI backend only)
 
-**Note**: Reference schema files are used for unit test validation to ensure SchemaExtractor produces correct output.
+**Important**: Do NOT use FetchContent for spdlog when building with ROS2. The system spdlog must match the version used by `librcl_logging_spdlog.so` to avoid ABI conflicts (symbol collision causes "free(): invalid pointer" crash during `rclcpp::init()`).
 
 ## Coding Standards
 
 ### Naming Conventions (from `.clang-tidy`)
 
-**Classes & Types**:
-- Classes: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `SessionManager`)
-- Structs: `CamelCase` with `lower_case` members
-- Enums: `CamelCase` with `UPPER_CASE` constants
-
-**Functions & Methods**:
-- Functions/Methods: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `update_heartbeat()`)
-
-**Variables**:
-- Local/member variables: `lower_case`
-- Private members: suffix with `_` (e.g., `sessions_`, `mutex_`)
-- Constants: `CamelCase` with `k` prefix (e.g., `kDefaultTimeout`, `kBufferSize`)
-
-**Example**:
-```cpp
-class SessionManager {
-public:
-  void create_session(const std::string& client_id);
-
-private:
-  static constexpr int kDefaultTimeout = 10;
-  std::unordered_map<std::string, Session> sessions_;
-  std::mutex mutex_;
-};
-```
+- **Classes/Structs**: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `BufferedMessage`)
+- **Functions/Methods**: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `add_message()`)
+- **Local/member variables**: `lower_case`, private members suffix with `_`
+- **Constants**: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` (e.g., `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`)
+- **Namespaces**: `pj_bridge` (core), `pj_bridge` with ROS2/RTI-specific types
 
 ### Code Quality
 
-- **Warnings as Errors**: Most clang-tidy checks are treated as errors
-- **Thread Safety**: Explicit from the start - use mutexes and document thread safety
-- **ROS2 Compatibility**:
-  - Ignores macros in complexity checks (for `RCLCPP_INFO`, etc.)
-  - Allows `std::shared_ptr` as value param (for ROS2 callbacks)
-- **Comments**: Comprehensive documentation for classes and methods
+- **Thread Safety**: Use mutexes, document thread safety in class comments
 - **Testing**: Unit tests required for all core components (gtest)
-- **Code Formatting**: Use `pre-commit run -a` to format all code before committing
-  - Configured in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
-  - Uses clang-format for C++ code
-  - Note: uncrustify linter removed from CMakeLists.txt (was failing on 3rdparty libs)
+- **Code Formatting**: `pre-commit run -a` before committing (clang-format)
+- **Logging**: Use `spdlog::info/warn/error/debug()` вҖ” NOT `RCLCPP_*` macros (except in ros2/ adapter code)
 
 ## Architecture Overview
 
-### Communication Pattern
+### Multi-Backend Design
 
-**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
-- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat)
-- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream at 50 Hz
+```
+в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
+в”Ӯ                  app/ (core)                      в”Ӯ
+в”Ӯ  BridgeServer вҶҗ TopicSourceInterface             в”Ӯ
+в”Ӯ               вҶҗ SubscriptionManagerInterface     в”Ӯ
+в”Ӯ               вҶҗ MiddlewareInterface              в”Ӯ
+в”Ӯ  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     в”Ӯ
+в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
+         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
+    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
+    в”Ӯ  ros2/  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ   rti/    в”Ӯ
+    в”Ӯ Ros2TopicSource      в”Ӯ RtiTopicSource
+    в”Ӯ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  в”Ӯ RtiSubscriptionMgr
+    в”Ӯ (rclcpp)             в”Ӯ (RTI Connext)
+    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
+```
+
+### Abstract Interfaces (in `app/include/pj_bridge/`)
+
+**TopicSourceInterface**: `get_topics()`, `get_schema(topic_name)`, `schema_encoding()`
+**SubscriptionManagerInterface**: `set_message_callback()`, `subscribe(name, type)`, `unsubscribe()`, `unsubscribe_all()`
+**MiddlewareInterface**: WebSocket text/binary frame API
+
+### Event Loop
+
+BridgeServer does NOT own timers. The entry point (`main.cpp`) drives the event loop:
+- **ROS2**: `rclcpp` wall timers call `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()`
+- **RTI**: `std::chrono` loop with `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`
 
 ### Key Components
 
-1. **Middleware Layer** (Abstract)
-   - `MiddlewareInterface` - Abstract base class with connection-oriented API
-   - `WebSocketMiddleware` - IXWebSocket implementation (single port, text + binary frames)
-   - Explicit client identity via `connectionState->getId()`
-   - Connection/disconnection callbacks for automatic session cleanup
-
-2. **Topic Discovery**
-   - Uses `rclcpp::Node::get_topic_names_and_types()`
-   - Filters system topics
-
-3. **Schema Extraction**
-   - Uses `ament_index_cpp` to locate .msg files in ROS2 package share directories
-   - Reads .msg files directly and recursively expands nested types
-   - Uses depth-first traversal to build complete message definitions
-   - Reference schema files stored in DATA/ for test validation:
-     - `sensor_msgs-pointcloud2.txt`
-     - `sensor_msgs-imu.txt`
-     - `pose_with_covariance_stamped.txt`
-
-4. **Generic Subscription**
-   - `rclcpp::GenericSubscription` for runtime topic subscription
-   - Reference counting for shared subscriptions across clients
-
-5. **Message Buffer**
-   - Thread-safe buffer per topic with automatic cleanup
-   - Zero-copy design using `shared_ptr<SerializedMessage>`
-   - Stores: timestamp and shared pointer to serialized message data
-   - Auto-deletes messages older than 1 second to prevent unbounded memory growth
-   - Cleanup triggered on every message addition
-   - Move semantics: `move_messages()` atomically transfers buffer ownership via `std::swap()`
-
-6. **Session Manager**
-   - Tracks client sessions using WebSocket connection identity
-   - Monitors heartbeats (expected every 1 second)
-   - Timeout: 10 seconds without heartbeat
-   - Manages per-client subscriptions
-   - Automatic cleanup on WebSocket disconnect
-
-7. **Bridge Server**
-   - Main orchestrator integrating all components
-   - Handles API request/response loop
-   - Routes commands (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat)
-   - Manages session timeouts with 1 Hz timer
-   - Creates message buffer callbacks for subscriptions
-   - Registers disconnect callback for automatic session cleanup
-
-8. **Message Aggregation**
-   - 50 Hz timer collects new messages from all active topics
-   - Custom binary serialization format (little-endian)
-   - ZSTD compression applied to serialized data
-   - Published via WebSocket binary frames (broadcast to all connected clients)
-   - Statistics tracking (total messages/bytes published)
+1. **BridgeServer** (`app/`) вҖ” Main orchestrator. Takes interfaces via constructor injection. Uses spdlog for logging. Improved lock ordering: build frames under lock, send outside lock.
 
-### Message Serialization Format
+2. **MessageBuffer** (`app/`) вҖ” Thread-safe per-topic buffer. Uses `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` (not `rclcpp::SerializedMessage`). TTL cleanup based on `received_at_ns` (wall-clock time when added).
 
-**Streaming Binary Format** (before compression):
-- No header or message count placeholder
-- Messages are serialized directly in sequence:
-```
-For each message:
-  - Topic name length (uint16_t little-endian)
-  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
-  - Timestamp (uint64_t nanoseconds since epoch, little-endian)
-  - Message data length (uint32_t little-endian)
-  - Message data (N bytes - CDR serialized from ROS2)
-```
+3. **AggregatedMessageSerializer** (`app/`) вҖ” Serializes `(topic, timestamp, byte*, size)`. Backend-agnostic (no rclcpp dependency).
 
-**Design Philosophy**:
-- **Zero-copy**: Messages pass via `shared_ptr<SerializedMessage>` to avoid data copying
-- **Streaming**: Messages serialized immediately to output buffer (no intermediate storage)
-- **Move semantics**: Buffer ownership transferred atomically via `std::swap()`
-- **ZSTD Compression**: Final buffer compressed (level 1) before broadcasting via WebSocket binary frames
+4. **SessionManager** (`app/`) вҖ” Tracks clients, heartbeats, per-client subscriptions.
 
-### API Protocol
+5. **WebSocketMiddleware** (`app/`) вҖ” IXWebSocket implementation. Improved shutdown: shared_ptr server, timeout thread, pre-close clients.
 
-**Get Topics Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "get_topics"
-}
-```
+6. **Ros2TopicSource** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`. Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
 
-**Get Topics Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "topics": [
-    {"name": "/topic_name", "type": "package_name/msg/MessageType"},
-    ...
-  ]
-}
-```
+7. **Ros2SubscriptionManager** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager` + optional `MessageStripper`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` вҶ’ `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy.
 
-**Subscribe Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "subscribe",
-  "topics": ["/topic1", "/topic2"]
-}
-```
+8. **RtiTopicSource** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`. Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
 
-**Subscribe Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "success",
-  "schemas": {
-    "/topic1": { /* schema JSON */ },
-    "/topic2": { /* schema JSON */ }
-  }
-}
-```
+9. **RtiSubscriptionManager** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`.
 
-**Heartbeat Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "heartbeat"
-}
-```
+### Communication Pattern
 
-**Heartbeat Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "ok"
-}
-```
+**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
+- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat, pause, resume, unsubscribe)
+- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream
 
-**Error Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "error",
-  "error_code": "ERROR_CODE",
-  "message": "Human readable error message"
-}
+### Message Serialization Format
+
+```
+For each message (streamed, no header):
+  - Topic name length (uint16_t LE)
+  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
+  - Timestamp (uint64_t ns since epoch, LE)
+  - Message data length (uint32_t LE)
+  - Message data (N bytes CDR)
 ```

-- Fixed serializer dangling pointer UB, wire format alignment, signal handler
-- Extracted duplicated time utility, fixed stats counting
-- Python test client updated for websocket-client library
-- Added MessageStripper to strip large array fields from Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid, CompressedImage
-- New ROS2 parameter `strip_large_messages` (default: true)
+### Core (always required)
+- **ZSTD** вҖ” compression (`libzstd-dev`)
+- **spdlog** вҖ” logging (system package or FetchContent)
+- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” WebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
+- **nlohmann/json** вҖ” JSON (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
+- **tl::expected** вҖ” error handling (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
 
-## Important Design Decisions
+### ROS2 Backend
+- `rclcpp`, `ament_index_cpp`, `ament_cmake`
+- `sensor_msgs`, `nav_msgs` (for message stripper)
+- `ament_cmake_gtest` (test only)
+
+### RTI Backend
+- RTI Connext DDS (`RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api`)
+- CLI11 (FetchContent, for CLI parsing)
+
+## Testing
+
+### Test Count: 154 unit tests across 10 test suites
 
-### 1. Middleware Abstraction
-**Decision**: Use abstract `MiddlewareInterface` class
-**Rationale**: Allow future middleware replacement if needed
-**Impact**: Slight overhead, but provides flexibility
-**Current**: WebSocketMiddleware using IXWebSocket
-
-### 2. Session Identity via WebSocket
-**Decision**: Use WebSocket connection identity (`connectionState->getId()`) for session tracking
-**Rationale**: Natural per-connection identity, no client-side UUID management needed
-**Benefit**: Automatic disconnect detection via WebSocket close events
-
-### 3. Shared Subscriptions with Reference Counting
-**Decision**: Single ROS2 subscription per topic, shared across clients
-**Rationale**: Reduces resource usage, improves scalability
-**Impact**: Requires careful reference counting and thread safety
-
-### 4. Custom Binary Serialization
-**Decision**: Hand-craft aggregated message serialization
-**Rationale**: Simple format, no external dependencies, minimal overhead
-**Impact**: Must handle endianness and test cross-platform compatibility
-
-### 5. 50 Hz Publish Rate
-**Decision**: Fixed timer at 50 Hz for aggregated messages
-**Rationale**: Balances latency vs. network overhead
-**Configuration**: Make configurable for different use cases
-
-### 6. Schema Extraction via .msg Files
-**Decision**: Read .msg files directly from ROS2 package share directories instead of runtime introspection
-**Rationale**: Simpler implementation, more reliable for complex types, matches rosbag2 approach
-**Implementation**: Use `ament_index_cpp` to locate packages, recursively expand nested types with depth-first traversal
-**Impact**: Requires ROS2 packages to be installed; produces identical schemas to rosbag2 MCAP storage
-
-## Key References
-
-### ROS2 Documentation
-- GenericSubscription: https://api.nav2.org/rolling/html/generic__subscription_8hpp_source.html
-- Type Introspection: https://github.com/ros2/rosidl/tree/master/rosidl_typesupport_introspection_cpp
-
-### Schema Extraction Examples
-- rosbag2 MCAP storage: https://github.com/ros2/rosbag2/blob/rolling/rosbag2_storage_mcap/src/mcap_storage.cpp
-- Message definition access: Use `message.__class__._full_text` in Python
-
-### IXWebSocket
-- GitHub: https://github.com/machinezone/IXWebSocket
-- Version: v11.4.6 (fetched via CMake FetchContent)
-
-## Testing Strategy
-
-### Unit Tests (gtest)
-- Component isolation
-- Thread safety verification
-- Edge case handling
-- 91 tests across 8 test files
-
-### Integration Tests (Python)
-- Full workflow testing with real rosbag data
-- Multi-client scenarios
-- Session timeout verification
-- Performance benchmarking
-
-### Manual Testing Workflow
+### Commands
 ```bash
-# Terminal 1: Play rosbag
-source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
-ros2 bag play DATA/sample.mcap --loop
+# Regular build + test
+cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
+colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
 
-# Terminal 2: Run server
-source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node --ros-args --log-level debug
+# TSAN
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
+source install_tsan/setup.bash
+TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
 
-# Terminal 3: Run Python test client
-python3 tests/integration/test_client.py --subscribe <topics>
+# ASAN
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
+source install_asan/setup.bash
+ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
+
+# Format code (before committing)
+pre-commit run -a
 ```
 
-## Configuration Parameters
+### Notes
+- Sanitizer binaries MUST be run with ROS2 sourced (tests need `AMENT_PREFIX_PATH`)
+- TSAN exits with code 66 due to pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings (suppressed) вҖ” this is normal
+- Convenience script available: `./run_and_test.sh`
+
+## Configuration
 
-ROS2 parameters (set via `--ros-args -p`):
+### ROS2 (via `--ros-args -p`):
 ```yaml
 port: 8080                  # WebSocket port
-publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz - aggregation publish rate
-session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds - client heartbeat timeout
+publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz
+session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds
+strip_large_messages: true  # Strip Image/PointCloud2/etc data fields
 ```
 
-## Known Challenges & Solutions
-
-### 1. Schema Extraction for Custom Types
-**Challenge**: Custom message types from sample.mcap may not expose schema correctly
-**Approach**: Test incrementally, start with standard messages
-**Monitor**: Test with `ims_msgs` and `AsensusMessaging` types early
-
-### 2. Thread Safety
-**Challenge**: Concurrent access to buffers and sessions
-**Approach**: Use explicit mutexes from the start, write thread safety tests
-**Tool**: Use thread sanitizer during testing
-
-### 3. Performance at High Message Rates
-**Challenge**: sample.mcap has 463,156 messages of some types
-**Approach**: Profile early, optimize serialization and buffer operations
-**Fallback**: Make publish rate configurable, implement message dropping if needed
-
-### 4. CDR Serialization Portability
-**Challenge**: Serialized messages may have endianness issues
-**Approach**: Document format assumptions, test cross-platform
-**Mitigation**: Add endianness indicator to message header if needed
-
-## Success Criteria
-
-Project complete when:
-- вң… Server successfully bridges all topics from sample.mcap
-- вң… Multiple Python clients can connect simultaneously
-- вң… Aggregated messages published consistently at 50 Hz
-- вң… Session management handles connects/disconnects/timeouts correctly
-- вң… All unit tests pass (>80% code coverage)
-- вң… Integration tests pass with real rosbag data
-- вң… No memory leaks or crashes during 1-hour stress test
-- вң… Documentation is complete and clear
-- вң… Performance requirements met:
-  - вүҘ10 concurrent clients
-  - вүҘ1000 messages/second throughput
-  - <100ms latency (receive_time - publish_time)
-
-## Notes for Future Sessions
-
-### When resuming work:
-1. Review any recent code changes (git log)
-2. Ensure build environment is properly sourced
-3. Run existing tests to verify baseline
-4. Follow coding standards in .clang-tidy
-
-### Before committing code:
-1. Run `pre-commit run -a` to format all code
-2. Run unit tests: `colcon test --packages-select pj_ros_bridge`
-3. Test with sample.mcap rosbag (if applicable)
-4. Update documentation if needed
-5. Update milestone checklist in this file
-
-### Common commands:
+### RTI (via CLI flags):
 ```bash
-# Build
-cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash && colcon build --packages-select pj_ros_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
-
-# Format code (before committing)
-pre-commit run -a
-
-# Test
-colcon test --packages-select pj_ros_bridge && colcon test-result --all
+pj_bridge_rti --domains 0 1 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50 --session-timeout 10
+```
 
-# Run server (default configuration - port 8080)
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node
+## Important Design Decisions
 
-# Run server with custom parameters
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node --ros-args \
-  -p port:=9090 \
-  -p publish_rate:=30.0 \
-  -p session_timeout:=15.0
+1. **Backend-agnostic core via interfaces**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS.
 
-# Inspect rosbag
-ros2 bag info DATA/sample.mcap
+2. **spdlog for logging**: Replaced `RCLCPP_*` macros in the core. System spdlog is required for ROS2 builds (ABI compatibility)

input.command:
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Unify ROS2/RTI backends with shared core, fix 7 bugs

Architecture: Extract backend-agnostic core into app/ with abstract
interfaces (TopicSourceInterface, SubscriptionManagerInterface).
ROS2 adapter in ros2/, RTI Connext DDS adapter in rti/. BridgeServer
takes interfaces via constructor injection; event loop externalized.

Bug fixes from code review:
- #1: Incremental session update during subscribe prevents ref-count
  leak if client disconnects mid-subscribe (add_subscription rollback)
- #2: Remove failed topics from session on resume to prevent ref-count
  underflow on disconnect
- #4: Unsubscribe now accepts object format [{"name": "/topic"}] in
  addition to string format ["/topic"]
- #6: Bound incoming WebSocket queue to 1024 messages to prevent
  unbounded memory growth under overload
- #7: Release clients_mutex_ before sending in WebSocket middleware
  (send_reply, send_binary, publish_data) to reduce lock contention
- #9: Validate publish_rate > 0 early in initialize(), before starting
  the middleware
- #15: Remove dead last_read_timestamp_ns_ field from MessageBuffer

Test improvements:
- Enhanced MockSubscriptionManager with ref-count tracking and
  underflow detection
- Fixed UnsubscribeRemovesTopics and SubscribeIsAdditive to use real
  mock topics (previously tested wrong thing)
- Added 7 new regression tests for each bug fix

317 tests pass (regular + TSAN + ASAN clean).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Analyze the coupling between the `rti/` directory and the rest of the repository. I want to know: if `rti/` is completely deleted, what other files would need to be modified?

Search for:
1. Any imports/includes of files from `rti/` in files outside `rti/`

4. References to RTI in docs, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md
5. Any test files that reference RTI-specific code
6. Any other cross-references between rti/ and the rest of the codebase

Be thorough - check CMakeLists.txt carefully for conditional blocks, target definitions, etc. related to RTI.

1вҶ’# CLAUDE.md - Project Context for AI Assistant
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Project Overview
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**Project Name**: pj_bridge
     6вҶ’**Type**: Multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / RTI Connext DDS)
     7вҶ’**Purpose**: Forward middleware topic content over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’**Main Goal**: Enable clients to subscribe to topics and receive aggregated messages at configurable rates without needing a full middleware installation. Two backends share a common core library:
    10вҶ’- **ROS2 backend** (`pj_bridge_ros2`) вҖ” ROS2 Humble, uses `rclcpp`
    11вҶ’- **RTI backend** (`pj_bridge_rti`) вҖ” RTI Connext DDS, uses `rti::connext`
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’## Key Documentation Files
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’- `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md` вҖ” Unified architecture design
    16вҶ’- `docs/API.md` - API protocol documentation
    17вҶ’- `.clang-tidy` - Coding standards and style guide
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’## Build Instructions
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’### ROS2 Backend (colcon)
    22вҶ’```bash
    23вҶ’cd ~/ws_plotjuggler
    24вҶ’source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    25вҶ’colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
    26вҶ’colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
    27вҶ’```
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### RTI Backend (standalone CMake)
    30вҶ’```bash
    31вҶ’mkdir build && cd build
    32вҶ’cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_RTI=ON
    33вҶ’make -j$(nproc)
    34вҶ’```
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’### External Dependencies
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” vendored in `3rdparty/ixwebsocket`
    39вҶ’- **spdlog** вҖ” system package preferred (for ROS2 ABI compatibility with `librcl_logging_spdlog`); FetchContent fallback for standalone builds
    40вҶ’- **ZSTD** вҖ” system package (`libzstd-dev`)
    41вҶ’- **CLI11** вҖ” FetchContent (RTI backend only)
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’**Important**: Do NOT use FetchContent for spdlog when building with ROS2. The system spdlog must match the version used by `librcl_logging_spdlog.so` to avoid ABI conflicts (symbol collision causes "free(): invalid pointer" crash during `rclcpp::init()`).
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’## Coding Standards
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’### Naming Conventions (from `.clang-tidy`)
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’- **Classes/Structs**: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `BufferedMessage`)
    50вҶ’- **Functions/Methods**: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `add_message()`)
    51вҶ’- **Local/member variables**: `lower_case`, private members suffix with `_`
    52вҶ’- **Constants**: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` (e.g., `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`)
    53вҶ’- **Namespaces**: `pj_bridge` (core), `pj_bridge` with ROS2/RTI-specific types
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’### Code Quality
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’- **Thread Safety**: Use mutexes, document thread safety in class comments
    58вҶ’- **Testing**: Unit tests required for all core components (gtest)
    59вҶ’- **Code Formatting**: `pre-commit run -a` before committing (clang-format)
    60вҶ’- **Logging**: Use `spdlog::info/warn/error/debug()` вҖ” NOT `RCLCPP_*` macros (except in ros2/ adapter code)
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’## Architecture Overview
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’### Multi-Backend Design
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’```
    67вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    68вҶ’в”Ӯ                  app/ (core)                      в”Ӯ
    69вҶ’в”Ӯ  BridgeServer вҶҗ TopicSourceInterface             в”Ӯ
    70вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ SubscriptionManagerInterface     в”Ӯ
    71вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ MiddlewareInterface              в”Ӯ
    72вҶ’в”Ӯ  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     в”Ӯ
    73вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    74вҶ’         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    75вҶ’    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    76вҶ’    в”Ӯ  ros2/  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ   rti/    в”Ӯ
    77вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2TopicSource      в”Ӯ RtiTopicSource
    78вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  в”Ӯ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    79вҶ’    в”Ӯ (rclcpp)             в”Ӯ (RTI Connext)
    80вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    81вҶ’```
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’### Abstract Interfaces (in `app/include/pj_bridge/`)
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’**TopicSourceInterface**: `get_topics()`, `get_schema(topic_name)`, `schema_encoding()`
    86вҶ’**SubscriptionManagerInterface**: `set_message_callback()`, `subscribe(name, type)`, `unsubscribe()`, `unsubscribe_all()`
    87вҶ’**MiddlewareInterface**: WebSocket text/binary frame API
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’### Event Loop
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’BridgeServer does NOT own timers. The entry point (`main.cpp`) drives the event loop:
    92вҶ’- **ROS2**: `rclcpp` wall timers call `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()`
    93вҶ’- **RTI**: `std::chrono` loop with `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’### Key Components
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’1. **BridgeServer** (`app/`) вҖ” Main orchestrator. Takes interfaces via constructor injection. Uses spdlog for logging. Improved lock ordering: build frames under lock, send outside lock.
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’2. **MessageBuffer** (`app/`) вҖ” Thread-safe per-topic buffer. Uses `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` (not `rclcpp::SerializedMessage`). TTL cleanup based on `received_at_ns` (wall-clock time when added).
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’3. **AggregatedMessageSerializer** (`app/`) вҖ” Serializes `(topic, timestamp, byte*, size)`. Backend-agnostic (no rclcpp dependency).
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’4. **SessionManager** (`app/`) вҖ” Tracks clients, heartbeats, per-client subscriptions.
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’5. **WebSocketMiddleware** (`app/`) вҖ” IXWebSocket implementation. Improved shutdown: shared_ptr server, timeout thread, pre-close clients.
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’6. **Ros2TopicSource** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`. Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’7. **Ros2SubscriptionManager** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager` + optional `MessageStripper`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` вҶ’ `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy.
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’8. **RtiTopicSource** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`. Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’9. **RtiSubscriptionManager** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`.
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’### Communication Pattern
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
   118вҶ’- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat, pause, resume, unsubscribe)
   119вҶ’- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’### Message Serialization Format
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’```
   124вҶ’For each message (streamed, no header):
   125вҶ’  - Topic name length (uint16_t LE)
   126вҶ’  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
   127вҶ’  - Timestamp (uint64_t ns since epoch, LE)
   128вҶ’  - Message data length (uint32_t LE)
   129вҶ’  - Message data (N bytes CDR)
   130вҶ’```
   131вҶ’

   202вҶ’## CMake Targets
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’| Target | Type | Description |
   205вҶ’|--------|------|-------------|
   206вҶ’| `pj_bridge_app` | STATIC | Core library (no ROS2/DDS deps) |
   207вҶ’| `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` | STATIC | ROS2 adapter library |
   208вҶ’| `pj_bridge_ros2` | EXECUTABLE | ROS2 entry point |
   209вҶ’| `pj_bridge_rti_lib` | STATIC | RTI adapter library (if `ENABLE_RTI=ON`) |

   213вҶ’## Dependencies
   214вҶ’
   215вҶ’### Core (always required)
   216вҶ’- **ZSTD** вҖ” compression (`libzstd-dev`)
   217вҶ’- **spdlog** вҖ” logging (system package or FetchContent)
   218вҶ’- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” WebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
   219вҶ’- **nlohmann/json** вҖ” JSON (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
   220вҶ’- **tl::expected** вҖ” error handling (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’### ROS2 Backend
   223вҶ’- `rclcpp`, `ament_index_cpp`, `ament_cmake`
   224вҶ’- `sensor_msgs`, `nav_msgs` (for message stripper)
   225вҶ’- `ament_cmake_gtest` (test only)
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’### RTI Backend
   228вҶ’- RTI Connext DDS (`RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api`)
   229вҶ’- CLI11 (FetchContent, for CLI parsing)
   230вҶ’
   231вҶ’## Testing
   232вҶ’
   233вҶ’### Test Count: 154 unit tests across 10 test suites
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’### Commands
   236вҶ’```bash
   237вҶ’# Regular build + test
   238вҶ’cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   239вҶ’colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
   240вҶ’colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
   241вҶ’
   242вҶ’# TSAN
   243вҶ’colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
   244вҶ’source install_tsan/setup.bash
   245вҶ’TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
   246вҶ’
   247вҶ’# ASAN
   248вҶ’colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
   249вҶ’source install_asan/setup.bash
   250вҶ’ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
   251вҶ’
   252вҶ’# Format code (before committing)
   253вҶ’pre-commit run -a
   254вҶ’```
   255вҶ’
   256вҶ’### Notes
   257вҶ’- Sanitizer binaries MUST be run with ROS2 sourced (tests need `AMENT_PREFIX_PATH`)
   258вҶ’- TSAN exits with code 66 due to pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings (suppressed) вҖ” this is normal
   259вҶ’- Convenience script available: `./run_and_test.sh`
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’## Configuration
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’### ROS2 (via `--ros-args -p`):
   264вҶ’```yaml
   265вҶ’port: 8080                  # WebSocket port
   266вҶ’publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz
   267вҶ’session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds
   268вҶ’strip_large_messages: true  # Strip Image/PointCloud2/etc data fields
   269вҶ’```
   270вҶ’
   271вҶ’### RTI (via CLI flags):
   272вҶ’```bash
   273вҶ’pj_bridge_rti --domains 0 1 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50 --session-timeout 10
   274вҶ’```
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’## Important Design Decisions
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’1. **Backend-agnostic core via interfaces**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS.
   279вҶ’
   280вҶ’2. **spdlog for logging**: Replaced `RCLCPP_*` macros in the core. System spdlog is required for ROS2 builds (ABI compatibility). FetchContent fallback for standalone builds.
   281вҶ’
   282вҶ’3. **Event loop externalized**: `BridgeServer` exposes `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()` as public methods. The entry point drives timing.
   283вҶ’
   284вҶ’4. **Message data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`**: Backend-agnostic type. ROS2 adapter copies from `SerializedMessage`; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
   285вҶ’
   286вҶ’5. **TTL cleanup by received_at_ns**: MessageBuffer cleanup uses wall-clock reception time, not the message timestamp (which could be from sim time).
   287вҶ’
   288вҶ’6. **Improved lock ordering**: Build serialized frames under `last_sent_mutex_`, send outside lock (adopted from DDS bridge to minimize lock contention).
   289вҶ’
   290вҶ’---
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’**Last Updated**: 2026-02-26
   293вҶ’**Project Phase**: Unified multi-backend architecture
   294вҶ’**Test Status**: 154 unit tests passing (all sanitizers clean)
   295вҶ’**Executables**: `pj_bridge_ros2` (ROS2), `pj_bridge_rti` (RTI DDS)
   296вҶ’

1вҶ’# Architecture
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Overview
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’pj_bridge is a multi-backend bridge server that forwards middleware topic data over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients. A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) is shared by two backend-specific adapters:
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’- **ROS2 backend** (`ros2/`) вҖ” uses `rclcpp`, schema from `.msg` files
     8вҶ’- **RTI backend** (`rti/`) вҖ” uses RTI Connext DDS, schema from OMG IDL
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’```
    11вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    12вҶ’в”Ӯ                  app/ (core)                      в”Ӯ
    13вҶ’в”Ӯ  BridgeServer вҶҗ TopicSourceInterface             в”Ӯ
    14вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ SubscriptionManagerInterface     в”Ӯ
    15вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ MiddlewareInterface              в”Ӯ
    16вҶ’в”Ӯ  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     в”Ӯ
    17вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    18вҶ’         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    19вҶ’    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    20вҶ’    в”Ӯ  ros2/  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ   rti/    в”Ӯ
    21вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2TopicSource      в”Ӯ RtiTopicSource
    22вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  в”Ӯ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    23вҶ’    в”Ӯ (rclcpp)             в”Ӯ (RTI Connext)
    24вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    25вҶ’```
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’## Communication Pattern
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’Single WebSocket port (default 8080), two frame types:
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’- **Text frames**: JSON API requests and responses (`get_topics`, `subscribe`, `unsubscribe`, `heartbeat`, `pause`, `resume`)
    32вҶ’- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream, sent per-client based on their subscriptions
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## Abstract Interfaces
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Three interfaces decouple the core from any specific middleware:
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’### TopicSourceInterface
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’Discovers available topics and retrieves their schemas. Implementations:
    41вҶ’- `Ros2TopicSource`: wraps `TopicDiscovery` (rclcpp enumeration) + `SchemaExtractor` (.msg file parsing). Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
    42вҶ’- `RtiTopicSource`: wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery` (DDS participant discovery). Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’### SubscriptionManagerInterface
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’Manages ref-counted middleware subscriptions. A single global `MessageCallback` delivers all incoming messages as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` to the `MessageBuffer`. Implementations:
    47вҶ’- `Ros2SubscriptionManager`: wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` to `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy. Optionally strips large message fields (Image, PointCloud2) via `MessageStripper`.
    48вҶ’- `RtiSubscriptionManager`: wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, no conversion needed.
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### MiddlewareInterface
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’Abstract transport layer for client connections. Separates text (request/reply) and binary (per-client push) channels. Single implementation: `WebSocketMiddleware` (IXWebSocket).
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’## Core Components
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’### BridgeServer
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’Main orchestrator. Takes all three interfaces via constructor injection (dependency injection). Does **not** own timers вҖ” the entry point drives the event loop by calling three public methods:
    59вҶ’- `process_requests()` вҖ” polls and handles one JSON API request
    60вҶ’- `publish_aggregated_messages()` вҖ” drains the message buffer, serializes per subscription group, compresses with ZSTD, sends per-client binary frames
    61вҶ’- `check_session_timeouts()` вҖ” evicts sessions with expired heartbeats
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’Key behaviors:
    64вҶ’- **Schema-before-subscribe**: `get_schema()` is called before `subscribe()` to avoid corrupting ref counts if schema extraction fails
    65вҶ’- **Additive subscriptions**: `handle_subscribe()` only adds topics, never removes; rate limits can be updated for already-subscribed topics
    66вҶ’- **Ref-counted pause/resume**: `handle_pause()` decrements subscription ref counts; `handle_resume()` increments them; `cleanup_session()` skips unsubscribe for paused clients
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### MessageBuffer
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’Thread-safe per-topic message buffer. Messages are stored as `BufferedMessage` containing:
    71вҶ’- `timestamp_ns` вҖ” source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    72вҶ’- `received_at_ns` вҖ” wall-clock time at insertion (used for TTL cleanup)
    73вҶ’- `data` вҖ” `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, CDR-encoded payload
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’Stale messages (older than 1 second by `received_at_ns`) are removed on every `add_message()` call. `move_messages()` atomically drains the buffer into the caller's map.
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’### SessionManager
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’Tracks client sessions by WebSocket connection identity:
    80вҶ’- Heartbeat monitoring (default 10-second timeout)
    81вҶ’- Per-client subscription tracking with per-topic rate limits
    82вҶ’- Pause/resume state
    83вҶ’- Thread-safe for concurrent access
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’### AggregatedMessageSerializer
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’Streaming binary serializer, no middleware dependencies. Per-message wire format:
    88вҶ’- Topic name length (uint16_t LE) + topic name (UTF-8)
    89вҶ’- Timestamp (uint64_t ns, LE)
    90вҶ’- Message data length (uint32_t LE) + message data (CDR)
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’`finalize()` produces a 16-byte header (`PJRB` magic, message count, uncompressed size, flags) followed by a ZSTD-compressed payload. See [API docs](API.md#binary-message-format) for full format.
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’### WebSocketMiddleware
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’IXWebSocket-based implementation of `MiddlewareInterface`:
    97вҶ’- Connection-oriented client identity via `connectionState->getId()`
    98вҶ’- Incoming text requests queued and dequeued via `receive_request()`
    99вҶ’- Per-client binary send for aggregated message frames
   100вҶ’- Connect/disconnect callbacks for automatic session lifecycle
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’## Event Loop
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’BridgeServer exposes methods; the entry point drives timing:
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’**ROS2** (`ros2/src/main.cpp`): Three `rclcpp` wall timers:
   107вҶ’- 10 ms вҶ’ `process_requests()`
   108вҶ’- 1/publish_rate вҶ’ `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   109вҶ’- 1 s вҶ’ `check_session_timeouts()`
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’Spun via `SingleThreadedExecutor::spin_some(100ms)`.
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’**RTI** (`rti/src/main.cpp`): `std::chrono` loop with 1 ms sleep:
   114вҶ’- Every iteration вҶ’ `process_requests()`
   115вҶ’- At publish_rate вҶ’ `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   116вҶ’- Every 1 s вҶ’ `check_session_timeouts()`
   117вҶ’- Every 5 s (optional) вҶ’ stats snapshot
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’## ROS2-Specific Components
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’- **TopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via `rclcpp::Node::get_topic_names_and_types()`, filtering system topics
   122вҶ’- **SchemaExtractor**: Reads `.msg` files from ROS2 package share directories via `ament_index_cpp`, recursively expanding nested types (matches rosbag2 MCAP format)
   123вҶ’- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Ref-counted `rclcpp::GenericSubscription` per topic
   124вҶ’- **MessageStripper**: Strips data fields from large message types (Image, PointCloud2, etc.)
   125вҶ’
   126вҶ’## RTI-Specific Components
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’- **DdsTopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via DDS participant discovery across configured domain IDs
   129вҶ’- **DdsSubscriptionManager**: Manages DDS DataReaders, natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’## Design Decisions
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’### Backend-Agnostic Core via Interfaces
   134вҶ’`TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS without compile-time dependencies on either middleware.
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’### Message Data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   137вҶ’Backend-agnostic type eliminates `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` from the core. ROS2 adapter converts via memcpy; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’### Externalized Event Loop
   140вҶ’BridgeServer has no internal timers. This avoids coupling to any specific executor model (rclcpp timers vs. `std::chrono` loop) and keeps the core library free of middleware dependencies.
   141вҶ’
   142вҶ’### Shared Subscriptions with Reference Counting
   143вҶ’One underlying middleware subscription per topic, shared across all clients. Ref count incremented on subscribe/resume, decremented on unsubscribe/pause/disconnect.
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’### TTL Cleanup by Wall-Clock Time
   146вҶ’MessageBuffer cleanup uses `received_at_ns` (wall clock), not `timestamp_ns` (source clock). This prevents sim-time offsets from causing premature eviction or unbounded growth.
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’### Per-Client Message Filtering
   149вҶ’Each client receives only messages for its subscribed topics, with per-topic rate limiting. The server serializes, compresses, and sends individually per client.
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’## Thread Safety
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’### Lock Ordering (to prevent deadlock)
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’```
   156вҶ’cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’`SessionManager::mutex_` may be acquired while holding any of these. Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’### Per-Component Guarantees
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’- **MessageBuffer**: Internal mutex protects all buffer operations
   164вҶ’- **SessionManager**: Internal mutex protects session map
   165вҶ’- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Internal mutex protects subscription map
   166вҶ’- **BridgeServer**: `cleanup_mutex_` prevents concurrent cleanup; `last_sent_mutex_` protects rate-limiting state; `stats_mutex_` protects counters. Frames are built under `last_sent_mutex_`, sent outside it (minimizes lock contention)
   167вҶ’- **WebSocketMiddleware**: Separate mutexes for state, client map, and message queue
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’## Shutdown Sequence
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’Both entry points follow the same ordered shutdown to avoid use-after-free:
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’1. Cancel timers / exit event loop
   174вҶ’2. Clear the subscription manager's message callback (`set_message_callback(nullptr)`)
   175вҶ’3. Unsubscribe all middleware subscriptions
   176вҶ’4. Shutdown WebSocket server (BridgeServer destructor handles this if not done explicitly)
   177вҶ’
   178вҶ’## Performance Characteristics
   179вҶ’
   180вҶ’- **Compression**: ZSTD level 1 (typically 50-70% size reduction)
   181вҶ’- **Memory**: Automatic 1-second TTL prevents unbounded growth
   182вҶ’- **Concurrent Clients**: 10+ clients supported
   183вҶ’

REDACTED.md-132-
REDACTED.md-133-### Backend-Agnostic Core via Interfaces
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:134:`TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS without compile-time dependencies on either middleware.
REDACTED.md-135-
REDACTED.md-136-### Message Data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:137:Backend-agnostic type eliminates `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` from the core. ROS2 adapter converts via memcpy; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
REDACTED.md-138-
REDACTED.md-139-### Externalized Event Loop
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md-140-BridgeServer has no internal timers. This avoids coupling to any specific executor model (rclcpp timers vs. `std::chrono` loop) and keeps the core library free of middleware dependencies.
