1вҶ’# plotjuggler_ros_bridge
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’[![ROS2 Humble](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-humble.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-humble.yaml)
     4вҶ’[![ROS2 Jazzy](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml)
     5вҶ’[![ROS2 Rolling](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml)
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’A high-performance ROS2 bridge server that forwards ROS2 topic content over WebSocket, without requiring DDS on the client side.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## Overview
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’`plotjuggler_ros_bridge` enables clients to subscribe to ROS2 topics and receive aggregated messages at 50 Hz without needing a full ROS2/DDS installation. This is particularly useful for visualization tools like PlotJuggler, remote monitoring applications, and lightweight clients that need access to ROS2 data.
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’### Key Features
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’- **No DDS Required**: Clients connect via WebSocket (single port) without needing ROS2/DDS installed
    16вҶ’- **High Performance**: 50 Hz message aggregation with ZSTD compression
    17вҶ’- **Multi-Client Support**: Multiple clients can connect simultaneously with shared subscriptions
    18вҶ’- **Session Management**: Automatic cleanup of disconnected clients via heartbeat monitoring and WebSocket close events
    19вҶ’- **Runtime Schema Discovery**: Automatic extraction of message schemas from installed ROS2 packages
    20вҶ’- **Zero-Copy Design**: Efficient message handling using shared pointers and move semantics
    21вҶ’- **Large Message Stripping**: Automatic stripping of large array fields (Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid) to reduce bandwidth while preserving metadata
    22вҶ’- **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Comprehensive error reporting using `tl::expected`
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’## Architecture
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’For detailed architecture documentation, see [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md).
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’### Dependencies
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’#### ROS2 Packages (Runtime)
    31вҶ’- `rclcpp` - ROS2 C++ client library
    32вҶ’- `ament_index_cpp` - Locate ROS2 package share directories
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’#### System Libraries
    35вҶ’- **ZSTD** (libzstd): `sudo apt install libzstd-dev`
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’#### Vendored Packages
    38вҶ’- **IXWebSocket** (`v11.4.6`): WebSocket server/client
    39вҶ’- **nlohmann/json** - JSON library
    40вҶ’- **tl/expected** - Type-safe error handling
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’## Installation
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’### Building from Source
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’```bash
    47вҶ’# 1. Create workspace (if needed)
    48вҶ’mkdir -p ~/ws_plotjuggler/src
    49вҶ’cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’# 2. Clone the repository
    52вҶ’git clone <repository_url> plotjuggler_ros_bridge
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’# 3. Install system dependencies
    55вҶ’sudo apt install libzstd-dev
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’# 4. Source ROS2
    58вҶ’source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’# 5. Build the package
    61вҶ’cd ~/ws_plotjuggler
    62вҶ’colcon build --packages-select plotjuggler_ros_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’# 6. Source the workspace
    65вҶ’source install/setup.bash
    66вҶ’```
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### Running Tests
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’```bash
    71вҶ’# Run all unit tests (150 tests)
    72вҶ’colcon test --packages-select plotjuggler_ros_bridge
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’# View test results
    75вҶ’colcon test-result --verbose
    76вҶ’```
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’## Usage
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’### Starting the Server
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’#### Basic Usage (Default Configuration)
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’```bash
    85вҶ’source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    86вҶ’source ~/ws_plotjuggler/install/setup.bash
    87вҶ’ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node
    88вҶ’```
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’Default configuration:
    91вҶ’- WebSocket port: 8080
    92вҶ’- Publish rate: 50 Hz
    93вҶ’- Session timeout: 10 seconds
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’#### Custom Configuration
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’```bash
    98вҶ’ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args \
    99вҶ’  -p port:=9090 \
   100вҶ’  -p publish_rate:=30.0 \
   101вҶ’  -p session_timeout:=15.0
   102вҶ’```
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’### Configuration Parameters
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
   107вҶ’|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
   108вҶ’| `port` | int | 8080 | WebSocket server port |
   109вҶ’| `publish_rate` | double | 50.0 | Aggregation publish rate in Hz |
   110вҶ’| `session_timeout` | double | 10.0 | Client timeout duration in seconds |
   111вҶ’| `strip_large_messages` | bool | true | Strip large arrays from Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid messages |
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’### Testing with Sample Data
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’```bash
   116вҶ’# Terminal 1: Play rosbag
   117вҶ’source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   118вҶ’ros2 bag play /path/to/sample.mcap --loop
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’# Terminal 2: Run server
   121вҶ’source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   122вҶ’source ~/ws_plotjuggler/install/setup.bash
   123вҶ’ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’# Terminal 3: Run Python test client
   126вҶ’cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/plotjuggler_ros_bridge
   127вҶ’python3 tests/integration/test_client.py --subscribe /topic1 /topic2
   128вҶ’```
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’## API Protocol
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’For the full API protocol documentation (commands, responses, binary wire format), see [docs/API.md](docs/API.md).
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’### Server fails to start with "Failed to listen on port"
   137вҶ’
   138вҶ’Another process is using the port. Either kill the conflicting process or use a custom port:
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’```bash
   141вҶ’ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args -p port:=9090
   142вҶ’```
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’### Client receives no data
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’1. Verify server is running: `ps aux | grep plotjuggler_ros_bridge`
   147вҶ’2. Check topics are being published: `ros2 topic list`
   148вҶ’3. Verify heartbeat is being sent (required every 1 second)
   149вҶ’4. Check server logs: `ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args --log-level debug`
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’### "Failed to get schema for topic" error
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’The message type's .msg file was not found. Ensure the ROS2 package containing the message type is installed and sourced:
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’```bash
   156вҶ’ros2 interface show <package_name>/msg/<MessageType>
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’### Session timeout / Automatic unsubscription
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’The client stopped sending heartbeats. Ensure the client sends a heartbeat every 1 second. The default timeout is 10 seconds. Increase if needed:
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’```bash
   164вҶ’ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args -p session_timeout:=20.0
   165вҶ’```

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive analysis report.

## Analysis Report: RTI Directory Coupling

Based on a thorough search of the repository, here is the complete coupling analysis between the `rti/` directory and the rest of the codebase:

### Summary
The `rti/` directory is **cleanly decoupled** from the rest of the repository. If deleted, **only the CMakeLists.txt file would need modifications** вҖ” no code changes are required anywhere else.

---

## 1. Imports/Includes of RTI Files from Outside `rti/`

**Result: NONE found**

The `rti/` directory is self-contained. No files outside the `rti/` directory `#include` any headers from `rti/include/pj_bridge_rti/`. The RTI adapter components are only used in `rti/src/main.cpp` (the RTI entry point).

---

## 2. CMakeLists.txt - RTI-Specific Build Configurations

**File**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CMakeLists.txt`

The RTI backend is conditionally built with these blocks that MUST be removed:

**Lines 32-46** вҖ” RTI option and detection:
```cmake
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found вҖ” skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()
```

**Lines 168-205** вҖ” RTI library and executable targets:
```cmake
if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
  # CLI11 for command-line parsing
  FetchContent_Declare(
    cli11
    URL https://github.com/CLIUtils/CLI11/archive/refs/tags/v2.4.2.tar.gz
  )
  set(CLI11_PRECOMPILED OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cli11)

  add_library(pj_bridge_rti_lib STATIC
    rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp
    rti/src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp
    rti/src/rti_topic_source.cpp
    rti/src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp
  )

  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/rti/include
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
    RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api
  )

  add_executable(pj_bridge_rti
    rti/src/main.cpp
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti
    pj_bridge_rti_lib
    CLI11::CLI11
  )

  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_rti
    DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME}
  )
endif()
```

**No changes needed in**:
- Core library (`pj_bridge_app`) вҖ” Lines 93-110 have NO RTI-specific code
- ROS2 section вҖ” Lines 115-163 (conditional on `ament_cmake_FOUND` only)
- Test section вҖ” Lines 234-271 (only tests ROS2 components via `ament_cmake`)
- Installation section for common files вҖ” Lines 210-229

---

## 3. References in package.xml

**File**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/package.xml`

**Result: NO changes needed**

The `package.xml` contains only ROS2 dependencies. It has no RTI-specific entries and no conditional build type definitions for RTI. The file is structured for colcon (ROS2 build system) which doesn't build the RTI backend.

---

## 4. References in Documentation

**Files needing updates**:

1. **`/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CLAUDE.md`** (lines to remove/update):
   - Line 6: "Multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / RTI Connext DDS)" вҶ’ change to ROS2-only
   - Line 10: "RTI backend (`pj_bridge_rti`) вҖ” RTI Connext DDS, uses `rti::connext`" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 29-34: RTI Backend build instructions вҖ” remove entire section
   - Line 41: "CLI11 вҖ” FetchContent (RTI backend only)" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 53: Reference to RTI namespace вҖ” update
   - Lines 79-80: RTI backend architecture diagram вҖ” remove `rti/` box
   - Lines 111-113: RtiTopicSource and RtiSubscriptionManager descriptions вҖ” remove
   - Lines 172-182: RTI directory structure and RTI-specific files вҖ” remove
   - Lines 209-210: `pj_bridge_rti_lib` and `pj_bridge_rti` targets вҖ” remove 2 rows
   - Lines 227-229: RTI Backend dependencies section вҖ” remove
   - Lines 293-295: RTI mention in executables list вҖ” update

2. **`REDACTED.md`** (sections to remove/update):
   - Line 8: "RTI backend (`rti/`) вҖ” uses RTI Connext DDS, schema from OMG IDL" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 20-24: RTI box in architecture diagram вҖ” remove
   - Lines 42: RtiTopicSource description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 48: RtiSubscriptionManager description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 113-117: RTI event loop description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 126-129: RTI-Specific Components section вҖ” remove entirely
   - Line 134: RTI mention in design decisions вҖ” remove

3. **`/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/README.md`**
   - Line 7: Change "ROS2 bridge server" (currently shows only ROS2, but context shows multi-backend)
   - Overall, the README is already ROS2-centric, so minimal changes needed

4. **`REDACTED.md`** (informational only):
   - This is a design document describing the unification. It could be archived or marked historical, but doesn't affect the build. No functional impact if kept.

---

## 5. Test Files

**Result: NO test files reference RTI code**

All 10 test files in `tests/unit/` are ROS2-agnostic (mocks or core tests):
- `test_bridge_server.cpp` вҖ” uses mocks, no RTI code
- `test_session_manager.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no middleware references
- `test_message_buffer.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_message_serializer.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_websocket_middleware.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_protocol_constants.cpp` вҖ” constants, no RTI references
- `test_topic_discovery.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_schema_extractor.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_generic_subscription_manager.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_message_stripper.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)

No RTI-specific tests exist (RTI backend was optional and not in this branch's test suite).

---

## 6. Other Cross-References

**Result: NO other cross-references found**

Checked:
- `.github/workflows/release-debs.yaml` вҖ” No RTI mentions
- All app layer headers and source вҖ” No RTI dependencies
- All ROS2 layer headers and source вҖ” No RTI imports or references
- All integration tests вҖ” No RTI references

---

## 7. Namespace/Extern Declarations

**Result: NONE**

All RTI-specific types are in the `pj_bridge::` namespace (shared with the core and ROS2 code), but they are isolated to the `rti/` directory. No extern declarations or exports from RTI headers into app or ROS2 layers.

---

## Summary of Files to Modify if Deleting `rti/`

| File Path | Severity | Change Type | Lines Affected |
|-----------|----------|-------------|----------------|
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CMakeLists.txt` | **Required** | Code removal | Lines 32-46, 168-205 |
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CLAUDE.md` | **Recommended** | Documentation cleanup | Multiple sections (see detail above) |
| `REDACTED.md` | **Recommended** | Documentation cleanup | Multiple sections (see detail above) |
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/README.md` | **Optional** | Minor update | 1 line (if needed for clarity) |
| `REDACTED.md` | **None** | Informational | N/A (historical document) |

---

## Conclusion

The RTI backend is **cleanly decoupled** using the plugin architecture pattern:

- **Core layer** (`app/`) has NO RTI dependencies вҖ” it uses only abstract interfaces
- **ROS2 layer** (`ros2/`) has NO RTI imports or conditional code
- **RTI layer** (`rti/`) is self-contained and imports ONLY from `app/`
- **CMake** uses conditional blocks (`if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)`) to safely guard all RTI building

**To delete `rti/`, you need to modify ONLY CMakeLists.txt** (and optionally update documentation). No code changes are required in any non-RTI file.

1. Any imports/includes of files from `rti/` in files outside `rti/`
2. References to RTI-specific targets, types, or files in CMakeLists.txt
3. References to RTI in package.xml
4. References to RTI in docs, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md
5. Any test files that reference RTI-specific code
6. Any other cross-references between rti/ and the rest of the codebase

Be thorough - check CMakeLists.txt carefully for conditional blocks, target definitions, etc. related to RTI.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive analysis report.

## Analysis Report: RTI Directory Coupling

Based on a thorough search of the repository, here is the complete coupling analysis between the `rti/` directory and the rest of the codebase:

### Summary
The `rti/` directory is **cleanly decoupled** from the rest of the repository. If deleted, **only the CMakeLists.txt file would need modifications** вҖ” no code changes are required anywhere else.

---

## 1. Imports/Includes of RTI Files from Outside `rti/`

**Result: NONE found**

The `rti/` directory is self-contained. No files outside the `rti/` directory `#include` any headers from `rti/include/pj_bridge_rti/`. The RTI adapter components are only used in `rti/src/main.cpp` (the RTI entry point).

---

## 2. CMakeLists.txt - RTI-Specific Build Configurations

**File**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CMakeLists.txt`

The RTI backend is conditionally built with these blocks that MUST be removed:

**Lines 32-46** вҖ” RTI option and detection:
```cmake
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found вҖ” skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()
```

**Lines 168-205** вҖ” RTI library and executable targets:
```cmake
if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
  # CLI11 for command-line parsing
  FetchContent_Declare(
    cli11
    URL https://github.com/CLIUtils/CLI11/archive/refs/tags/v2.4.2.tar.gz
  )
  set(CLI11_PRECOMPILED OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cli11)

  add_library(pj_bridge_rti_lib STATIC
    rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp
    rti/src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp
    rti/src/rti_topic_source.cpp
    rti/src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp
  )

  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/rti/include
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
    RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api
  )

  add_executable(pj_bridge_rti
    rti/src/main.cpp
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti
    pj_bridge_rti_lib
    CLI11::CLI11
  )

  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_rti
    DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME}
  )
endif()
```

**No changes needed in**:
- Core library (`pj_bridge_app`) вҖ” Lines 93-110 have NO RTI-specific code
- ROS2 section вҖ” Lines 115-163 (conditional on `ament_cmake_FOUND` only)
- Test section вҖ” Lines 234-271 (only tests ROS2 components via `ament_cmake`)
- Installation section for common files вҖ” Lines 210-229

---

## 3. References in package.xml

**File**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/package.xml`

**Result: NO changes needed**

The `package.xml` contains only ROS2 dependencies. It has no RTI-specific entries and no conditional build type definitions for RTI. The file is structured for colcon (ROS2 build system) which doesn't build the RTI backend.

---

## 4. References in Documentation

**Files needing updates**:

1. **`/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CLAUDE.md`** (lines to remove/update):
   - Line 6: "Multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / RTI Connext DDS)" вҶ’ change to ROS2-only
   - Line 10: "RTI backend (`pj_bridge_rti`) вҖ” RTI Connext DDS, uses `rti::connext`" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 29-34: RTI Backend build instructions вҖ” remove entire section
   - Line 41: "CLI11 вҖ” FetchContent (RTI backend only)" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 53: Reference to RTI namespace вҖ” update
   - Lines 79-80: RTI backend architecture diagram вҖ” remove `rti/` box
   - Lines 111-113: RtiTopicSource and RtiSubscriptionManager descriptions вҖ” remove
   - Lines 172-182: RTI directory structure and RTI-specific files вҖ” remove
   - Lines 209-210: `pj_bridge_rti_lib` and `pj_bridge_rti` targets вҖ” remove 2 rows
   - Lines 227-229: RTI Backend dependencies section вҖ” remove
   - Lines 293-295: RTI mention in executables list вҖ” update

2. **`REDACTED.md`** (sections to remove/update):
   - Line 8: "RTI backend (`rti/`) вҖ” uses RTI Connext DDS, schema from OMG IDL" вҖ” remove
   - Lines 20-24: RTI box in architecture diagram вҖ” remove
   - Lines 42: RtiTopicSource description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 48: RtiSubscriptionManager description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 113-117: RTI event loop description вҖ” remove
   - Lines 126-129: RTI-Specific Components section вҖ” remove entirely
   - Line 134: RTI mention in design decisions вҖ” remove

3. **`/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/README.md`**
   - Line 7: Change "ROS2 bridge server" (currently shows only ROS2, but context shows multi-backend)
   - Overall, the README is already ROS2-centric, so minimal changes needed

4. **`REDACTED.md`** (informational only):
   - This is a design document describing the unification. It could be archived or marked historical, but doesn't affect the build. No functional impact if kept.

---

## 5. Test Files

**Result: NO test files reference RTI code**

All 10 test files in `tests/unit/` are ROS2-agnostic (mocks or core tests):
- `test_bridge_server.cpp` вҖ” uses mocks, no RTI code
- `test_session_manager.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no middleware references
- `test_message_buffer.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_message_serializer.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_websocket_middleware.cpp` вҖ” core logic, no RTI references
- `test_protocol_constants.cpp` вҖ” constants, no RTI references
- `test_topic_discovery.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_schema_extractor.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_generic_subscription_manager.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)
- `test_message_stripper.cpp` вҖ” ROS2-specific (imports from ros2/)

No RTI-specific tests exist (RTI backend was optional and not in this branch's test suite).

---

## 6. Other Cross-References

**Result: NO other cross-references found**

Checked:
- `.github/workflows/release-debs.yaml` вҖ” No RTI mentions
- All app layer headers and source вҖ” No RTI dependencies
- All ROS2 layer headers and source вҖ” No RTI imports or references
- All integration tests вҖ” No RTI references

---

## 7. Namespace/Extern Declarations

**Result: NONE**

All RTI-specific types are in the `pj_bridge::` namespace (shared with the core and ROS2 code), but they are isolated to the `rti/` directory. No extern declarations or exports from RTI headers into app or ROS2 layers.

---

## Summary of Files to Modify if Deleting `rti/`

| File Path | Severity | Change Type | Lines Affected |
|-----------|----------|-------------|----------------|
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CMakeLists.txt` | **Required** | Code removal | Lines 32-46, 168-205 |
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CLAUDE.md` | **Recommended** | Documentation cleanup | Multiple sections (see detail above) |
| `REDACTED.md` | **Recommended** | Documentation cleanup | Multiple sections (see detail above) |
| `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/README.md` | **Optional** | Minor update | 1 line (if needed for clarity) |
| `REDACTED.md` | **None** | Informational | N/A (historical document) |

---

## Conclusion

The RTI backend is **cleanly decoupled** using the plugin architecture pattern:

- **Core layer** (`app/`) has NO RTI dependencies вҖ” it uses only abstract interfaces
- **ROS2 layer** (`ros2/`) has NO RTI imports or conditional code
- **RTI layer** (`rti/`) is self-contained and imports ONLY from `app/`
- **CMake** uses conditional blocks (`if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)`) to safely guard all RTI building

**To delete `rti/`, you need to modify ONLY CMakeLists.txt** (and optionally update documentation). No code changes are required in any non-RTI file.

The RTI backend is **very cleanly decoupled**. If you delete `rti/`, only **one file requires code changes**:

| File | Change needed |
|------|---------------|
| **`CMakeLists.txt`** | Remove the `ENABLE_RTI` option (lines 32-46) and the RTI library/executable block (lines 168-205) |

Everything else is documentation updates:

- **`CLAUDE.md`** вҖ” remove RTI references (build instructions, architecture diagram, component descriptions, targets table)
- **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`** вҖ” remove RTI boxes from diagram and RTI component sections
- **`docs/plans/...`** вҖ” historical, can keep as-is

No source code, no tests, no `package.xml` changes needed. The interface abstraction (`TopicSourceInterface`, `SubscriptionManagerInterface`) means `app/` and `ros2/` have zero awareness that RTI exists. The same is true in reverse вҖ” deleting `ros2/` would also only require `CMakeLists.txt` changes.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md - Project Context for AI Assistant

## Project Overview

**Project Name**: pj_bridge
**Type**: Multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / RTI Connext DDS)
**Purpose**: Forward middleware topic content over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients

**Main Goal**: Enable clients to subscribe to topics and receive aggregated messages at configurable rates without needing a full middleware installation. Two backends share a common core library:
- **ROS2 backend** (`pj_bridge_ros2`) вҖ” ROS2 Humble, uses `rclcpp`
- **RTI backend** (`pj_bridge_rti`) вҖ” RTI Connext DDS, uses `rti::connext`

## Key Documentation Files

- `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md` вҖ” Unified architecture design
- `docs/API.md` - API protocol documentation
- `.clang-tidy` - Coding standards and style guide

## Build Instructions

### ROS2 Backend (colcon)
```bash
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
```

### RTI Backend (standalone CMake)
```bash
mkdir build && cd build
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_RTI=ON
make -j$(nproc)
```

### External Dependencies

- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” vendored in `3rdparty/ixwebsocket`
- **spdlog** вҖ” system package preferred (for ROS2 ABI compatibility with `librcl_logging_spdlog`); FetchContent fallback for standalone builds
- **ZSTD** вҖ” system package (`libzstd-dev`)
- **CLI11** вҖ” FetchContent (RTI backend only)

**Important**: Do NOT use FetchContent for spdlog when building with ROS2. The system spdlog must match the version used by `librcl_logging_spdlog.so` to avoid ABI conflicts (symbol collision causes "free(): invalid pointer" crash during `rclcpp::init()`).

## Coding Standards

### Naming Conventions (from `.clang-tidy`)

- **Classes/Structs**: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `BufferedMessage`)
- **Functions/Methods**: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `add_message()`)
- **Local/member variables**: `lower_case`, private members suffix with `_`
- **Constants**: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` (e.g., `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`)
- **Namespaces**: `pj_bridge` (core), `pj_bridge` with ROS2/RTI-specific types

### Code Quality

- **Thread Safety**: Use mutexes, document thread safety in class comments
- **Testing**: Unit tests required for all core components (gtest)
- **Code Formatting**: `pre-commit run -a` before committing (clang-format)
- **Logging**: Use `spdlog::info/warn/error/debug()` вҖ” NOT `RCLCPP_*` macros (except in ros2/ adapter code)

## Architecture Overview

### Multi-Backend Design

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                  app/ (core)                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  BridgeServer вҶҗ TopicSourceInterface             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ               вҶҗ SubscriptionManagerInterface     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ               вҶҗ MiddlewareInterface              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    в”Ӯ  ros2/  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ   rti/    в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ Ros2TopicSource      в”Ӯ RtiTopicSource
    в”Ӯ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  в”Ӯ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    в”Ӯ (rclcpp)             в”Ӯ (RTI Connext)
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Abstract Interfaces (in `app/include/pj_bridge/`)

**TopicSourceInterface**: `get_topics()`, `get_schema(topic_name)`, `schema_encoding()`
**SubscriptionManagerInterface**: `set_message_callback()`, `subscribe(name, type)`, `unsubscribe()`, `unsubscribe_all()`
**MiddlewareInterface**: WebSocket text/binary frame API

### Event Loop

BridgeServer does NOT own timers. The entry point (`main.cpp`) drives the event loop:
- **ROS2**: `rclcpp` wall timers call `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()`
- **RTI**: `std::chrono` loop with `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`

### Key Components

1. **BridgeServer** (`app/`) вҖ” Main orchestrator. Takes interfaces via constructor injection. Uses spdlog for logging. Improved lock ordering: build frames under lock, send outside lock.

2. **MessageBuffer** (`app/`) вҖ” Thread-safe per-topic buffer. Uses `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` (not `rclcpp::SerializedMessage`). TTL cleanup based on `received_at_ns` (wall-clock time when added).

3. **AggregatedMessageSerializer** (`app/`) вҖ” Serializes `(topic, timestamp, byte*, size)`. Backend-agnostic (no rclcpp dependency).

4. **SessionManager** (`app/`) вҖ” Tracks clients, heartbeats, per-client subscriptions.

5. **WebSocketMiddleware** (`app/`) вҖ” IXWebSocket implementation. Improved shutdown: shared_ptr server, timeout thread, pre-close clients.

6. **Ros2TopicSource** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`. Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.

7. **Ros2SubscriptionManager** (`ros2/`) вҖ” Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager` + optional `MessageStripper`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` вҶ’ `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy.

8. **RtiTopicSource** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`. Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.

9. **RtiSubscriptionManager** (`rti/`) вҖ” Wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`.

### Communication Pattern

**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat, pause, resume, unsubscribe)
- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream

### Message Serialization Format

```
For each message (streamed, no header):
  - Topic name length (uint16_t LE)
  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
  - Timestamp (uint64_t ns since epoch, LE)
  - Message data length (uint32_t LE)
  - Message data (N bytes CDR)
```

## Project Structure

```
pj_bridge/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ app/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ include/pj_bridge/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ topic_source_interface.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ subscription_manager_interface.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ middleware/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ middleware_interface.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ websocket_middleware.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ bridge_server.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ session_manager.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ message_buffer.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ message_serializer.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ protocol_constants.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ time_utils.hpp
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ src/
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ bridge_server.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ session_manager.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ message_buffer.cpp
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ message_serializer.cpp
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ros2/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ include/pj_bridge_ros2/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ros2_topic_source.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ros2_subscription_manager.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ topic_discovery.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ schema_extractor.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ generic_subscription_manager.hpp
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ message_stripper.hpp
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ src/
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ ros2_topic_source.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ ros2_subscription_manager.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ topic_discovery.cpp
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ schema_extractor.cpp

```

## CMake Targets

| Target | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `pj_bridge_app` | STATIC | Core library (no ROS2/DDS deps) |
| `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` | STATIC | ROS2 adapter library |
| `pj_bridge_ros2` | EXECUTABLE | ROS2 entry point |
| `pj_bridge_rti_lib` | STATIC | RTI adapter library (if `ENABLE_RTI=ON`) |
| `pj_bridge_rti` | EXECUTABLE | RTI entry point |
| `pj_bridge_tests` | EXECUTABLE | All unit tests |

## Dependencies

### Core (always required)
- **ZSTD** вҖ” compression (`libzstd-dev`)
- **spdlog** вҖ” logging (system package or FetchContent)
- **IXWebSocket** вҖ” WebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
- **nlohmann/json** вҖ” JSON (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
- **tl::expected** вҖ” error handling (header-only, in 3rdparty/)

### ROS2 Backend
- `rclcpp`, `ament_index_cpp`, `ament_cmake`
- `sensor_msgs`, `nav_msgs` (for message stripper)
- `ament_cmake_gtest` (test only)

### RTI Backend
- RTI Connext DDS (`RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api`)
- CLI11 (FetchContent, for CLI parsing)

## Testing

### Test Count: 154 unit tests across 10 test suites

### Commands
```bash
# Regular build + test
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose

# TSAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests

# ASAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests

# Format code (before committing)
pre-commit run -a
```

### Notes
- Sanitizer binaries MUST be run with ROS2 sourced (tests need `AMENT_PREFIX_PATH`)
- TSAN exits with code 66 due to pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings (suppressed) вҖ” this is normal
- Convenience script available: `./run_and_test.sh`

## Configuration

### ROS2 (via `--ros-args -p`):
```yaml
port: 8080                  # WebSocket port
publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz
session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds
strip_large_messages: true  # Strip Image/PointCloud2/etc data fields
```

### RTI (via CLI flags):
```bash
pj_bridge_rti --domains 0 1 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50 --session-timeout 10
```

## Important Design Decisions

1. **Backend-agnostic core via interfaces**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS.

2. **spdlog for logging**: Replaced `RCLCPP_*` macros in the core. System spdlog is required for ROS2 builds (ABI compatibility). FetchContent fallback for standalone builds.

3. **Event loop externalized**: `BridgeServer` exposes `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()` as public methods. The entry point drives timing.

4. **Message data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`**: Backend-agnostic type. ROS2 adapter copies from `SerializedMessage`; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.

5. **TTL cleanup by received_at_ns**: MessageBuffer cleanup uses wall-clock reception time, not the message timestamp (which could be from sim time).

6. **Improved lock ordering**: Build serialized frames under `last_sent_mutex_`, send outside lock (adopted from DDS bridge to minimize lock contention).

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-02-26
**Project Phase**: Unified multi-backend architecture
**Test Status**: 154 unit tests passing (all sanitizers clean)
**Executables**: `pj_bridge_ros2` (ROS2), `pj_bridge_rti` (RTI DDS)

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. The user provided a detailed plan to fix 7 bugs found in a code review. The plan had steps 0-4 with specific file changes.

2. I read all the relevant files to understand the codebase:
   - test_bridge_server.cpp (the main test file with mocks)
   - bridge_server.cpp (main orchestrator)
   - bridge_server.hpp
   - session_manager.hpp and .cpp
   - message_buffer.hpp and .cpp
   - websocket_middleware.hpp and .cpp
   - ros2/src/main.cpp
   - rti/src/main.cpp
   - subscription_manager_interface.hpp

3. I created tasks to track progress and implemented changes:

   **Step 0**: Enhanced MockSubscriptionManager
   - Replaced `std::set<std::string> subscribed_topics_` with `std::unordered_map<std::string, int> ref_counts_`
   - Added `bool underflow_detected_`
   - subscribe() increments ref count
   - unsubscribe() decrements, detects underflow
   - Added helpers: ref_count(), has_underflow(), reset_underflow()
   - Added remove_topic() to MockTopicSource
   - Added `<algorithm>` and `<unordered_map>` includes

   **Step 1**: Fixed two broken tests
   - UnsubscribeRemovesTopics: Added real topics to mock, verified subscribe succeeds, verified unsubscribe removes /topic_a while /topic_b remains
   - SubscribeIsAdditive: Added real topics, verified both subscriptions succeed and both remain active

   **Step 2**: Added new tests for each bug
   - DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts (Bug #1)
   - ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession (Bug #2)
   - UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat (Bug #4)
   - IncomingQueueBounded (Bug #6) - in test_websocket_middleware.cpp
   - InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate and InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate (Bug #9)

   **Step 3**: Implemented bug fixes
   - Bug #15: Removed `last_read_timestamp_ns_` from message_buffer.hpp and .cpp
   - Bug #4: Added `else if (topic_item.is_object() && ...)` branch in handle_unsubscribe
   - Bug #9: Already validated in initialize() - no change needed for entry points
   - Bug #6: Added kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024 and queue bound check
   - Bug #7: Changed send_reply, send_binary, publish_data to copy shared_ptr under lock, release, then send
   - Bug #1: Added add_subscription/remove_subscription to SessionManager, used incremental updates in handle_subscribe with rollback
   - Bug #2: Collect failed topics during resume, remove from session via remove_subscription

4. Errors encountered:
   - `final_subscriptions` was not declared after refactoring handle_subscribe - fixed by replacing with `session_manager_->get_subscriptions(client_id)`
   - clang-format pre-commit hook reformatted files on first commit attempt - fixed by re-staging and recommitting

5. After implementation, I ran tests:
   - Regular build: 317 tests pass
   - TSAN: 161 gtest cases pass (29 pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings)
   - ASAN: 161 gtest cases pass, clean

6. User asked for code review before commit - I used the code-reviewer agent which found:
   - 0 critical, 0 important issues
   - S2 suggestion: publish_rate validation happens after middleware init - I fixed this by moving the check before middleware initialization

7. User said "commit" - I committed with detailed message, commit 83a70db

8. User asked about coupling between app and backends - I used Explore agent to analyze. Finding: RTI is cleanly decoupled, only CMakeLists.txt needs code changes if rti/ is deleted, plus documentation updates.

9. User said "if the plans are completed, delete the" (sentence cut off) - I asked for clarification, user chose to delete entire docs/plans/ directory. I removed it and the reference in CLAUDE.md. Commit 9c8b9f9.

10. User said "commit" again - working tree was already clean from the previous commit.

Now the user asks for a summary of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked me to implement a detailed plan to fix 7 bugs found in a code review for the `pj_bridge` project. The plan had 5 steps (Step 0-4): enhance the mock subscription manager with ref counting, fix two incorrect existing tests, write failing tests for each bug, implement all 7 bug fixes, and verify all tests pass. After implementation, the user asked me to run a code review agent before committing, then to commit. The user then asked about coupling between app/ and rti/ backends, and finally asked to delete the completed plan documents from docs/plans/.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Multi-backend architecture**: `app/` (core), `ros2/` (ROS2 adapter), `rti/` (RTI Connext DDS adapter) with abstract interfaces
   - **Reference-counted subscriptions**: `SubscriptionManagerInterface` with subscribe/unsubscribe ref counting
   - **Incremental session updates**: `add_subscription()`/`remove_subscription()` replacing batch `update_subscriptions()` to prevent races
   - **Thread safety**: Lock ordering (`cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_`), copying shared_ptrs under lock then releasing before I/O
   - **Bounded queues**: `kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024` to prevent unbounded memory growth
   - **Mock-based testing**: MockSubscriptionManager with ref-count tracking and underflow detection
   - **Build verification**: Regular build + TSAN + ASAN sanitizer passes required
   - **Pre-commit hooks**: clang-format enforced via pre-commit

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`**
     - Central test file containing MockMiddleware, MockTopicSource, MockSubscriptionManager, BridgeServerTest fixture, and all bridge server tests
     - **MockSubscriptionManager rewritten** from set-based to ref-count-based:
       ```cpp
       class MockSubscriptionManager : public SubscriptionManagerInterface {
        public:
         bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& /*topic_type*/) override {
           if (known_topics_.find(topic_name) == known_topics_.end()) return false;
           ref_counts_[topic_name]++;
           return true;
         }
         bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) override {
           auto it = ref_counts_.find(topic_name);
           if (it == ref_counts_.end() || it->second <= 0) { underflow_detected_ = true; return false; }
           it->second--;
           if (it->second == 0) ref_counts_.erase(it);
           return true;
         }
         // helpers: ref_count(), has_underflow(), reset_underflow(), is_subscribed(), add_known_topic()
        private:
         std::unordered_map<std::string, int> ref_counts_;
         bool underflow_detected_{false};
       };
       ```
     - **MockTopicSource** got `remove_topic()` helper for Bug #2 test
     - **Fixed UnsubscribeRemovesTopics**: Added real topics via `set_topics()` and `add_known_topic()`, verified subscribe succeeds, then verified unsubscribe removes /topic_a while /topic_b remains
     - **Fixed SubscribeIsAdditive**: Added real topics, verified both subscriptions succeed independently
     - **Added 5 new tests**: DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts, ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession, UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat, InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate, InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate

   - **`tests/unit/test_websocket_middleware.cpp`**
     - Added `IncomingQueueBounded` test: sends 2000 messages, verifies at most 1024 received

   - **`app/src/bridge_server.cpp`**
     - **Bug #4 fix** in `handle_unsubscribe()`: Added object format parsing:
       ```cpp
       } else if (topic_item.is_object() && topic_item.contains("name") && topic_item["name"].is_string()) {
         topic_name = topic_item["name"].get<std::string>();
       }
       ```
     - **Bug #1 fix** in `handle_subscribe()`: Replaced batch `update_subscriptions()` with incremental `add_subscription()` after each successful subscribe, with rollback if session is gone:
       ```cpp
       if (!session_manager_->add_subscription(client_id, topic_name, requested_topics[topic_name])) {
         spdlog::warn("Session gone for client '{}', rolling back subscribe for '{}'", client_id, topic_name);
         subscription_manager_->unsubscribe(topic_name);
         continue;
       }
       ```
     - **Bug #2 fix** in `handle_resume()`: Collect failed topics and remove from session:
       ```cpp
       std::vector<std::string> failed_topics;
       // ... in loop: failed_topics.push_back(topic);
       for (const auto& topic : failed_topics) {
         session_manager_->remove_subscription(client_id, topic);
       }
       ```
     - **Bug #9 fix**: Moved `publish_rate_ <= 0.0` validation before middleware initialization
     - Removed `#include <unordered_set>` (no longer needed after removing `successfully_subscribed` set)

   - **`app/include/pj_bridge/session_manager.hpp`** and **`app/src/session_manager.cpp`**
     - Added two new methods for incremental subscription management:
       ```cpp
       bool add_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic, double rate);
       bool remove_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic);
       ```

   - **`app/include/pj_bridge/message_buffer.hpp`** and **`app/src/message_buffer.cpp`**
     - **Bug #15**: Removed `uint64_t last_read_timestamp_ns_` field and all assignments in constructor, `move_messages()`, and `clear()`

   - **`app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/websocket_middleware.hpp`**
     - **Bug #6**: Added `static constexpr size_t kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024;`

   - **`app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`**
     - **Bug #6**: Added queue bound check before push:
       ```cpp
       if (incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize) {
         spdlog::warn("Incoming queue full ({} messages), dropping message from client '{}'",
                      kMaxIncomingQueueSize, client_id);
         return;
       }
       ```
     - **Bug #7**: Changed `send_reply()`, `send_binary()`, `publish_data()` to copy shared_ptr under lock, release lock, then send. Example for send_reply:
       ```cpp
       std::shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket> ws;
       {
         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(clients_mutex_);
         auto it = clients_.find(client_identity);
         if (it == clients_.end() || !it->second) return false;
         ws = it->second;
       }
       std::string text_data(data.begin(), data.end());
       auto send_info = ws->send(text_data);
       return send_info.success;
       ```

   - **`CLAUDE.md`**
     - Removed reference to `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md` after plans directory deletion

   - **`docs/plans/`** вҖ” Entire directory deleted (5 completed plan files)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`final_subscriptions` not declared**: After refactoring `handle_subscribe()` to use incremental `add_subscription()`, the response-building section still referenced the removed `final_subscriptions` variable. Fixed by replacing with `session_manager_->get_subscriptions(client_id)`.
   - **clang-format pre-commit hook failure**: First commit attempt failed because clang-format reformatted some files. Fixed by re-staging the auto-formatted changes with `git add -u` and recommitting.
   - **Code review suggestion S2**: The reviewer agent noted that `publish_rate_` validation happened after middleware initialization, meaning middleware could start accepting connections before discovering an invalid rate. Fixed by moving the validation before `middleware_->initialize()`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - All 7 bugs were implemented and verified with tests
   - 317 tests pass in regular build, 161 gtest cases pass under both TSAN and ASAN
   - Code review found no critical or important issues
   - Backend coupling analysis confirmed clean decoupling (only CMakeLists.txt + docs need changes if rti/ is removed)
   - Completed plans directory was cleaned up

8. Current Work:

All tasks from the bug fix plan are complete, the code review was done, two commits were made (83a70db for the bug fixes + architecture, 9c8b9f9 for plans cleanup), and the coupling analysis was answered.

I found search results related to Fast DDS XTypes and remote type discovery. Here's what the search revealed:

## Fast DDS XTypes and Remote Type Discovery

eProsima Fast DDS supports the OMG Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types for DDS specification (also known as XTypes).

### DynamicType Discovery Process

Discovery-Time Data Typing allows the discovering of simple DynamicTypes. A TypeObject that depends on other TypeObjects, cannot be built locally using Discovery-Time Data Typing and should use TypeLookup Service instead.

### Key Components in Type Discovery

When a participant discovers a remote endpoint that sends a complete TypeObject or a simple TypeIdentifier describing a type that the participant does not know, the participant listener's function on_type_discovery is called with the received Type.

TypeInformation is an extension of XTypes 1.2 that allow Endpoints to share information about data types without sending the TypeObject. Endpoints instead share a TypeInformation containing the TypeIdentifier of the data type. Then each Endpoint can request the complete TypeObject for the data types it is interested in.

### Builtin Topic Integration

The local data types must be registered into the ITypeObjectRegistry. TypeInformation should be received with the DomainParticipant's endpoint discovery information.

The documentation references indicate these features are available across multiple versions of Fast DDS, with consistent support for dynamic type discovery mechanisms through builtin topics.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about eProsima Fast DDS XTypes, DynamicType discovery, and related concepts:

## Overview

eProsima Fast DDS supports the OMG Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types for DDS specification (also known as XTypes).

## DynamicType Discovery

Discovery-Time Data Typing allows the discovering of simple DynamicTypes. However, a TypeObject that depends on other TypeObjects, cannot be built locally using Discovery-Time Data Typing and should use TypeLookup Service instead.

## TypeObject and TypeIdentifier

TypeObject is an IDL union with both Minimal and Complete representation, both described in the annexes of DDS-XTypes V1.2 document. There are two kinds of TypeObjects: CompleteTypeObject and MinimalTypeObject, with CompleteTypeObject fully describing the type, the same way as the IDL representation does.

TypeIdentifier provides a unique way to identify each type - for basic types, the information contained in the TypeIdentifier completely describes the type, while for complex ones, it serves as a search key to retrieve the complete TypeObject.

## Type Discovery Mechanism

When using DynamicType support, Fast DDS checks the optional TypeObject and TypeIdentifier values during endpoint matching, with the matching only verifying that both endpoints are using the same topic data type, but not negotiating about it.

To ease the sharing of the TypeObject and TypeIdentifier used by Discovery-Time Data Typing, TopicDataType contains a function member named auto_fill_type_object, and if set to true, the local participant will send the TypeObject and TypeIdentifier to the remote endpoint during discovery.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# XTypes FAQ Response

Based on the provided documentation, the XTypes FAQ section does not contain information about:

- Getting DynamicType from remote publishers
- TypeLookup Service
- TypeObject propagation
- Interoperability with other DDS vendors like RTI Connext

The FAQ only addresses six topics:

1. **XTypes definition**: "Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types for DDS specification"
2. **Remote type discovery mechanism**: Based on optimized exchange of type information
3. **Prerequisites**: Local types registered in `ITypeObjectRegistry` and TypeInformation receipt
4. **Dynamic Language Binding purpose**: Defining data types at runtime
5. **Complex data type management**: Two approaches using `get_complex_value`/`set_complex_value` or `loan_value`
6. **Serialization formats**: IDL representation and JSON

For the specific topics you asked about, you would need to consult additional documentation sections or the full [XTypes](../../xtypes/xtypes.html) guide referenced in the FAQ.

content.content:
# Fast DDS 3.x DynamicType Discovery from Remote Endpoints

Based on the documentation provided, here's what's available:

## 1) Getting DynamicType from Discovered TypeObject

Yes, Fast DDS supports this through the **Remote Data Types Discovery** mechanism. The documentation states that Fast DDS provides a "DDS builtin mechanism to automatically discover remote data types."

## 2) TypeLookup Service

The documentation references a "TypeLookup Service" implicitly through the discovery matching process, though explicit service details aren't elaborated in this excerpt.

## 3) On-Type Discovery Callback

The documentation mentions **"DomainParticipantListener Discovery Callbacks"** as a feature, with a dedicated section at `5.3.5. DomainParticipantListener Discovery Callbacks`. This suggests callback mechanisms exist for type discovery events.

## 4) API Classes for Remote Type Discovery

Key classes exposed include:

- **TypeObjectRegistry** - for accessing type representations
- **TypeObjectUtils** - utilities for type object manipulation
- **DynamicTypeBuilderFactory** - creates dynamic types programmatically
- **DynamicType** - the runtime type representation
- **DynamicDataFactory** - instantiates data from discovered types

The documentation emphasizes that "eProsima Fast DDS supports the OMG Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types for DDS specification," enabling runtime type discovery without compile-time IDL knowledge.

**Note:** Type compatibility rules among evolved types remain unsupported in Fast DDS.

input.prompt: Show the full details of how DynamicType discovery works in Fast DDS. Specifically: 1) The on_type_discovery callback - what parameters does it receive? Does it give you a DynamicType? 2) How does TypeLookup Service work? 3) Can you get the complete DynamicType from a remote RTI publisher? 4) What is the relationship between TypeObject and DynamicType?
Show the full details of type serialization utilities. Can you convert a DynamicType to IDL string representation? What serialization/deserialization is available for types?

content.content:
# Type Serialization Utilities in Fast DDS

Yes, Fast DDS supports converting DynamicType objects to IDL representation and provides several serialization options.

## DynamicType to IDL Conversion

The `idl_serialize` method enables conversion of DynamicType objects to their IDL format. According to the documentation, "The method `idl_serialize` serializes a `DynamicType` object to its IDL representation."

**Limitations:**
- Only supports four builtin annotations: `@bit_bound`, `@extensibility`, `@key`, and `@position`
- Bitset inheritance merges derived and base Bitsets
- Omits values explicitly set to default values from the output

## Available Serialization Methods

Fast DDS provides two primary serialization utilities:

1. **DynamicType вҶ’ IDL**: The `idl_serialize` function converts discovered types into human-readable IDL format, useful for understanding remote type structures.

2. **DynamicData вҶ’ JSON**: The `json_serialize` function transforms data instances into JSON format. It supports two output formats:
   - **eProsima format**: Provides detailed structure including names and values
   - **OMG format**: Delivers simplified, standards-compliant output

The JSON serialization handles all major type categories including primitives, strings, enumerations, sequences, arrays, structures, unions, and bitsets.

content.content:
# DynamicType Discovery in Fast DDS

## 1) on_type_discovery Callback

The callback receives these parameters:
- A `DomainParticipant` pointer
- A `SampleIdentity` 
- The remote endpoint's type name
- A `TypeIdentifier` (if available)
- A `TypeObject` (if available)
- **A pointer to a `DynamicType` ready to use** (when possible)

As stated: "when a participant discovers a remote endpoint that sends a complete TypeObject or a simple TypeIdentifier describing a type that the participant does not know, the participant listener's function `on_type_discovery` is called with the received TypeObject or TypeIdentifier, and, when possible, a pointer to a DynamicType ready to be used."

However, this only works for simple types. Complex types depending on other TypeObjects require TypeLookup Service instead.

## 2) TypeLookup Service Operation

When discovering an endpoint with unknown type information, the `on_type_information_received()` callback triggers. Users can then invoke `register_remote_type()` on the DomainParticipant, which:
- Uses TypeLookup Service internally to retrieve the full TypeObject hierarchy
- Builds the remote type locally
- Invokes a callback with the resulting `DynamicType` (or `nullptr` if unsuccessful)

TypeLookup must be enabled in DomainParticipant QoS settings.

## 3) Retrieving Complete DynamicType from Remote Publisher

Yes, but with limitations. Simple types can be obtained directly via `on_type_discovery`. Complex types require enabling TypeLookup Service and using `register_remote_type()` to retrieve and register the complete hierarchy.

## 4) TypeObject vs DynamicType Relationship

`TypeObject` is the IDL representation (either Complete or Minimal form) that fully describes a data type. It serves as the foundation from which a `DynamicType` can be constructed locally, enabling runtime type discovery and manipulation.

## 4) TypeObject vs DynamicType Relationship

`TypeObject` is the IDL representation (either Complete or Minimal form) that fully describes a data type. It serves as the foundation from which a `DynamicType` can be constructed locally, enabling runtime type discovery and manipulation.

content.text:
Good news вҖ” **yes, FastDDS has the full equivalent**. Here's the breakdown:

### DynamicType from remote publishers

FastDDS provides two mechanisms:

1. **`on_type_discovery()` callback** вҖ” for simple types, the `DomainParticipantListener` receives a `DynamicType*` directly when a remote publisher is discovered (analogous to RTI's `extensions().type()`)

2. **TypeLookup Service** вҖ” for complex types (which depend on other TypeObjects), you call `register_remote_type()` on the participant, which fetches the full TypeObject hierarchy and gives you a `DynamicType` via callback. This is the standard XTypes mechanism and **should work cross-vendor** since RTI also implements TypeLookup.

### IDL serialization

FastDDS 3.x has `idl_serialize()` вҖ” a direct equivalent of RTI's `rti::core::xtypes::to_string(..., idl)`:

```cpp
// RTI (current code, line 57-59 of dds_topic_discovery.cpp):
rti::core::xtypes::DynamicTypePrintFormatProperty print_format{...};
std::string schema_idl = rti::core::xtypes::to_string(type.get(), print_format);

// FastDDS equivalent:
std::string schema_idl = idl_serialize(dynamic_type);
```

Minor limitations: only 4 annotations supported (`@bit_bound`, `@extensibility`, `@key`, `@position`), and default values are omitted.

### The real question: cross-vendor interop

The catch isn't FastDDS's API вҖ” it's whether **RTI publishers send TypeObject/TypeInformation** in their discovery data. By default RTI does propagate type info, but this depends on QoS configuration. If RTI publishers have TypeObject propagation enabled (which is the default), a FastDDS participant on the same network should be able to:

1. Receive the TypeObject via builtin discovery
2. Build a `DynamicType` from it (via `on_type_discovery` or TypeLookup Service)
3. Serialize it to IDL with `idl_serialize()`

So the answer is: **the API exists in FastDDS, and the architecture would be the same**. The risk is in XTypes interop edge cases between vendors (complex nested types, non-standard annotations, etc.), which would need testing.

Sources:
- [Fast DDS 3.2.2 XTypes](https://fast-dds.docs.eprosima.com/en/v3.2.2/fastdds/xtypes/xtypes.html)
- [Fast DDS 3.1.2 Serialization Utilities (idl_serialize)](https://fast-dds.docs.eprosima.com/en/3.1.x/fastdds/xtypes/type_serializing.html)
- [Fast DDS 2.14.3 Dynamic Types Discovery](https://fast-dds.docs.eprosima.com/en/v2.14.3/fastdds/dynamic_types/discovery.html)
- [Fast DDS XTypes FAQ](https://fast-dds.docs.eprosima.com/en/latest/fastdds/faq/xtypes/xtypes.html)

eProsima Fast DDS supports the OMG Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types for DDS specification (also known as XTypes). This specification defines the following concepts: DDS supported type system, including the concept of extensible types that might evolve in time.

The search results also include documentation on:

- **Type Support and Definition**: A complete description of the dynamic definition of types can be found on the XTypes section.

- **Serialization Utilities**: Fast DDS provides methods to serialize XTypes objects, enabling efficient data exchange and manipulation within distributed systems.

content.content:
# Fast DDS 3.5.0 XTypes Overview

Based on the documentation, XTypes in Fast DDS 3.5.0 implements the OMG Extensible and Dynamic Topic Types specification with these key features:

## Core Capabilities

**Type System & Representation:**
- "DDS supported type system, including the concept of extensible types that might evolve in time"
- IDL and TypeObject representations for data types
- Data representation protocols over the wire

## Main Features & Subsections

1. **Remote Data Types Discovery** - Automatic discovery of remote data types via DDS builtin mechanisms, with configuration and example implementations

2. **Dynamic Language Binding** - API for defining and manipulating data types at runtime, including:
   - TypeDescriptor, MemberDescriptor, AnnotationDescriptor interfaces
   - DynamicTypeBuilderFactory for creating types
   - DynamicDataFactory for creating instances
   - Support for 12+ type categories (primitives, strings, enumerations, sequences, structures, unions, etc.)

3. **Serialization Utilities** - Converting between formats:
   - Dynamic Type to IDL conversion
   - DynamicData to JSON serialization
   - JSON to DynamicData deserialization

4. **IDL Parsing** - Creating Dynamic Types directly from IDL files with iteration capabilities over parsed types

## Notable Limitation

"Type compatibility rules among evolved types are still unsupported in Fast DDS."

content.content:
# XTypes Changes in Fast DDS v3

Based on the migration guide provided, here are the key XTypes-related changes:

## Type Discovery Enhancement

Fast DDS v3 introduces a significant feature: **"a new feature XTypes that allows to discover remote types."** This enhancement increases discovery traffic during startup. The guide recommends disabling type propagation if you experience high load during discovery.

## API Method Updates

Several type-related APIs were updated:

- `DynamicTypeBuilder XMLProfileManager::getDynamicTypeByName()` вҶ’ `get_dynamic_type_builder_from_xml_by_name()`
- `TopicDataType::getSerializedSizeProvider()` вҶ’ `TopicDataType::calculate_serialized_size()`
- `TopicDataType::createData()` вҶ’ `TopicDataType::create_data()`
- `TopicDataType::deleteData()` вҶ’ `TopicDataType::delete_data()`

## Compatibility Requirements

**"Fast DDS v3 requires Fast DDS Gen v4 for code generation."** You must regenerate all types using the compatible version.

## Built-in Topics Expansion

The migration guide notes that built-in topics (`SubscriptionBuiltinTopicData`, `PublicationBuiltinTopicData`, `ParticipantBuiltinTopicData`) were extended with additional fields and relocated to the `eprosima::fastdds::rtps::` namespace.

The guide doesn't explicitly detail changes to TypeLookup Service mechanics or idl_serialize specifics beyond these namespace and API updates.

input.prompt: Show EVERYTHING: how remote type discovery works, prerequisites, configuration, code examples. How do you get a DynamicType from a remote publisher? What callbacks exist?
Show EVERYTHING about idl_serialize and type serialization. Full API, code examples, limitations.

content.content:
# Remote Type Discovery in Fast DDS

## How It Works

Remote type discovery operates through a DDS-XTypes mechanism that exchanges type information at runtime. According to the documentation, "The remote data type discovery mechanism is based on the exchange of the data type information optimized in order to reduce the required bandwidth."

The system uses two key structures:

1. **TypeInformation** - Contains minimal and complete type descriptions with dependency information
2. **TypeObject** - A union holding either `CompleteTypeObject` (full description) or `MinimalTypeObject` (compatibility-checking data only)

The `TypeIdentifier` hashes data type information to uniquely identify types across the network.

## Prerequisites

Three conditions must be met:

1. Local types must be registered in the `ITypeObjectRegistry` automatically when calling `register_type()` if code was generated by Fast DDS-Gen with TypeObject support enabled

2. `TypeInformation` must arrive with endpoint discovery data; participants not sharing this information won't trigger remote discovery

3. If prerequisites aren't satisfied, endpoint matching falls back to comparing type names and topic names

## Configuration

Type propagation levels are configurable through the property policies documented in the framework.

## Getting DynamicTypes from Remote Publishers

The documentation provided does not include specific code examples showing how to obtain a `DynamicType` from a remote publisher or details about available callbacks. The page references a use-case section for implementation examples but doesn't contain that content.

content.content:
# Fast DDS Serialization Utilities: idl_serialize API

## Overview

The `idl_serialize` function converts `DynamicType` objects into their IDL (Interface Definition Language) representation, enabling type information to be expressed in a human-readable format suitable for data exchange and documentation.

## Function Signature

```cpp
ReturnCode_t idl_serialize(
    const DynamicType::_ref_type& dynamic_type,
    std::ostream& output);
```

## Core Functionality

According to the documentation, the method "serializes a `DynamicType` object to its IDL representation." It accepts a reference to a dynamic type and writes the corresponding IDL code to an output stream.

## Supported Annotations

The conversion supports only four builtin annotations:
- `@bit_bound`
- `@extensibility`
- `@key`
- `@position`

## Important Limitations

**Bitset Inheritance:** "The conversion to IDL of a Bitset with inheritance merges derived Bitsets with their base Bitset."

**Default Values:** "The conversion to IDL dismisses values explicitly set to their default value." For example, if a Bitmask's `@bit_bound` is explicitly set to 32 (the default), it won't appear in the serialized IDL output.

## Practical Example

```cpp
// Build remotely discovered type
DynamicType::_ref_type remote_type = 
    DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::get_instance()
        ->create_type_w_type_object(remote_type_object)
        ->build();

// Serialize to IDL
std::stringstream idl;
if (RETCODE_OK != idl_serialize(remote_type, idl)) {
    // Handle error
}

std::cout << idl.str() << std::endl;
```

## Return Value

Returns `RETCODE_OK` on success; other return codes indicate failure during serialization.

input.command: gh release view v3.5.0.0 --repo eProsima/Fast-DDS --json body --jq '.body'
input.description: Fetch Fast DDS v3.5.0.0 release notes via gh CLI

content.content:
# Fast DDS v3.5.0

This release includes the following **improvements**:

1. Update Qt version to 6.8.3 in CI (#6223)
2. Verify Safe DDS signature
3. Open RTPSParticipantImpl for extension (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6222)
4. Use new sample rejection value (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6233)
5. Make DomainParticipant::get_instance_handle return by value (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6149)
6. Discovery Server clients always behave as super clients (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6272)
7. Enhance filtering logic for virtual and external endpoints (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6290)
8. Refactor Secure Discovery Server (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6295)
9. Update foonathan.net links (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6264)
10. Apply uncrustify to all source files (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6286)
11. Remove unused keep_alive logic (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6317)
12. RPC refactor (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6308)
13. Bump version to 3.5.0 (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6262)
14. Extend DomainParticipant API to include return codes (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6314)
15. Regenerate types with Fast DDS Gen 4.3.0 (#6318)

This release includes the following **fixes**: 

1. Fix compilation on PDPTests (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6226)
2. Force history QoS consistency and change resource limits default values (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6261)
3. Fix wrong update of default locators (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6273)
4. Fix ABI compliance (#6319)
5. Update CMake minimum required and Tier1 (#6274)

This release includes the following **CI improvements**: 

1. Branchout 3.5.x (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6220)
2. Updates after v3.3.1 EOL (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6282)
3. Run xtypes test cases in parallel (https://github.com/eProsima/Fast-DDS/pull/6294)
4. Update CMake minimum required and Tier1 (#6274)

PRs in merged order:

#6220, #6223, #6222, #6226, #6233, #6261, #6273, #6272, #6282, #6294, #6290, #6295, #6264, #6286, #6317, #6308, #6314, #6318, #6319, #6274

content.content:
# Fast DDS v3.4.0

This minor release includes the following **features**:

* Changes to support __Fast DDS Pro__ features:
  * Infrastructure modifications (#6005, #6035, #6057, #6058, #6060, #6069, #6072, #6063)
  * Add tweak version (#6012)
  * Prepare RTPSDomainImpl to be extended (#6031)
  * Support for `@feed` operations only in _Fast DDS Pro_ (#6052)
  * Add support for ethernet locator kind (#5999)
  * Add support for priority mappings in UDP (#6029)
  * Add XML tags for low bandwidth transports (#6051)
* Pass value of `TransportPriorityQosPolicy` to transport layer (#5933)
* Add field `original_writer_info` to `WriteParams` and `SampleInfo` (#5975, #5977, #6001)
* Support annotated types and builtin annotations in IDL Parser (#5908)
* Iterate over declared types processed with IDL Parser (#5954)
* Support aliases in Content Filtered Topic (#6032)

This minor release includes the following **improvements**:

* Ubuntu installers are now `.deb` packages
* Update to Fast DDS Gen 4.2.0 (#5959, #6019, #6050, #6071)
* Return sample notifying changes on instance state (#5943)
* Performance improvements with large history caches (#5986, #5997, #5998)
* Fix doxygen documentation typos (#5951, #6034)
* Change eprosima::fastdds::rtps::octet to be uint8_t according to C++11 standard (#5960)
* Add 'tcp_negotiation_timeout' to XML Schema (#5983)

This minor release includes the following **fixes**:

* Fix memory consumption issues in PKIDH plugin (#5935)
* Fix tests build with strict C++11 (#6061, #6074)
* Fix socket buffer size handling (#5926)
* Avoid setting thread affinity of 0 in mac (#5962)
* Some RPC related fixes (#5972, #5974)
* Fix latency & troughput tests names (#5938)
* Allow empty partition list to match against `"*"` (#5989)
* Fix duplicated transport when using specific XML with CLI (#5988)
* Fix topic argument in Discovery Server example (#5996)
* Some TCP transport fixes (#6073, #6066)
* Remote writers considered volatile by default (#6085)
* Fixed CPU usage issue with very short deadline period (#6016)

This minor release includes the following **CI and release management updates**:

* Prepare for 3.4.0 release (#5940, #5950, #5964, #6038, #6065, #6075, #6089)
* Add `--quiet` to `git submodule` invocations in CI (#5944)
* Split Mac CI build and testing phases (#6011)
* Update fallback branches to use environment variables (#6039)

PRs in merged order:
#5926, #5938, #5940, #5944, #5933, #5943, #5950, #5959, #5908, #5964, #5951, #5962, #5960, #5972, #5975, #5954, #5983, #5974, #5977, #5986, #5989, #5988, #6005, #6001, #5996, #6012, #5935, #5999, #5998, #6011, #5997, #6019, #6029, #6034, #6038, #6039, #6032, #6035, #6050, #6057, #6031, #6051, #6058, #6052, #6060, #6061, #6063, #6065, #6069, #6071, #6072, #6075, #6074, #6073, #6066, #6085, #6016, #6089

content.text:
Two items in 3.4.0 are directly relevant to us:

1. **"Support annotated types and builtin annotations in IDL Parser" (#5908)** вҖ” Improves the IDL parsing/serialization path. More annotations are handled correctly, which matters for `idl_serialize()` output fidelity.

2. **"Iterate over declared types processed with IDL Parser" (#5954)** вҖ” Lets you enumerate all types parsed from IDL. Useful if we ever need to parse IDL strings back into DynamicTypes (round-trip).

Beyond that, 3.4.0 and 3.5.0 are mostly infra/perf/TCP fixes. The core XTypes remote discovery + `idl_serialize()` that we need was already in place since 3.0/3.1.

**Summary**: Fast DDS 3.4.0 via Conan has everything we need вҖ” remote type discovery via TypeObject exchange, `DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::create_type_w_type_object()`, and `idl_serialize()`. Nothing in 3.5.0 adds anything we'd miss.

    34вҶ’class RtiSubscriptionManager : public SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    35вҶ’ public:
    36вҶ’  explicit RtiSubscriptionManager(DdsSubscriptionManager& inner);
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’  void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) override;
    39вҶ’  bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) override;
    40вҶ’  bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) override;
    41вҶ’  void unsubscribe_all() override;
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’ private:
    44вҶ’  DdsSubscriptionManager& inner_;
    45вҶ’};
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    48вҶ’
