    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <string>
    23вҶ’#include <vector>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// A discovered topic with its fully-qualified name and message type.
    28вҶ’struct TopicInfo {
    29вҶ’  std::string name;  ///< e.g. "/sensor/imu"
    30вҶ’  std::string type;  ///< e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu"
    31вҶ’};
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Abstract interface for discovering topics and retrieving their schemas.
    34вҶ’///
    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Return the encoding identifier for schemas produced by this source.
    58вҶ’  /// Used in the subscribe response so clients know how to parse the schema.
    59вҶ’  /// @return "ros2msg" for ROS2, "omgidl" for RTI DDS.
    60вҶ’  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
    61вҶ’};
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    64вҶ’

1вҶ’/*
     2вҶ’ * Copyright (C) 2026 Davide Faconti
     3вҶ’ *
     4вҶ’ * This file is part of pj_bridge.
     5вҶ’ *
     6вҶ’ * pj_bridge is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify
     7вҶ’ * it under the terms of the GNU Affero General Public License as published by
     8вҶ’ * the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or
     9вҶ’ * (at your option) any later version.
    10вҶ’ *
    11вҶ’ * pj_bridge is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
    12вҶ’ * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
    13вҶ’ * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the
    14вҶ’ * GNU Affero General Public License for more details.
    15вҶ’ *
    16вҶ’ * You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License
    17вҶ’ * along with pj_bridge. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’#include <functional>
    25вҶ’#include <memory>
    26вҶ’#include <string>
    27вҶ’#include <vector>
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    80вҶ’

    12вҶ’ * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
    13вҶ’ * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the
    14вҶ’ * GNU Affero General Public License for more details.
    15вҶ’ *
    16вҶ’ * You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License
    17вҶ’ * along with pj_bridge. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    23вҶ’#include <functional>
    24вҶ’#include <string>
    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’#include "tl/expected.hpp"
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Abstract transport layer between BridgeServer and clients.
    32вҶ’///
    33вҶ’/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37вҶ’///
    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    85вҶ’

1вҶ’cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
     2вҶ’project(pj_bridge)
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’# Compiler settings
     5вҶ’if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
     6вҶ’  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
     7вҶ’endif()
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
    10вҶ’set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# Sanitizer options
    13вҶ’option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
    14вҶ’if(ENABLE_TSAN)
    15вҶ’  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
    16вҶ’  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
    17вҶ’endif()
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
    20вҶ’if(ENABLE_ASAN)
    21вҶ’  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
    22вҶ’  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
    23вҶ’endif()
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’# Custom cmake modules
    26вҶ’list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’# ============================================================================
    29вҶ’# Backend detection
    30вҶ’# ============================================================================
    31вҶ’find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
    32вҶ’# RTI Connext DDS вҖ” only look for it if explicitly requested or available
    33вҶ’option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
    36вҶ’  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_ros2")
    37вҶ’endif()
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’if(ENABLE_RTI)
    40вҶ’  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
    41вҶ’  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    42вҶ’    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_rti")
    43вҶ’  else()
    44вҶ’    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found вҖ” skipping RTI backend")
    45вҶ’  endif()
    46вҶ’endif()
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’# ============================================================================
    49вҶ’# Common dependencies (for app layer)
    50вҶ’# ============================================================================
    51вҶ’find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’# IXWebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
    54вҶ’option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
    55вҶ’if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
    56вҶ’  message(STATUS "Using vendored IXWebSocket (3rdparty/ixwebsocket)")
    57вҶ’  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    58вҶ’  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    59вҶ’  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    60вҶ’  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
    61вҶ’else()
    62вҶ’  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
    63вҶ’endif()
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’# spdlog вҖ” prefer system package (required for ROS2 ABI compatibility with
    66вҶ’# librcl_logging_spdlog), fall back to FetchContent for standalone builds.
    67вҶ’include(FetchContent)
    68вҶ’find_package(spdlog QUIET)
    69вҶ’if(NOT spdlog_FOUND)
    70вҶ’  message(STATUS "System spdlog not found вҖ” fetching via FetchContent")
    71вҶ’  FetchContent_Declare(
    72вҶ’    spdlog
    73вҶ’    URL https://github.com/gabime/spdlog/archive/refs/tags/v1.15.0.tar.gz
    74вҶ’    URL_HASH SHA256=9962648c9b4f1a7bbc76fd8d9172555bad1871fdb14ff4f842ef87949682caa5
    75вҶ’  )
    76вҶ’  set(SPDLOG_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    77вҶ’  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(spdlog)
    78вҶ’else()
    79вҶ’  message(STATUS "Using system spdlog ${spdlog_VERSION}")
    80вҶ’endif()
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’# ============================================================================
    83вҶ’# Data path for tests
    84вҶ’# ============================================================================
    85вҶ’get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
    86вҶ’configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
    87вҶ’add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
    88вҶ’target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’# ============================================================================
    91вҶ’# pj_bridge_app вҖ” backend-agnostic core library
    92вҶ’# ============================================================================
    93вҶ’add_library(pj_bridge_app STATIC
    94вҶ’  app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
    95вҶ’  app/src/message_buffer.cpp
    96вҶ’  app/src/session_manager.cpp
    97вҶ’  app/src/message_serializer.cpp
    98вҶ’  app/src/bridge_server.cpp
    99вҶ’)
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’target_include_directories(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
   102вҶ’  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/app/include
   103вҶ’  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/3rdparty
   104вҶ’)
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
   107вҶ’  zstd::libzstd_shared
   108вҶ’  ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket
   109вҶ’  spdlog::spdlog
   110вҶ’)
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’# ============================================================================
   113вҶ’# pj_bridge_ros2 вҖ” ROS2 backend (only if ament_cmake found)
   114вҶ’# ============================================================================
   115вҶ’if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
   116вҶ’  find_package(ament_index_cpp REQUIRED)
   117вҶ’  find_package(rclcpp REQUIRED)
   118вҶ’  find_package(sensor_msgs REQUIRED)
   119вҶ’  find_package(nav_msgs REQUIRED)
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’  # ROS2 plugin library (sources shared between executable and tests)
   122вҶ’  add_library(pj_bridge_ros2_lib STATIC
   123вҶ’    ros2/src/topic_discovery.cpp
   124вҶ’    ros2/src/schema_extractor.cpp
   125вҶ’    ros2/src/generic_subscription_manager.cpp
   126вҶ’    ros2/src/message_stripper.cpp
   127вҶ’    ros2/src/ros2_topic_source.cpp
   128вҶ’    ros2/src/ros2_subscription_manager.cpp
   129вҶ’  )
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   132вҶ’    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/ros2/include
   133вҶ’  )
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   136вҶ’    pj_bridge_app
   137вҶ’  )
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   140вҶ’    ament_index_cpp
   141вҶ’    rclcpp
   142вҶ’    sensor_msgs
   143вҶ’    nav_msgs
   144вҶ’  )
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’  # ROS2 executable
   147вҶ’  add_executable(pj_bridge_ros2
   148вҶ’    ros2/src/main.cpp
   149вҶ’  )
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2
   152вҶ’    pj_bridge_ros2_lib
   153вҶ’  )
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2

    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <string>
    23вҶ’#include <vector>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// A discovered topic with its fully-qualified name and message type.
    28вҶ’struct TopicInfo {
    29вҶ’  std::string name;  ///< e.g. "/sensor/imu"
    30вҶ’  std::string type;  ///< e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu"
    31вҶ’};
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Abstract interface for discovering topics and retrieving their schemas.
    34вҶ’///
    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Return the encoding identifier for schemas produced by this source.
    58вҶ’  /// Used in the subscribe response so clients know how to parse the schema.
    59вҶ’  /// @return "ros2msg" for ROS2, "omgidl" for RTI DDS.
    60вҶ’  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
    61вҶ’};
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’#include <functional>
    25вҶ’#include <memory>
    26вҶ’#include <string>
    27вҶ’#include <vector>

    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

1вҶ’/*
     2вҶ’ * Copyright (C) 2026 Davide Faconti

     9вҶ’ * (at your option) any later version.
    10вҶ’ *
    11вҶ’ * pj_bridge is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
    12вҶ’ * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
    13вҶ’ * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the
    14вҶ’ * GNU Affero General Public License for more details.
    15вҶ’ *
    16вҶ’ * You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License
    17вҶ’ * along with pj_bridge. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <memory>
    23вҶ’#include <mutex>
    24вҶ’#include <nlohmann/json.hpp>
    25вҶ’#include <string>
    26вҶ’#include <unordered_map>
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/message_buffer.hpp"
    29вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/middleware/middleware_interface.hpp"
    30вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/session_manager.hpp"
    31вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/subscription_manager_interface.hpp"
    32вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/topic_source_interface.hpp"
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/**
    37вҶ’ * @brief Main bridge server that coordinates all components
    38вҶ’ *
    39вҶ’ * Orchestrates the bridge by managing:
    40вҶ’ * - Client sessions and heartbeats
    41вҶ’ * - Topic discovery and subscriptions (via abstract interfaces)
    42вҶ’ * - Message buffering and aggregation
    43вҶ’ * - API request/response handling
    44вҶ’ *
    45вҶ’ * Thread-safe for concurrent client connections.
    46вҶ’ * Event loop is driven externally (no internal timers).
    47вҶ’ */
    48вҶ’class BridgeServer {
    49вҶ’ public:
    50вҶ’  struct StatsSnapshot {
    51вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts;
    52вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts;
    53вҶ’    uint64_t publish_cycles;
    54вҶ’    uint64_t total_bytes_published;
    55вҶ’  };
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /**
    58вҶ’   * @brief Constructor
    59вҶ’   * @param topic_source Backend-specific topic discovery and schema provider
    60вҶ’   * @param subscription_manager Backend-specific subscription manager
    61вҶ’   * @param middleware Middleware interface for network communication
    62вҶ’   * @param port Server port (default: 8080)
    63вҶ’   * @param session_timeout Session timeout in seconds (default: 10.0)
    64вҶ’   * @param publish_rate Message aggregation publish rate in Hz (default: 50.0)
    65вҶ’   */
    66вҶ’  explicit BridgeServer(
    67вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source,
    68вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager,
    69вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware, int port = 8080, double session_timeout = 10.0,
    70вҶ’      double publish_rate = 50.0);
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /// Shuts down middleware before members are destroyed, preventing
    73вҶ’  /// disconnect callbacks from firing into a partially destroyed object.
    74вҶ’  ~BridgeServer();
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /**
    77вҶ’   * @brief Initialize the bridge server
    78вҶ’   * @return true if initialization successful, false otherwise
    79вҶ’   */
    80вҶ’  bool initialize();
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  /**
    83вҶ’   * @brief Process incoming API requests
    84вҶ’   *
    85вҶ’   * Non-blocking call that checks for pending requests,
    86вҶ’   * processes them, and sends responses.
    87вҶ’   *
    88вҶ’   * @return true if a request was processed, false if no requests pending
    89вҶ’   */
    90вҶ’  bool process_requests();
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’  /**
    93вҶ’   * @brief Publish aggregated messages to connected clients
    94вҶ’   *
    95вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. at 50 Hz).
    96вҶ’   * Moves messages from the buffer, serializes per subscription group,
    97вҶ’   * compresses with ZSTD, and sends binary frames.
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  void publish_aggregated_messages();
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’  /**
   102вҶ’   * @brief Check for timed-out sessions and clean them up
   103вҶ’   *
   104вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. every 1 second).
   105вҶ’   */
   106вҶ’  void check_session_timeouts();
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  /**
   109вҶ’   * @brief Get the number of active client sessions
   110вҶ’   */
   111вҶ’  size_t get_active_session_count() const;
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’  /**
   114вҶ’   * @brief Get statistics about published messages
   115вҶ’   * @return Pair of (total_messages_published, total_bytes_published)
   116вҶ’   */
   117вҶ’  std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t> get_publish_stats() const;
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’  /**
   120вҶ’   * @brief Snapshot and reset statistics counters
   121вҶ’   */
   122вҶ’  StatsSnapshot snapshot_and_reset_stats();
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’ private:
   125вҶ’  std::string handle_get_topics(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   126вҶ’  std::string handle_subscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   127вҶ’  std::string handle_unsubscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   128вҶ’  std::string handle_heartbeat(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   129вҶ’  std::string handle_pause(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   130вҶ’  std::string handle_resume(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   131вҶ’  std::string create_error_response(
   132вҶ’      const std::string& error_code, const std::string& message, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   133вҶ’  void inject_response_fields(nlohmann::json& response, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   134вҶ’  void cleanup_session(const std::string& client_id);
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’  // Backend interfaces (owned)
   137вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source_;
   138вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager_;
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’  // Core components
   141вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware_;
   142вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MessageBuffer> message_buffer_;
   143вҶ’  std::unique_ptr<SessionManager> session_manager_;
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’  // Configuration
   146вҶ’  int port_;
   147вҶ’  double session_timeout_;
   148вҶ’  double publish_rate_;
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’  // State
   151вҶ’  bool initialized_;
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’  // Statistics
   154вҶ’  uint64_t total_messages_published_;
   155вҶ’  uint64_t total_bytes_published_;
   156вҶ’  uint64_t publish_cycles_;
   157вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts_;
   158вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts_;
   159вҶ’  mutable std::mutex stats_mutex_;
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’  // Lock ordering (to prevent deadlock):
   162вҶ’  //   cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   163вҶ’  // SessionManager::mutex_ may be acquired while holding any of these.
   164вҶ’  // Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   165вҶ’  std::mutex cleanup_mutex_;
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’  // Per-client per-topic last-sent timestamp (nanoseconds) for rate limiting
   168вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t>> last_sent_times_;
   169вҶ’  std::mutex last_sent_mutex_;
   170вҶ’};
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    16вҶ’# * Try to find Facebook zstd library This will define ZSTD_FOUND
    17вҶ’#   ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_LIBRARY
    18вҶ’#
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’if(UNIX)
    21вҶ’  find_package(PkgConfig QUIET)
    22вҶ’  pkg_search_module(PC_ZSTD libzstd)
    23вҶ’endif()
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’find_path(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR zstd.h
    26вҶ’  HINTS
    27вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDEDIR}
    28вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIRS}
    29вҶ’)
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’find_library(ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
    32вҶ’  NAMES ${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
    33вҶ’  HINTS
    34вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    35вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
    36вҶ’)
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’find_library(ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY
    39вҶ’  NAMES ${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
    40вҶ’  HINTS
    41вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    42вҶ’    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
    43вҶ’)
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’include(FindPackageHandleStandardArgs)
    46вҶ’find_package_handle_standard_args(ZSTD
    47вҶ’  DEFAULT_MSG
    48вҶ’  ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR
    49вҶ’  ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
    50вҶ’  ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY
    51вҶ’)
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’if(ZSTD_FOUND)
    54вҶ’  message(STATUS "Found Zstd: shared=${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}, static=${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}")
    55вҶ’else()
    56вҶ’  message(STATUS "ZSTD not found")
    57вҶ’  return()
    58вҶ’endif()
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’mark_as_advanced(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’# Create imported targets

    65вҶ’  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_shared PROPERTIES
    66вҶ’    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}"
    67вҶ’    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
    68вҶ’  )
    69вҶ’endif()
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’if(NOT TARGET zstd::libzstd_static AND ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)
    72вҶ’  add_library(zstd::libzstd_static STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
    73вҶ’  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_static PROPERTIES
    74вҶ’    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}"
    75вҶ’    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
    76вҶ’  )
    77вҶ’endif()

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis of the RTI backend implementation.

## Complete RTI Backend Implementation Overview

I have read **all 9 files** in the `rti/` directory completely. Here is the thorough breakdown:

---

### **File Structure**

**Headers (4 files):**
- `include/pj_bridge_rti/dds_topic_discovery.hpp`
- `include/pj_bridge_rti/dds_subscription_manager.hpp`
- `include/pj_bridge_rti/rti_topic_source.hpp`
- `include/pj_bridge_rti/rti_subscription_manager.hpp`

**Sources (5 files):**
- `src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp`
- `src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp`
- `src/rti_topic_source.cpp`
- `src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp`
- `src/main.cpp`

---

### **1. DDS Topic Discovery (`dds_topic_discovery.*`)**

**Purpose:** Discover available DDS topics across one or more DDS domains using the built-in publication topic reader.

**Key Components:**

- **Struct `DdsTopicInfo`**: Simple data carrier (name, type_name)

- **Struct `DiscoveredTopic`**: Internal cache entry containing:
  - `type_name`: DDS type identifier
  - `struct_type`: RTI's `dds::core::xtypes::StructType` (the actual type descriptor)
  - `schema_idl`: Pre-serialized OMG IDL schema text
  - `domain_id`: Which domain the topic came from

- **Inner class `PublisherListener`**:
  - Inherits from `dds::sub::NoOpDataReaderListener<dds::topic::PublicationBuiltinTopicData>`
  - Implements `on_data_available()` callback
  - Called whenever a new publisher appears on the DDS bus
  - Validates that the type is a STRUCT_TYPE (skips other types)
  - Uses `rti::core::xtypes::to_string()` with `DynamicTypePrintFormatProperty` to convert the type to IDL text
  - Filters out topics that don't have valid type info
  - Deduplicates: only adds topic if it hasn't been seen before

**Constructor:**
```cpp
DdsTopicDiscovery(const std::vector<int32_t>& domain_ids, const std::string& qos_profile_path = "")
```
- Creates one `DomainParticipant` per domain ID
- Sets up built-in subscriber access via `dds::sub::builtin_subscriber()`
- Finds the publication built-in reader using `dds::sub::find()`
- Attaches `PublisherListener` to each built-in reader for on-data-available callbacks
- Optionally loads a QoS profile XML file

**Destructor:**
- Carefully removes listeners, closes readers, then closes participants
- Has comprehensive error handling with spdlog warnings

**Public Methods:**
- `get_topics()`: Snapshots the discovered topics, returns vector of DdsTopicInfo
- `get_schema(topic_name)`: Returns the pre-cached IDL schema string
- `get_type(topic_name)`: Returns the `StructType` object (used during subscription setup)
- `get_domain_id(topic_name)`: Returns which domain a topic belongs to
- `get_participant(domain_id)`: Retrieves the `DomainParticipant` for a domain

**Thread Safety:** Uses `std::shared_mutex` (read lock for gets, write lock for on_topic_discovered)

---

### **2. DDS Subscription Manager (`dds_subscription_manager.*`)**

**Purpose:** Handle DDS subscriptions with reference counting and callback dispatch.

**Key Components:**

- **Callback Type:**
  ```cpp
  using DdsMessageCallback = std::function<void(
      const std::string& topic_name, 
      std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, 
      uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
  ```

- **Struct `SubscriptionInfo`**: Stores per-subscription state:
  - `subscriber`: The `dds::sub::Subscriber` (one per topic)
  - `reader`: The `dds::sub::DataReader<dds::core::xtypes::DynamicData>` (dynamic, not typed)
  - `listener`: Shared ptr to the reader's listener
  - `reference_count`: For multi-client reference counting

- **Inner class `InternalReaderListener`**:
  - Inherits from `dds::sub::NoOpDataReaderListener<dds::core::xtypes::DynamicData>`
  - Implements `on_data_available()` for incoming DDS messages
  - **Key insight**: Extracts CDR buffer from `DynamicData` using `get_cdr_buffer()` which returns `(const char*, size_t)` pair
  - Creates `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` from the raw CDR bytes
  - Extracts `source_timestamp()` from sample info, converts to nanoseconds since epoch
  - Calls the global callback with `(topic_name, cdr_data, timestamp_ns)`
  - Thread-safe: acquires mutex to fetch the callback before calling it

**Subscribe Logic:**
1. Checks if topic already subscribed вҶ’ increment ref count and return true
2. Looks up the `StructType` from discovery
3. Gets the domain ID from discovery
4. Registers the type with RTI using `rti::domain::register_type()` and `DynamicDataTypeSerializationProperty` (with `skip_deserialization=true` for performance)
5. Creates a `Topic<DynamicData>` with the topic name and type name
6. Creates a `Subscriber` and `DataReader<DynamicData>` on that topic
7. Attaches listener to reader
8. Stores in `subscriptions_` map with ref count = 1

**Unsubscribe Logic:**
- Decrements ref count; if zero, removes from map and closes reader/subscriber outside the lock
- Prevents deadlocks by closing readers outside the critical section

**unsubscribe_all():**
- Swaps all subscriptions out, releases lock
- Closes all readers and subscribers

**Thread Safety:** Uses `std::mutex` (simple lock)

---

### **3. RTI Topic Source (`rti_topic_source.*`)**

**Purpose:** Thin adapter implementing `TopicSourceInterface` for the abstract core library.

**Implementation:**
- Holds reference to `DdsTopicDiscovery`
- `get_topics()`: Calls `discovery_.get_topics()`, maps `DdsTopicInfo` вҶ’ `TopicInfo` (same fields)
- `get_schema()`: Delegates to `discovery_.get_schema()`
- `schema_encoding()`: Returns `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl` (constant = `"omgidl"`)

**Role:** Pure adapter with no logic, enabling BridgeServer to be backend-agnostic.

---

### **4. RTI Subscription Manager (`rti_subscription_manager.*`)**

**Purpose:** Thin adapter implementing `SubscriptionManagerInterface` for the abstract core library.

**Implementation:**
- Holds reference to `DdsSubscriptionManager`
- `set_message_callback()`: Forwards callback to inner manager (signatures are identical)

- `unsubscribe_all()`: Delegates to inner

**Key insight:** The callback signature matches exactly, so it's a 1:1 passthrough.

---

### **5. Main Event Loop (`main.cpp`)**

**Architecture:** Standalone executable with manual event loop (no rclcpp timers).

**Initialization (lines 45вҖ“98):**
1. Parse CLI flags using **CLI11** library:
   - `--domains` (required, multi-value): Domain IDs to monitor
   - `--port` (default 8080): WebSocket port
   - `--publish-rate` (default 50 Hz): Message publication frequency
   - `--session-timeout` (default 10 s): Client heartbeat timeout
   - `--qos-profile` (optional): QoS profile XML file
   - `--log-level` (default "info"): spdlog level
   - `--stats` (flag): Enable 5-second statistics printing
2. Set spdlog level from CLI
3. Create `DdsTopicDiscovery` (spawns participants, starts listening for topics)
4. Create `DdsSubscriptionManager` (reference to discovery)
5. Wrap both in adapters: `RtiTopicSource` and `RtiSubscriptionManager`
6. Create `WebSocketMiddleware` (IXWebSocket-based server)
7. Create `BridgeServer` (the main orchestrator)
8. Call `server.initialize()` to start WebSocket listening

**Event Loop (lines 111вҖ“151):**
```
while (!g_shutdown.load()):
  1. process_requests()        -- handle WebSocket frames, API commands
  2. Check if publish_interval elapsed вҶ’ publish_aggregated_messages()
  3. Check if 1 sec elapsed вҶ’ check_session_timeouts()
  4. Optional: every 5 sec вҶ’ print stats (receive rates, publish freq, throughput)
  5. Sleep 1 ms
```

| `dds::sub::builtin_subscriber()` | RTI Connext | Access to built-in topics (publications, subscriptions) |
| `dds::topic::PublicationBuiltinTopicData` | RTI Connext | Metadata about publishers (topic name, type) |
| `dds::sub::DataReader<T>` | RTI Connext | Receives messages from DDS topics |
| `dds::sub::NoOpDataReaderListener<T>` | RTI Connext | Base class for listeners (all no-op except on_data_available) |
| `dds::core::xtypes::StructType` | RTI Connext | Type descriptor for structured types |
| `dds::core::xtypes::DynamicData` | RTI Connext | Runtime-typed message (no generated code) |
| `rti::core::xtypes::to_string()` | RTI Connext | Converts type to IDL text with formatting options |
| `rti::domain::register_type()` | RTI Connext | Register a type with a participant before using it |
| `rti::core::QosProviderParams` | RTI Connext | QoS configuration from XML file |
| `dds::sub::status::DataState::new_instance()` | RTI Connext | Filter for new topic discoveries |
| `sample.info().valid()` | RTI Connext | Check if a sample contains valid data (not just metadata) |
| `sample.info().source_timestamp()` | RTI Connext | Get the sender's timestamp |

---

### **Key Design Patterns**

1. **Two-Level Architecture:**
   - **Lower level** (`Dds*` classes): Raw RTI DDS APIs, complex type handling, listener callbacks
   - **Upper level** (`Rti*` adapters): Simple passthrough to interface contracts

2. **Built-in Topic Discovery:**
   - Avoids manual DomainParticipant scanning
   - Automatically notified of new publishers via listener
   - Schema extracted on-the-fly from type extension

3. **Dynamic Types:**
   - Uses `DynamicData` (no code generation needed)
   - Type metadata extracted from `PublicationBuiltinTopicData`

4. **Reference-Counted Subscriptions:**
   - Multiple clients can subscribe to same topic
   - First subscriber creates DDS DataReader
   - Last unsubscriber destroys it
   - Ref count tracks active clients

5. **Careful Shutdown Sequencing:**
   - Clear callback вҶ’ unsubscribe all вҶ’ shutdown middleware
   - Prevents use-after-free and dangling references

6. **CDR Serialization:**
   - DynamicData provides `.get_cdr_buffer()` directly
   - No deserialization overhead (skip_deserialization=true)
   - Direct byte copy to `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`

---

### **Critical Implementation Details for FastDDS Port**

**Similarities to expect:**
- Topic discovery via built-in topics
- Dynamic data handling (DynamicData equivalent)
- Listener-based callbacks for topic discovery and message reception
- Reference-counted subscriptions

**Potential differences to handle:**
- FastDDS may use different APIs for accessing built-in readers
- Type representation and IDL serialization may differ
- CDR buffer extraction may have different API
- Timestamp format/extraction may vary
- Shutdown sequence may need adjustment

  - `domain_id`: Which domain the topic came from

- **Inner class `PublisherListener`**:

  - Implements `on_data_available()` callback
  - Called whenever a new publisher appears on the DDS bus
  - Validates that the type is a STRUCT_TYPE (skips other types)
  - Uses `rti::core::xtypes::to_string()` with `DynamicTypePrintFormatProperty` to convert the type to IDL text

- Finds the publication built-in reader using `dds::sub::find()`
- Attaches `PublisherListener` to each built-in reader for on-data-available callbacks
- Optionally loads a QoS profile XML file

- `get_domain_id(topic_name)`: Returns which domain a topic belongs to
- `get_participant(domain_id)`: Retrieves the `DomainParticipant` for a domain

**Thread Safety:** Uses `std::shared_mutex` (read lock for gets, write lock for on_topic_discovered)

### **2. DDS Subscription Manager (`dds_subscription_manager.*`)**

**Purpose:** Handle DDS subscriptions with reference counting and callback dispatch.

      const std::string& topic_name, 
      std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, 
      uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;

  - `reader`: The `dds::sub::DataReader<dds::core::xtypes::DynamicData>` (dynamic, not typed)
  - `listener`: Shared ptr to the reader's listener
  - `reference_count`: For multi-client reference counting

- **Inner class `InternalReaderListener`**:

  - Implements `on_data_available()` for incoming DDS messages
  - **Key insight**: Extracts CDR buffer from `DynamicData` using `get_cdr_buffer()` which returns `(const char*, size_t)` pair
  - Creates `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` from the raw CDR bytes
  - Extracts `source_timestamp()` from sample info, converts to nanoseconds since epoch
  - Calls the global callback with `(topic_name, cdr_data, timestamp_ns)`
  - Thread-safe: acquires mutex to fetch the callback before calling it

**Subscribe Logic:**
1. Checks if topic already subscribed вҶ’ increment ref count and return true
2. Looks up the `StructType` from discovery
3. Gets the domain ID from discovery
4. Registers the type with RTI using `rti::domain::register_type()` and `DynamicDataTypeSerializationProperty` (with `skip_deserialization=true` for performance)
5. Creates a `Topic<DynamicData>` with the topic name and type name
6. Creates a `Subscriber` and `DataReader<DynamicData>` on that topic
7. Attaches listener to reader
8. Stores in `subscriptions_` map with ref count = 1

**Unsubscribe Logic:**
- Decrements ref count; if zero, removes from map and closes reader/subscriber outside the lock
- Prevents deadlocks by closing readers outside the critical section

**unsubscribe_all():**
- Swaps all subscriptions out, releases lock
- Closes all readers and subscribers

**Thread Safety:** Uses `std::mutex` (simple lock)

## Summary: Abstract Interfaces and Build System

I've thoroughly explored the pj_bridge architecture. Here's what a new FastDDS backend needs to implement and understand:

---

### **1. Abstract Interfaces (in `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/include/pj_bridge/`)**

#### **TopicSourceInterface** (`topic_source_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class TopicSourceInterface {
  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
};
```
- **Purpose**: Discover topics and retrieve their schemas
- **ROS2 Implementation** (`Ros2TopicSource`): Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`, returns schema encoding `"ros2msg"`
- **RTI Implementation** (`RtiTopicSource`): Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`, returns schema encoding `"omgidl"`
- **FastDDS needs**: Equivalent discovery mechanism and schema encoding (likely `"omgidl"` for FastDDS, similar to RTI)

#### **SubscriptionManagerInterface** (`subscription_manager_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
};
```
- **MessageCallback signature**: `void(const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)`
- **Purpose**: Manage middleware subscriptions with reference counting
- **ROS2 Implementation** (`Ros2SubscriptionManager`): Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager`, converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` вҶ’ `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
- **RTI Implementation** (`RtiSubscriptionManager`): Thin adapter over `DdsSubscriptionManager` (already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`)
- **FastDDS needs**: Reference-counted subscription to FastDDS DataReaders, message callback delivery in CDR format

#### **MiddlewareInterface** (`middleware/middleware_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class MiddlewareInterface {
  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
};
```
- **Provided**: `WebSocketMiddleware` in the core (already handles this for FastDDS, shared across all backends)

---

### **2. CMakeLists.txt Build System** (`/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/CMakeLists.txt`)

**Key Structure**:
- **Lines 29-46**: Backend detection via `find_package()`
  - ROS2: Uses `ament_cmake`
  - RTI: Controlled by `ENABLE_RTI=ON` option + `find_package(RTIConnextDDS)`
  - **FastDDS needs**: Add conditional backend detection (e.g., `ENABLE_FASTDDS=ON` + `find_package(fastcdr, fastrtps)`)

- **Lines 90-111**: `pj_bridge_app` (core library, backend-agnostic)
  - Depends on: `ZSTD`, `ixwebsocket`, `spdlog`
  - No middleware-specific dependencies

- **Lines 114-163**: ROS2 backend structure
  ```cmake
  if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
    add_library(pj_bridge_ros2_lib STATIC ...)  # Shared sources

- **Lines 167-205**: RTI backend structure

  if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)

  ```

### **3. Directory Structure Pattern**

---

### **4. Main.cpp Entry Point Pattern**

**ROS2** (`ros2/src/main.cpp`, lines 31-119):
- Uses `rclcpp::init()` and `rclcpp::Node`
- Declares parameters via `node->declare_parameter()`

#### **TopicSourceInterface** (`topic_source_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class TopicSourceInterface {
  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
};
```
- **Purpose**: Discover topics and retrieve their schemas
- **ROS2 Implementation** (`Ros2TopicSource`): Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`, returns schema encoding `"ros2msg"`
- **RTI Implementation** (`RtiTopicSource`): Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`, returns schema encoding `"omgidl"`
- **FastDDS needs**: Equivalent discovery mechanism and schema encoding (likely `"omgidl"` for FastDDS, similar to RTI)

#### **SubscriptionManagerInterface** (`subscription_manager_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
};
```
- **MessageCallback signature**: `void(const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)`
- **Purpose**: Manage middleware subscriptions with reference counting
- **ROS2 Implementation** (`Ros2SubscriptionManager`): Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager`, converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` вҶ’ `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
- **RTI Implementation** (`RtiSubscriptionManager`): Thin adapter over `DdsSubscriptionManager` (already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`)
- **FastDDS needs**: Reference-counted subscription to FastDDS DataReaders, message callback delivery in CDR format

#### **MiddlewareInterface** (`middleware/middleware_interface.hpp`)
```cpp
class MiddlewareInterface {
  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
};
```
- **Provided**: `WebSocketMiddleware` in the core (already handles this for FastDDS, shared across all backends)

input.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for adding a FastDDS backend to pj_bridge. Here's all the context:

## Project Structure
- `app/` вҖ” backend-agnostic core library (BridgeServer, MessageBuffer, SessionManager, WebSocketMiddleware)
- `ros2/` вҖ” ROS2 adapter (rclcpp-based)
- `rti/` вҖ” RTI Connext DDS adapter (to be disabled but not deleted)
- New: `fastdds/` вҖ” eProsima Fast DDS adapter

## Abstract Interfaces to Implement

**TopicSourceInterface** (app/include/pj_bridge/topic_source_interface.hpp):
```cpp
virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
```

**SubscriptionManagerInterface** (app/include/pj_bridge/subscription_manager_interface.hpp):
```cpp
using MessageCallback = std::function<void(const std::string&, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>>, uint64_t)>;
virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
```

## RTI Backend Structure (template to follow)

**Lower-level DDS classes:**
1. `DdsTopicDiscovery` вҖ” creates DomainParticipant per domain, listens to PublicationBuiltinTopicData, caches topic name + type_name + StructType + schema_idl. Uses `dds::sub::NoOpDataReaderListener<PublicationBuiltinTopicData>`. Key RTI-specific: `sample.data().extensions().type()` for type object, `rti::core::xtypes::to_string(type, idl_format)` for IDL generation.

2. `DdsSubscriptionManager` вҖ” reference-counted subscriptions. Creates DataReader<DynamicData> per topic. Uses `DynamicDataTypeSerializationProperty::skip_deserialization(true)` for performance. Extracts CDR via `sample.data().get_cdr_buffer()`. Extracts timestamp via `sample.info().source_timestamp()`.

**Thin adapter classes:**
3. `RtiTopicSource` вҖ” wraps DdsTopicDiscovery, implements TopicSourceInterface, returns schema_encoding = "omgidl"
4. `RtiSubscriptionManager` вҖ” wraps DdsSubscriptionManager, implements SubscriptionManagerInterface (1:1 passthrough)

**Entry point:**
5. `main.cpp` вҖ” CLI11 parsing (--domains, --port, --publish-rate, --session-timeout, --qos-profile, --log-level, --stats), standalone event loop with std::chrono + signal handlers

## FastDDS 3.4.0 API Equivalents

**Topic Discovery:**
- FastDDS uses `DomainParticipantListener::on_type_discovery()` callback to get DynamicType from remote publishers
- OR TypeLookup Service via `register_remote_type()` for complex types
- TypeObject propagation must be enabled

**IDL Serialization:**
```cpp
// FastDDS equivalent of RTI's to_string():
ReturnCode_t idl_serialize(const DynamicType::_ref_type& dynamic_type, std::ostream& output);
```

**CDR Buffer:**
- FastDDS: `SerializedPayload_t` from DataReader, raw bytes accessible via `.data` and `.length`
- Need to find equivalent of `skip_deserialization` вҖ” FastDDS may need different approach for raw CDR access

**Key Classes in FastDDS 3.4.0:**
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DomainParticipant`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DomainParticipantListener`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DataReader`, `DataReaderListener`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DynamicType`, `DynamicData`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DynamicTypeBuilderFactory`
- `idl_serialize()` for type-to-IDL conversion

## Build System

**Current CMakeLists.txt pattern:**
- `ENABLE_RTI` option + `find_package(RTIConnextDDS)` for RTI backend
- Need: `ENABLE_FASTDDS` option + `find_package(fastcdr REQUIRED)` + `find_package(fastrtps REQUIRED)` for FastDDS
- FastDDS to be acquired via Conan (fast-dds/3.4.0) вҖ” need conanfile.txt
- CLI11 already FetchContent'd for RTI, can reuse

**Targets to create:**
- `pj_bridge_fastdds_lib` (STATIC) вҖ” FastDDS adapter library
- `pj_bridge_fastdds` (EXECUTABLE) вҖ” Entry point

**RTI to be disabled:** Remove or comment out the `if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)` block, or just don't set ENABLE_RTI.

## Design Decisions Needed

1. **File naming**: Should lower-level classes keep "Dds" prefix (like RTI's DdsTopicDiscovery/DdsSubscriptionManager) or use "FastDds" prefix? I'd suggest "FastDds" to distinguish: `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`. Adapter classes: `FastDdsTopicSource`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`.

   Wait, that creates a name collision. Better: lower-level = `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` (the raw DDS implementation); adapters = `FastDdsTopicSource` (TopicSourceInterface), `FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter` (SubscriptionManagerInterface).

   Actually, looking at RTI pattern more carefully:
   - Lower: `DdsTopicDiscovery`, `DdsSubscriptionManager`
   - Adapters: `RtiTopicSource`, `RtiSubscriptionManager`
   
   For FastDDS, we could use the same pattern since the lower-level classes are DDS-generic in concept:
   - Lower: `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`
   - Adapters: `FastDdsTopicSource`, `FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter`
   
   Or simpler: just have the adapter classes directly without a separate lower layer, since both levels are FastDDS-specific anyway.

2. **Conan integration**: Need conanfile.txt in project root. Other workspace projects use CMakeDeps/CMakeToolchain generators.

3. **Multiple domains**: FastDDS supports multiple DomainParticipants вҖ” should mirror RTI's --domains flag.

4. **Raw CDR access without deserialization**: In FastDDS, this is typically done via `DataReader::take()` with raw sample access or by using `DynamicData` and extracting serialized payload. Need to verify the exact API for getting raw CDR from DynamicData in FastDDS 3.4.

Please design the implementation plan with:
- Directory structure
- File list with brief description of each
- API mapping (RTI вҶ’ FastDDS) for each critical operation
- Build system changes (CMakeLists.txt + conanfile.txt)
- Implementation order (what to implement first for fastest iteration)
- Testing strategy

    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Return the encoding identifier for schemas produced by this source.
    58вҶ’  /// Used in the subscribe response so clients know how to parse the schema.
    59вҶ’  /// @return "ros2msg" for ROS2, "omgidl" for RTI DDS.
    60вҶ’  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
    61вҶ’};
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’#include <functional>
    25вҶ’#include <memory>

    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    20вҶ’#pragma once

    22вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/topic_source_interface.hpp"
    23вҶ’#include "pj_bridge_rti/dds_topic_discovery.hpp"
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/**
    28вҶ’ * @brief RTI Connext DDS implementation of TopicSourceInterface
    29вҶ’ *
    30вҶ’ * Thin adapter over DdsTopicDiscovery, mapping DdsTopicInfo вҶ’ TopicInfo
    31вҶ’ * and forwarding schema/encoding queries.
    32вҶ’ */

Based on the search results, here's what I found about eProsima FastDDS 3.4 and the `idl_serialize` function for DynamicType IDL string conversion:

## idl_serialize Function

The `idl_serialize` function serializes a DynamicType into its IDL representation. The function signature is:

```cpp
ReturnCode_t eprosima::fastdds::dds::idl_serialize(const DynamicType::_ref_type &dynamic_type, std::ostream &output) noexcept
```

It returns `RETCODE_OK` when serialization fully succeeds, and inner (member serialization) failing code otherwise.

## DynamicType and IDL String Conversion

When a subscriber discovers a new DataReader or DataWriter, it uses the DynamicTypeBuilderFactory to build a DynamicType and serialize it to IDL format.

For string type handling in DynamicData serialization, string types are used to represent sequences of characters, which are essential for handling textual data within the system.

The search results reference documentation for Fast DDS 3.4.1, which is the latest stable version, confirming that this functionality is available in FastDDS 3.4.

Based on the search results, here's information about eProsima FastDDS 3.4 and the `on_type_discovery` callback:

## DomainParticipantListener and on_type_discovery

DomainParticipantListener is an abstract class defining the callbacks that will be triggered in response to state changes on the DomainParticipant. By default, all these callbacks are empty and do nothing. The user should implement a specialization of this class overriding the callbacks that are needed on the application.

## on_type_discovery Callback

When a participant discovers a remote endpoint that sends a complete TypeObject or a simple TypeIdentifier describing a type that the participant does not know, the participant listener's function on_type_discovery is called with the received TypeObject or TypeIdentifier, and, when possible, a pointer to a DynamicType ready to be used.

The on_type_discovery method is called when a participant discovers a new Type. The ownership of all object belongs to the caller so if needs to be used after the method ends, a full copy should be performed (except for dyn_type due to its shared_ptr nature).

## Discovery-Time Data Typing

Discovery-Time Data Typing allows the discovering of simple DynamicTypes. A TypeObject that depends on other TypeObjects, cannot be built locally using Discovery-Time Data Typing and should use TypeLookup Service instead.

The search results show documentation across multiple FastDDS versions including 3.4.1 (the latest), which confirm that `on_type_discovery` is a key mechanism for handling dynamic type discovery during the topic discovery process in DDS.

content.content:
# DomainParticipantListener Class API

Based on the documentation provided, here's a summary of the DomainParticipantListener class:

## Overview
The DomainParticipantListener is an abstract class that defines callbacks triggered by state changes on a DomainParticipant. It inherits from TopicListener, PublisherListener, and SubscriberListener, allowing it to handle events from all attached entities.

## Key Virtual Methods

The documented methods include:

1. **`on_participant_discovery()`** - Detects when a new DomainParticipant is discovered, removed, or changes its QoS. Features an overload with a `should_be_ignored` boolean parameter to ignore discovered participants without deadlock risk.

2. **`on_data_reader_discovery()`** - Triggered when a DataReader is discovered, removed, or modified in the domain, with optional ignoring capability.

3. **`on_data_writer_discovery()`** - Activated when a DataWriter is discovered, removed, or altered, also supporting selective ignoring.

4. **`onParticipantAuthentication()`** - Reports authentication results for remote DomainParticipants (available when security features are enabled).

## Implementation Notes

The documentation indicates that callbacks are empty by default. Developers should override only the callbacks needed for their application. If both the standard and ignore-capable discovery overloads are implemented, the version with the boolean flag takes precedence.

The class example demonstrates proper implementation patterns for handling discovery events and authentication scenarios.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding eProsima FastDDS 3.x and the topics you're asking about:

## Remote Type Discovery with `on_data_writer_discovery`

The `on_data_writer_discovery()` callback is called depending on the kind of endpoint discovered, and provides access to the remotely discovered information including the TypeInformation. The callback receives a `PublicationBuiltinTopicData` parameter (the type used for DataWriters, as opposed to `SubscriptionBuiltinTopicData` for DataReaders).

## Working with TypeInformation and TypeObjects

When provided with the TypeInformation, the ITypeObjectRegistry singleton can be queried for the corresponding TypeObject representation, and DynamicTypeBuilderFactory provides an API that given a TypeObject representation returns the corresponding DynamicTypeBuilder, and the DynamicType can then be obtained and registered using DynamicPubSubType.

## TypeLookup Service and `register_remote_type`

The `register_remote_type` function on the DomainParticipant simplifies the process of retrieving the remote type from its TypeInformation and registering it, as it internally uses the TypeLookup Service to retrieve the full TypeObject, and if successful, calls a callback.

## Practical Example Pattern

In the `on_data_writer_discovery` callback, you retrieve remote type information by querying the type object registry using `info.type_information.type_information.complete().typeid_with_size().type_id()`.

The documentation provides examples showing these patterns in the Fast DDS 3.x serialization utilities and remote type discovery documentation sections.

# Remote Type Discovery and Endpoint Matching - Code Example

Based on the documentation, here's a comprehensive guide to the process:

## Overview

The documentation provides a complete example in the `on_data_writer_discovery` callback that demonstrates all five steps you requested.

## Key Steps Implementation

**1) Discover remote types in callback:**
The `on_data_writer_discovery()` method receives the `PublicationBuiltinTopicData` containing remote endpoint information including type details.

**2) Get TypeObject from TypeInformation:**
```cpp
xtypes::TypeObject remote_type_object;
DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->type_object_registry()
  .get_type_object(
    info.type_information.type_information.complete()
      .typeid_with_size().type_id(),
    remote_type_object);
```

**3) Build DynamicType from TypeObject:**
```cpp
DynamicType::_ref_type remote_type = 
  DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::get_instance()
    ->create_type_w_type_object(remote_type_object)
    ->build();
```

**4) Register type and create DataReader:**
```cpp
TypeSupport dyn_type_support(new DynamicPubSubType(remote_type));
dyn_type_support.register_type(participant);
Topic* topic = participant->create_topic(
  info.topic_name.to_string(), 
  dyn_type_support.get_type_name(),
  TOPIC_QOS_DEFAULT);
Subscriber* subscriber = participant->create_subscriber(SUBSCRIBER_QOS_DEFAULT);
DataReader* data_reader = subscriber->create_datareader(
  topic, DATAREADER_QOS_DEFAULT);
```

**5) Read/take data:**
The documentation focuses on setup; actual read/take operations use standard DataReader APIs not shown in this excerpt.

## Key Findings:

1. **FastDDS 3.x Conan Availability**: eProsima has never pushed Fast DDS releases to ConanCenter, though this is not managed by eProsima, and all contributions for recipes have been from external contributors.

2. **Conanfile Structure**: The conanfile.txt file is a simplified version of conanfile.py, aimed at simple consumption of dependencies, but it cannot be used to create a package.

3. **CMake Integration**: Projects using FastDDS with Conan typically use CMake 3.15+ with Conan 2.0+ and the conan toolchain for configuration.

4. **Project Examples**: There are community examples available that demonstrate how to use Fast DDS with Conan 2, such as the fastdds-qml-conan project that uses conanfile.py recipes for managing Fast DDS dependencies.

## Notes on Dependencies:
For a typical FastDDS 3.x conanfile.txt setup, you would normally need to declare requirements for fastdds and fastcdr (CDR serialization), along with appropriate CMake generators like CMakeDeps to enable `find_package()` calls in your CMakeLists.txt files.

Based on the GitHub discussion, here's what we know:

**Current Status:**
FastDDS 3.x.x is **not yet available** on Conan Center Index. A user requested it in October 2024, asking when version 3.x would be published.

**Key Points:**

1. **Management:** eProsima maintainers clarified that "This is not something we manage at eProsima." Recipe contributions come from external contributors.

2. **Recipe Location:** FastDDS recipes are hosted at the [Conan Center Index repository](https://github.com/conan-io/conan-center-index/tree/master/recipes/fast-dds).

3. **No Official Support:** eProsima has never pushed FastDDS releases to ConanCenter themselves; they rely on community contributions.

**Package Names & Requirements:**
The discussion does not specify exact package names, Conan requirements for `fast-dds` or `fastcdr`, or details about a working recipe for version 3.4.0.

**Recommendation:**
If you need FastDDS 3.x on Conan, you could contact the maintainers directly (raul@eprosima.com) to discuss your needs, or contribute a recipe to Conan Center Index yourself.

## Key Findings

**SerializedPayload Structure:**
The SerializedPayload_t structure contains the actual length of the data and a pointer to the data, with encapsulation as suggested in the RTPS specification.

**DynamicData Serialization:**
DynamicData serialization uses the DynamicPubSubType class with the serialize method, which takes a DynamicData object and populates a SerializedPayload_t structure with the CDR-encoded data.

**take_next_sample Method:**
The take_next_sample method is used with a DataReader to retrieve received data and populate both data and SampleInfo parameters.

**CDR Serialization:**
Data are serialized and encoded using Common Data Representation (CDR), which is a low-level representation for inter-agent transfer, mapping from OMG IDL data types to byte streams.

The search results show that eProsima FastDDS uses Fast CDR for serialization, and the SerializedPayload_t structure contains raw CDR bytes. However, the specific method `get_cdr_serialized_payload` doesn't appear in these documentation pages. You may want to check the FastDDS API reference directly or look at the source code repository for more detailed information about accessing raw CDR bytes from DynamicData objects.

# Fast-DDS Versions Available on Conan Center

Based on the package registry data, several versions of fast-dds are available:

## Latest Versions
- **3.4.0** (current, timestamp: 2025-11-18)
- **3.2.1** (timestamp: 2025-11-18)
- **2.14.3** (timestamp: 2025-11-18)
- **2.14.0** (unmaintained, 2024-04-30)
- **2.13.3** (unmaintained, 2024-04-30)
- **2.11.2** through **2.3.2** (older unmaintained versions)

## Dependencies for v3.4.0

The latest version declares these required packages:
- **asio/1.29.0**
- **fast-cdr/2.3.Z** (version 2.3.x series)
- **foonathan-memory/0.7.Z**
- **tinyxml2/10.0.Z**

## Platform Support (v3.4.0)

The package provides pre-built binaries for:
- Linux (x86_64, GCC 11, C++17)
- Windows (x86_64, MSVC 194, C++14)
- macOS (both x86_64 and ARM64, Apple Clang 17, C++gnu17)

Both static and shared library configurations are available across supported platforms.
