1вҶ’# Architecture
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Overview
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’pj_bridge is a multi-backend bridge server that forwards middleware topic data over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients. A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) is shared by two backend-specific adapters:
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’- **ROS2 backend** (`ros2/`) вҖ” uses `rclcpp`, schema from `.msg` files
     8вҶ’- **RTI backend** (`rti/`) вҖ” uses RTI Connext DDS, schema from OMG IDL
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’```
    11вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    12вҶ’в”Ӯ                  app/ (core)                      в”Ӯ
    13вҶ’в”Ӯ  BridgeServer вҶҗ TopicSourceInterface             в”Ӯ
    14вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ SubscriptionManagerInterface     в”Ӯ
    15вҶ’в”Ӯ               вҶҗ MiddlewareInterface              в”Ӯ
    16вҶ’в”Ӯ  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     в”Ӯ
    17вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    18вҶ’         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    19вҶ’    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    20вҶ’    в”Ӯ  ros2/  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ   rti/    в”Ӯ
    21вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2TopicSource      в”Ӯ RtiTopicSource
    22вҶ’    в”Ӯ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  в”Ӯ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    23вҶ’    в”Ӯ (rclcpp)             в”Ӯ (RTI Connext)
    24вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    25вҶ’```
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’## Communication Pattern
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’Single WebSocket port (default 8080), two frame types:
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’- **Text frames**: JSON API requests and responses (`get_topics`, `subscribe`, `unsubscribe`, `heartbeat`, `pause`, `resume`)
    32вҶ’- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream, sent per-client based on their subscriptions
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## Abstract Interfaces
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Three interfaces decouple the core from any specific middleware:
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’### TopicSourceInterface
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’Discovers available topics and retrieves their schemas. Implementations:
    41вҶ’- `Ros2TopicSource`: wraps `TopicDiscovery` (rclcpp enumeration) + `SchemaExtractor` (.msg file parsing). Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
    42вҶ’- `RtiTopicSource`: wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery` (DDS participant discovery). Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’### SubscriptionManagerInterface
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’Manages ref-counted middleware subscriptions. A single global `MessageCallback` delivers all incoming messages as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` to the `MessageBuffer`. Implementations:
    47вҶ’- `Ros2SubscriptionManager`: wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` to `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy. Optionally strips large message fields (Image, PointCloud2) via `MessageStripper`.
    48вҶ’- `RtiSubscriptionManager`: wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, no conversion needed.
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### MiddlewareInterface
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’Abstract transport layer for client connections. Separates text (request/reply) and binary (per-client push) channels. Single implementation: `WebSocketMiddleware` (IXWebSocket).
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’## Core Components
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’### BridgeServer
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’Main orchestrator. Takes all three interfaces via constructor injection (dependency injection). Does **not** own timers вҖ” the entry point drives the event loop by calling three public methods:
    59вҶ’- `process_requests()` вҖ” polls and handles one JSON API request
    60вҶ’- `publish_aggregated_messages()` вҖ” drains the message buffer, serializes per subscription group, compresses with ZSTD, sends per-client binary frames
    61вҶ’- `check_session_timeouts()` вҖ” evicts sessions with expired heartbeats
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’Key behaviors:
    64вҶ’- **Schema-before-subscribe**: `get_schema()` is called before `subscribe()` to avoid corrupting ref counts if schema extraction fails
    65вҶ’- **Additive subscriptions**: `handle_subscribe()` only adds topics, never removes; rate limits can be updated for already-subscribed topics
    66вҶ’- **Ref-counted pause/resume**: `handle_pause()` decrements subscription ref counts; `handle_resume()` increments them; `cleanup_session()` skips unsubscribe for paused clients
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### MessageBuffer
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’Thread-safe per-topic message buffer. Messages are stored as `BufferedMessage` containing:
    71вҶ’- `timestamp_ns` вҖ” source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    72вҶ’- `received_at_ns` вҖ” wall-clock time at insertion (used for TTL cleanup)
    73вҶ’- `data` вҖ” `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, CDR-encoded payload
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’Stale messages (older than 1 second by `received_at_ns`) are removed on every `add_message()` call. `move_messages()` atomically drains the buffer into the caller's map.
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’### SessionManager
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’Tracks client sessions by WebSocket connection identity:
    80вҶ’- Heartbeat monitoring (default 10-second timeout)
    81вҶ’- Per-client subscription tracking with per-topic rate limits
    82вҶ’- Pause/resume state
    83вҶ’- Thread-safe for concurrent access
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’### AggregatedMessageSerializer
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’Streaming binary serializer, no middleware dependencies. Per-message wire format:
    88вҶ’- Topic name length (uint16_t LE) + topic name (UTF-8)
    89вҶ’- Timestamp (uint64_t ns, LE)
    90вҶ’- Message data length (uint32_t LE) + message data (CDR)
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’`finalize()` produces a 16-byte header (`PJRB` magic, message count, uncompressed size, flags) followed by a ZSTD-compressed payload. See [API docs](API.md#binary-message-format) for full format.
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’### WebSocketMiddleware
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’IXWebSocket-based implementation of `MiddlewareInterface`:
    97вҶ’- Connection-oriented client identity via `connectionState->getId()`
    98вҶ’- Incoming text requests queued and dequeued via `receive_request()`
    99вҶ’- Per-client binary send for aggregated message frames
   100вҶ’- Connect/disconnect callbacks for automatic session lifecycle
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’## Event Loop
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’BridgeServer exposes methods; the entry point drives timing:
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’**ROS2** (`ros2/src/main.cpp`): Three `rclcpp` wall timers:
   107вҶ’- 10 ms вҶ’ `process_requests()`
   108вҶ’- 1/publish_rate вҶ’ `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   109вҶ’- 1 s вҶ’ `check_session_timeouts()`
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’Spun via `SingleThreadedExecutor::spin_some(100ms)`.
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’**RTI** (`rti/src/main.cpp`): `std::chrono` loop with 1 ms sleep:
   114вҶ’- Every iteration вҶ’ `process_requests()`
   115вҶ’- At publish_rate вҶ’ `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   116вҶ’- Every 1 s вҶ’ `check_session_timeouts()`
   117вҶ’- Every 5 s (optional) вҶ’ stats snapshot
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’## ROS2-Specific Components
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’- **TopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via `rclcpp::Node::get_topic_names_and_types()`, filtering system topics
   122вҶ’- **SchemaExtractor**: Reads `.msg` files from ROS2 package share directories via `ament_index_cpp`, recursively expanding nested types (matches rosbag2 MCAP format)
   123вҶ’- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Ref-counted `rclcpp::GenericSubscription` per topic
   124вҶ’- **MessageStripper**: Strips data fields from large message types (Image, PointCloud2, etc.)
   125вҶ’
   126вҶ’## RTI-Specific Components
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’- **DdsTopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via DDS participant discovery across configured domain IDs
   129вҶ’- **DdsSubscriptionManager**: Manages DDS DataReaders, natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’## Design Decisions
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’### Backend-Agnostic Core via Interfaces
   134вҶ’`TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS without compile-time dependencies on either middleware.
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’### Message Data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   137вҶ’Backend-agnostic type eliminates `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` from the core. ROS2 adapter converts via memcpy; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’### Externalized Event Loop
   140вҶ’BridgeServer has no internal timers. This avoids coupling to any specific executor model (rclcpp timers vs. `std::chrono` loop) and keeps the core library free of middleware dependencies.
   141вҶ’
   142вҶ’### Shared Subscriptions with Reference Counting
   143вҶ’One underlying middleware subscription per topic, shared across all clients. Ref count incremented on subscribe/resume, decremented on unsubscribe/pause/disconnect.
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’### TTL Cleanup by Wall-Clock Time
   146вҶ’MessageBuffer cleanup uses `received_at_ns` (wall clock), not `timestamp_ns` (source clock). This prevents sim-time offsets from causing premature eviction or unbounded growth.
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’### Per-Client Message Filtering
   149вҶ’Each client receives only messages for its subscribed topics, with per-topic rate limiting. The server serializes, compresses, and sends individually per client.
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’## Thread Safety
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’### Lock Ordering (to prevent deadlock)
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’```
   156вҶ’cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’`SessionManager::mutex_` may be acquired while holding any of these. Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’### Per-Component Guarantees
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’- **MessageBuffer**: Internal mutex protects all buffer operations
   164вҶ’- **SessionManager**: Internal mutex protects session map
   165вҶ’- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Internal mutex protects subscription map
   166вҶ’- **BridgeServer**: `cleanup_mutex_` prevents concurrent cleanup; `last_sent_mutex_` protects rate-limiting state; `stats_mutex_` protects counters. Frames are built under `last_sent_mutex_`, sent outside it (minimizes lock contention)
   167вҶ’- **WebSocketMiddleware**: Separate mutexes for state, client map, and message queue
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’## Shutdown Sequence
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’Both entry points follow the same ordered shutdown to avoid use-after-free:
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’1. Cancel timers / exit event loop
   174вҶ’2. Clear the subscription manager's message callback (`set_message_callback(nullptr)`)
   175вҶ’3. Unsubscribe all middleware subscriptions
   176вҶ’4. Shutdown WebSocket server (BridgeServer destructor handles this if not done explicitly)
   177вҶ’
   178вҶ’## Performance Characteristics
   179вҶ’
   180вҶ’- **Compression**: ZSTD level 1 (typically 50-70% size reduction)
   181вҶ’- **Memory**: Automatic 1-second TTL prevents unbounded growth
   182вҶ’- **Concurrent Clients**: 10+ clients supported
   183вҶ’

    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames

    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};

Design an implementation plan for adding a FastDDS backend to pj_bridge. Here's all the context:

## Project Structure
- `app/` вҖ” backend-agnostic core library (BridgeServer, MessageBuffer, SessionManager, WebSocketMiddleware)
- `ros2/` вҖ” ROS2 adapter (rclcpp-based)
- `rti/` вҖ” RTI Connext DDS adapter (to be disabled but not deleted)
- New: `fastdds/` вҖ” eProsima Fast DDS adapter

## Abstract Interfaces to Implement

**TopicSourceInterface** (app/include/pj_bridge/topic_source_interface.hpp):
```cpp
virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
```

**SubscriptionManagerInterface** (app/include/pj_bridge/subscription_manager_interface.hpp):
```cpp
using MessageCallback = std::function<void(const std::string&, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>>, uint64_t)>;
virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
```

## RTI Backend Structure (template to follow)

**Lower-level DDS classes:**
1. `DdsTopicDiscovery` вҖ” creates DomainParticipant per domain, listens to PublicationBuiltinTopicData, caches topic name + type_name + StructType + schema_idl. Uses `dds::sub::NoOpDataReaderListener<PublicationBuiltinTopicData>`. Key RTI-specific: `sample.data().extensions().type()` for type object, `rti::core::xtypes::to_string(type, idl_format)` for IDL generation.

2. `DdsSubscriptionManager` вҖ” reference-counted subscriptions. Creates DataReader<DynamicData> per topic. Uses `DynamicDataTypeSerializationProperty::skip_deserialization(true)` for performance. Extracts CDR via `sample.data().get_cdr_buffer()`. Extracts timestamp via `sample.info().source_timestamp()`.

**Thin adapter classes:**
3. `RtiTopicSource` вҖ” wraps DdsTopicDiscovery, implements TopicSourceInterface, returns schema_encoding = "omgidl"
4. `RtiSubscriptionManager` вҖ” wraps DdsSubscriptionManager, implements SubscriptionManagerInterface (1:1 passthrough)

**Entry point:**
5. `main.cpp` вҖ” CLI11 parsing (--domains, --port, --publish-rate, --session-timeout, --qos-profile, --log-level, --stats), standalone event loop with std::chrono + signal handlers

## FastDDS 3.4.0 API Equivalents

**Topic Discovery:**
- FastDDS uses `DomainParticipantListener::on_type_discovery()` callback to get DynamicType from remote publishers
- OR TypeLookup Service via `register_remote_type()` for complex types
- TypeObject propagation must be enabled

**IDL Serialization:**
```cpp
// FastDDS equivalent of RTI's to_string():
ReturnCode_t idl_serialize(const DynamicType::_ref_type& dynamic_type, std::ostream& output);
```

**CDR Buffer:**
- FastDDS: `SerializedPayload_t` from DataReader, raw bytes accessible via `.data` and `.length`
- Need to find equivalent of `skip_deserialization` вҖ” FastDDS may need different approach for raw CDR access

**Key Classes in FastDDS 3.4.0:**
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DomainParticipant`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DomainParticipantListener`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DataReader`, `DataReaderListener`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DynamicType`, `DynamicData`
- `eprosima::fastdds::dds::DynamicTypeBuilderFactory`
- `idl_serialize()` for type-to-IDL conversion

## Build System

**Current CMakeLists.txt pattern:**
- `ENABLE_RTI` option + `find_package(RTIConnextDDS)` for RTI backend
- Need: `ENABLE_FASTDDS` option + `find_package(fastcdr REQUIRED)` + `find_package(fastrtps REQUIRED)` for FastDDS
- FastDDS to be acquired via Conan (fast-dds/3.4.0) вҖ” need conanfile.txt
- CLI11 already FetchContent'd for RTI, can reuse

**Targets to create:**
- `pj_bridge_fastdds_lib` (STATIC) вҖ” FastDDS adapter library
- `pj_bridge_fastdds` (EXECUTABLE) вҖ” Entry point

**RTI to be disabled:** Remove or comment out the `if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)` block, or just don't set ENABLE_RTI.

## Design Decisions Needed

1. **File naming**: Should lower-level classes keep "Dds" prefix (like RTI's DdsTopicDiscovery/DdsSubscriptionManager) or use "FastDds" prefix? I'd suggest "FastDds" to distinguish: `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`. Adapter classes: `FastDdsTopicSource`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`.

   Wait, that creates a name collision. Better: lower-level = `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` (the raw DDS implementation); adapters = `FastDdsTopicSource` (TopicSourceInterface), `FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter` (SubscriptionManagerInterface).

   Actually, looking at RTI pattern more carefully:
   - Lower: `DdsTopicDiscovery`, `DdsSubscriptionManager`
   - Adapters: `RtiTopicSource`, `RtiSubscriptionManager`
   
   For FastDDS, we could use the same pattern since the lower-level classes are DDS-generic in concept:
   - Lower: `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`
   - Adapters: `FastDdsTopicSource`, `FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter`
   
   Or simpler: just have the adapter classes directly without a separate lower layer, since both levels are FastDDS-specific anyway.

2. **Conan integration**: Need conanfile.txt in project root. Other workspace projects use CMakeDeps/CMakeToolchain generators.

3. **Multiple domains**: FastDDS supports multiple DomainParticipants вҖ” should mirror RTI's --domains flag.

4. **Raw CDR access without deserialization**: In FastDDS, this is typically done via `DataReader::take()` with raw sample access or by using `DynamicData` and extracting serialized payload. Need to verify the exact API for getting raw CDR from DynamicData in FastDDS 3.4.

Please design the implementation plan with:
- Directory structure
- File list with brief description of each
- API mapping (RTI вҶ’ FastDDS) for each critical operation
- Build system changes (CMakeLists.txt + conanfile.txt)
- Implementation order (what to implement first for fastest iteration)
- Testing strategy

  FetchContent_Declare(
    cli11
    URL https://github.com/CLIUtils/CLI11/archive/refs/tags/v2.4.2.tar.gz
  )
  set(CLI11_PRECOMPILED OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cli11)

  add_library(pj_bridge_rti_lib STATIC
    rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp
    rti/src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp
    rti/src/rti_topic_source.cpp
    rti/src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp
  )

  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/rti/include
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
    RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api
  )

  add_executable(pj_bridge_rti
    rti/src/main.cpp
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti
    pj_bridge_rti_lib
    CLI11::CLI11
  )

  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_rti
    DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME}
  )
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Install common
# ============================================================================
install(
  DIRECTORY app/include/
  DESTINATION include
)

install(
  TARGETS pj_bridge_app
  EXPORT ${PROJECT_NAME}_appTargets
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION lib
  LIBRARY DESTINATION lib

file.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(pj_bridge)

# Compiler settings
if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_TSAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

# Custom cmake modules
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)

# ============================================================================
# Backend detection
# ============================================================================
find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
# RTI Connext DDS вҖ” only look for it if explicitly requested or available
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found вҖ” will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found вҖ” skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Common dependencies (for app layer)
# ============================================================================

content.text: Now I have a few questions before finalizing.

questions.question: For the FastDDS backend, should we keep the two-level architecture (lower-level DDS classes + thin adapter) like RTI, or flatten into fewer classes since there's no reuse scenario?

options.label: Two-level (like RTI)
options.description: FastDdsTopicDiscovery + FastDdsTopicSource adapter, FastDdsSubscriptionManager + FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter. 4 classes + main.cpp = 9 files.

options.label: Flattened
options.description: FastDdsTopicSource directly implements TopicSourceInterface + discovery logic. FastDdsSubscriptionManager directly implements SubscriptionManagerInterface. 2 classes + main.cpp = 5 files.

questions.question: For raw CDR extraction: FastDDS doesn't have RTI's get_cdr_buffer(). We can deserialize into DynamicData then re-serialize to CDR (simple but extra copy), or intercept the raw payload at the RTPS layer (more complex but zero-copy). Which approach?

options.label: Deserialize + re-serialize (Recommended)
options.description: Simpler, correct, uses standard FastDDS API. Extra memcpy is negligible at 50Hz aggregation rate. Can optimize later if needed.

options.label: RTPS raw payload intercept
options.description: Override DynamicPubSubType::deserialize() to capture SerializedPayload_t before deserialization. More complex, but avoids the round-trip.

content.text: What would you like to clarify about these questions?

message.content: what do you suggest? what is the advantage of the two levels, in terms of maintenability and testability?

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to reason about the trade-offs. Let me think carefully about this.

**Two-level architecture (like RTI):**
- Lower level: `FastDdsTopicDiscovery`, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` вҖ” raw DDS logic
- Upper level: `FastDdsTopicSource`, `FastDdsSubscriptionAdapter` вҖ” thin adapters to interface

**Flattened:**
- `FastDdsTopicSource` directly implements `TopicSourceInterface` + contains all discovery logic
- `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` directly implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface` + contains all subscription logic

Let me think about what the two levels buy you in the RTI case:


The adapters themselves are minimal вҖ” just thin wrappers that map DDS-specific types like `dds::core::xtypes::StructType` and `DomainParticipant` to the core's abstractions. The real value is that the lower-level classes can expose these DDS-specific types internally while the adapters shield the rest of the system from that complexity.

But looking at testability and maintainability, the separation doesn't actually buy much. The adapter layer is so thin it barely needs testing, the lower-level classes are inherently coupled to DDS and can't be unit-tested in isolation anyway, and there's no way to mock `FastDdsTopicDiscovery` since it's not behind an interface. The core is already well-tested through its mock interfaces, so the backend implementation details don't matter there. On the maintainability side, I'm just adding more files and indirection without any real logic вҖ” it's pure type conversion with no actual benefit.

My instinct is to flatten this. The two-level design made sense as a speculative architecture, but in reality there's no scenario where `FastDdsTopicDiscovery` would be reused independently, the internal dependencies mean the lower level isn't self-contained anyway, and fewer files would make the codebase easier to navigate. I can keep `FastDdsTopicSource` exposing the methods that `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` needs directly without wrapping them in an interface. Actually wait вҖ” let me reconsider. The `SubscriptionManagerInterface` doesn't have a `get_type()` or `get_participant()` method. The subscription manager needs access to the discovery's type info to create readers. In the flattened approach, `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` takes a reference to `FastDdsTopicSource` and calls its non-interface methods like `get_dynamic_type()` and `get_participant()`. This is exactly what RTI does at the lower level, so flattening works cleanly вҖ” 5 files instead of 9, same functionality, less indirection.

For the CDR deserialization question, deserialize and re-serialize is the right approach to start with. At 50Hz aggregation, we're talking about microseconds of overhead per message, which is completely negligible.

content.text:
**My recommendation: Flatten.** Here's the reasoning:

**What the two levels give you in RTI:**
- Lower level (`DdsTopicDiscovery`, `DdsSubscriptionManager`) exposes RTI-specific types (`StructType`, `DomainParticipant`)
- Upper level (adapters) converts `DdsTopicInfo` вҶ’ `TopicInfo` and hides DDS types from the core

**Why it doesn't help in practice:**
- The adapters are ~20 lines of pure delegation, zero logic вҖ” not worth testing independently
- The lower-level classes can't be unit-tested without real DDS anyway (no mocking possible)
- The core `BridgeServer` is already fully tested via mock interfaces вҖ” it doesn't know or care about the backend
- The subscription manager always depends on the discovery class (needs `get_type()`, `get_participant()`), so they're never independently reusable
- More files = more indirection to navigate when debugging

**Flattened approach:**
- `FastDdsTopicSource` implements `TopicSourceInterface` directly, plus exposes `get_dynamic_type()` / `get_participant()` as non-interface methods
- `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface` directly, takes a reference to `FastDdsTopicSource` for type/participant access
- 5 files (2 headers, 2 sources, main.cpp) instead of 9

The testability story is unchanged вҖ” the real tests are at the `BridgeServer` level with mocks, and at the integration level with real DDS.

For the **CDR question**, I'd go with deserialize + re-serialize. At 50Hz aggregation the extra memcpy is nanoseconds. We can always optimize later if profiling shows it matters.

options.label: Keep two levels (like RTI)

input.content:
# Implement FastDDS Backend for pj_bridge

## Context

The project has two DDS backends: ROS2 (`ros2/`) and RTI Connext (`rti/`). We want to add a third backend using eProsima Fast DDS 3.4.0 (acquired via Conan), and stop compiling the RTI backend (without deleting it). FastDDS produces OMG IDL schemas and CDR-encoded messages, same as RTI вҖ” so the core `app/` layer and PlotJuggler client need no changes.

## Architecture: Flattened (2 classes + main)

Unlike RTI's 4-class two-level design, we flatten into 2 classes that directly implement the abstract interfaces:

- **`FastDdsTopicSource`** вҖ” implements `TopicSourceInterface`, manages `DomainParticipant`s, discovers topics via `on_data_writer_discovery()`, resolves `DynamicType` from `TypeObjectRegistry`, generates IDL via `idl_serialize()`
- **`FastDdsSubscriptionManager`** вҖ” implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface`, creates `DataReader`s with `DynamicPubSubType`, ref-counted subscriptions, extracts CDR via deserialize+re-serialize
- **`main.cpp`** вҖ” CLI11 parsing + `std::chrono` event loop (same pattern as RTI)

## Files to Create

```
fastdds/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ include/pj_bridge_fastdds/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_topic_source.hpp
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ fastdds_subscription_manager.hpp
в””в”Җв”Җ src/
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_topic_source.cpp
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_subscription_manager.cpp
    в””в”Җв”Җ main.cpp
```

Plus `conanfile.txt` at project root.

## Files to Modify

- **`CMakeLists.txt`** вҖ” add `ENABLE_FASTDDS` block, disable RTI block
- **`CLAUDE.md`** вҖ” add FastDDS backend to docs
- **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`** вҖ” add FastDDS to diagram

## Step 1: Build System

### `conanfile.txt` (new, project root)

```ini
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

### `CMakeLists.txt` changes

1. **Disable RTI**: Comment out the `if(ENABLE_RTI ...)` block (lines 168-205) with a note "RTI backend disabled вҖ” to be removed once FastDDS backend is stable"

2. **Add after the disabled RTI block** (before "Install common"):

```cmake
# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_fastdds вҖ” eProsima FastDDS backend
# ============================================================================
option(ENABLE_FASTDDS "Build eProsima FastDDS backend" OFF)

if(ENABLE_FASTDDS)
  find_package(fastcdr REQUIRED)
  find_package(fastdds REQUIRED)

  if(NOT TARGET CLI11::CLI11)
    FetchContent_Declare(cli11
      URL https://github.com/CLIUtils/CLI11/archive/refs/tags/v2.4.2.tar.gz)
    set(CLI11_PRECOMPILED OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cli11)
  endif()

  add_library(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib STATIC
    fastdds/src/fastdds_topic_source.cpp
    fastdds/src/fastdds_subscription_manager.cpp
  )
  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/fastdds/include
  )
  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
    fastdds
    fastcdr
  )

  add_executable(pj_bridge_fastdds fastdds/src/main.cpp)
  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds pj_bridge_fastdds_lib CLI11::CLI11)
  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_fastdds DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME})
endif()
```

> **Note**: Exact CMake target names (`fastdds`, `fastcdr`) need verification from Conan-generated files. May need `fastdds::fastdds` / `fastcdr::fastcdr`.

### Build commands

```bash
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge
conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing -s build_type=Release
cd build_fastdds
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON \
         -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake
make -j$(nproc)
```

## Step 2: FastDdsTopicSource

Implements `TopicSourceInterface`. Template: `rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp` + `rti/src/rti_topic_source.cpp` merged.

### Constructor: `FastDdsTopicSource(const std::vector<int32_t>& domain_ids)`

For each domain:
1. Create `DomainParticipant` via `DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->create_participant(domain_id, qos)` with TypeLookup enabled:
   ```cpp
   DomainParticipantQos pqos;
   pqos.wire_protocol().builtin.typelookup_config.use_client = true;
   pqos.wire_protocol().builtin.typelookup_config.use_server = true;
   ```
2. Attach a `ParticipantListener` (inner class) that overrides `on_data_writer_discovery()`

### ParticipantListener::on_data_writer_discovery()

When a new DataWriter is discovered (`reason == DISCOVERED_WRITER`):
1. Get topic name from `info.info.topicName()`
2. Get `TypeObject` from `ITypeObjectRegistry` using the type identifier from `info.info.type_information`
3. Build `DynamicType` via `DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::get_instance()->create_type_w_type_object(type_object)->build()`
4. Check `dynamic_type->get_kind() == TK_STRUCTURE`
5. Generate IDL: `idl_serialize(dynamic_type, oss)`
6. Cache in `topics_` map (deduplicated)

### Interface methods

- `get_topics()` вҶ’ snapshot `topics_` under read lock, map to `TopicInfo`
- `get_schema(name)` вҶ’ return cached IDL string
- `schema_encoding()` вҶ’ return `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl`

### Non-interface methods (used by subscription manager)

- `get_dynamic_type(name)` вҶ’ return `DynamicType::_ref_type`
- `get_participant(domain_id)` вҶ’ return `DomainParticipant*`
- `get_domain_id(name)` вҶ’ return `optional<int32_t>`

### RTI вҶ’ FastDDS API mapping (discovery)

| RTI | FastDDS 3.4 |
|-----|-------------|
| `DomainParticipant(domain_id)` | `DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->create_participant(domain_id, qos)` |
| `NoOpDataReaderListener<PublicationBuiltinTopicData>` | `DomainParticipantListener::on_data_writer_discovery()` |
| `sample.data().extensions().type()` | `TypeObjectRegistry::get_type_object()` + `DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::create_type_w_type_object()` |
| `rti::core::xtypes::to_string(type, idl)` | `idl_serialize(dynamic_type, ostream)` |
| `participant.close()` | `DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->delete_participant(p)` |

### Thread safety

`std::shared_mutex` вҖ” write lock in `on_data_writer_discovery()`, read lock in getters.

## Step 3: FastDdsSubscriptionManager

Implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface`. Template: `rti/src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp` + `rti/src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp` merged.

### Constructor: `FastDdsSubscriptionManager(FastDdsTopicSource& topic_source)`

### subscribe(topic_name, topic_type)

1. If already subscribed вҶ’ increment ref count, return true
2. Get `DynamicType` from `topic_source_.get_dynamic_type(topic_name)`
3. Get `DomainParticipant*` from `topic_source_.get_participant(domain_id)`
4. Register type: `TypeSupport ts(new DynamicPubSubType(dynamic_type)); ts.register_type(participant)`
5. Create Topic: `participant->create_topic(topic_name, type_name, TOPIC_QOS_DEFAULT)`
6. Create Subscriber: `participant->create_subscriber(SUBSCRIBER_QOS_DEFAULT)`
7. Create DataReader: `subscriber->create_datareader(topic, DATAREADER_QOS_DEFAULT, listener)`
8. Store in `subscriptions_` with ref count = 1

### InternalReaderListener::on_data_available(DataReader* reader)

CDR extraction via deserialize + re-serialize:
```cpp
DynamicData::_ref_type data = DynamicDataFactory::get_instance()->create_data(dynamic_type_);
SampleInfo info;
while (reader->take_next_sample(&data, &info) == RETCODE_OK) {
  if (!info.valid_data) continue;

  // Re-serialize to get CDR bytes
  DynamicPubSubType pub_sub_type(dynamic_type_);
  SerializedPayload_t payload;
  pub_sub_type.serialize(&data, &payload);

  auto cdr = std::make_shared<std::vector<std::byte>>(payload.length);
  std::memcpy(cdr->data(), payload.data, payload.length);

  uint64_t ts_ns = (uint64_t(info.source_timestamp.seconds()) * 1'000'000'000ULL)
                 + info.source_timestamp.nanosec();
  callback_(topic_name_, std::move(cdr), ts_ns);
}
```

> Cache `DynamicPubSubType` per subscription to avoid repeated construction.

### unsubscribe(topic_name)

Decrement ref count. If zero: remove listener, delete DataReader, Subscriber, Topic outside lock.

### unsubscribe_all()

Swap out all subscriptions, close outside lock (same pattern as RTI).

### RTI вҶ’ FastDDS API mapping (subscription)

| RTI | FastDDS 3.4 |
|-----|-------------|
| `rti::domain::register_type(p, name, type, prop)` | `TypeSupport(new DynamicPubSubType(type)).register_type(p)` |
| `dds::sub::Subscriber(p)` | `p->create_subscriber(QOS_DEFAULT)` |
| `dds::topic::Topic<DynamicData>(p, name, type)` | `p->create_topic(name, type_name, QOS_DEFAULT)` |
| `dds::sub::DataReader<DynamicData>(sub, topic)` | `sub->create_datareader(topic, QOS_DEFAULT, listener)` |
| `sample.data().get_cdr_buffer()` | Deserialize + `DynamicPubSubType::serialize()` вҶ’ `SerializedPayload_t` |
| `sample.info().source_timestamp()` | `info.source_timestamp.seconds()` + `.nanosec()` |
| `reader.close()` | `subscriber->delete_datareader(reader)` |

## Step 4: main.cpp

Copy from `rti/src/main.cpp` and replace RTI types with FastDDS types:

- CLI11 flags: `--domains`, `--port`, `--publish-rate`, `--session-timeout`, `--log-level`, `--stats`
- No `--qos-profile` initially
- Create `FastDdsTopicSource` and `FastDdsSubscriptionManager`
- Create `WebSocketMiddleware` and `BridgeServer`
- Same `std::chrono` event loop with 1ms sleep
- Same signal handling (`SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`)
- Same ordered shutdown: clear callback вҶ’ unsubscribe_all вҶ’ middleware shutdown

## Step 5: Documentation

- **`CLAUDE.md`**: Add FastDDS backend section, build commands, update architecture diagram
- **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`**: Add FastDDS to the backend diagram

## Implementation Order

1. **Build skeleton**: `conanfile.txt`, directory structure, header stubs, CMakeLists.txt changes вҶ’ verify `conan install` + `cmake` + `make` compile
2. **Topic discovery**: Implement `FastDdsTopicSource` with participant creation + `on_data_writer_discovery()` + IDL generation
3. **Subscription manager**: Implement `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` with DataReader creation + CDR extraction
4. **Event loop**: Implement `main.cpp` with CLI parsing + BridgeServer wiring
5. **Integration test**: Run against a FastDDS publisher, connect PlotJuggler

## Known Risks

1. **Conan target names**: `find_package(fastdds)` may create `fastdds::fastdds` instead of `fastdds`. Verify from Conan-generated config files.
2. **TypeObject availability**: If `on_data_writer_discovery()` fires before TypeObject is in the registry, `get_type_object()` may fail. Fallback: use `register_remote_type()` with TypeLookup Service callback.
3. **CDR encoding**: FastDDS defaults to XCDR2 for extensible types. PlotJuggler's `rosx_introspection` needs to handle the 4-byte CDR encapsulation header correctly.
4. **`on_data_writer_discovery` API**: Exact callback signature and `info` struct fields need verification against FastDDS 3.4.0 headers at implementation time.

## Verification

```bash
# Build FastDDS backend
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge
conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing -s build_type=Release
cd build_fastdds
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON \
         -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake
make -j$(nproc)

# Regular tests still pass (core + ROS2, RTI disabled)
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose

# Integration: run FastDDS backend + connect a client
./build_fastdds/pj_bridge_fastdds --domains 0 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50
```

# Fix 7 Bugs Found in Code Review

## Context

Code review revealed 7 bugs of varying severity. Two high-severity ref-counting issues (#1, #2) share a root cause: the session's subscription list can be out of sync with the subscription manager's ref counts. The plan fixes all 7, enhances the mock to detect ref-count bugs, and fixes two existing tests that test the wrong thing.

## Step 0: Enhance MockSubscriptionManager with ref counting

The current mock uses `std::set` вҖ” it can't detect ref-count leaks or underflows. Replace with `std::unordered_map<std::string, int>` ref counting.

**File**: `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp` (lines 206-247)

```cpp
// Replace std::set<std::string> subscribed_topics_ with:
std::unordered_map<std::string, int> ref_counts_;
bool underflow_detected_{false};
```

- `subscribe()`: `ref_counts_[topic]++`
- `unsubscribe()`: decrement; if already 0, set `underflow_detected_ = true` and return false
- `unsubscribe_all()`: clear `ref_counts_`
- Add helpers: `int ref_count(topic)`, `bool has_underflow()`, `bool is_subscribed(topic)` (returns `ref_counts_[topic] > 0`)

Also enhance `MockTopicSource` вҖ” add topics to both `topics_` AND `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` via a test helper so subscribe actually succeeds. Currently most subscribe tests fail because topics aren't in `known_topics_`.

## Step 1: Fix two incorrect existing tests

**File**: `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`

### Fix `UnsubscribeRemovesTopics` (line 855)

Currently subscribes to `/topic_a` and `/topic_b` which don't exist in the mock, so subscribe fails. The unsubscribe then has nothing to remove. The test is functionally identical to `UnsubscribeIgnoresUnsubscribedTopics`.

**Fix**: Add real topics to the mock before subscribing:
```cpp
mock_topic_source_->set_topics({{"/topic_a", "std_msgs/msg/String"}, {"/topic_b", "std_msgs/msg/String"}});
mock_sub_manager_->add_known_topic("/topic_a");
mock_sub_manager_->add_known_topic("/topic_b");
```
Then verify the subscribe succeeds, and the unsubscribe of `/topic_a` actually removes it (the `removed` array should contain `/topic_a`). Verify `/topic_b` remains subscribed.

### Fix `SubscribeIsAdditive` (line 897)

Same problem вҖ” topics don't exist so subscribes fail. Cannot verify additivity.

**Fix**: Add topics to mock, verify that after subscribing to `/topic_a` then `/topic_b`, both appear in the session's subscriptions (verify via a get_topics or subscribe response that shows schemas for both).

## Step 2: Write failing tests for each bug

All tests go in `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp` unless noted.

### Bug #1 вҖ” Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe
Test `DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts`:
- Add topics `/t1`, `/t2`, `/t3` to mock
- Subscribe to all three
- Immediately simulate disconnect
- Verify `ref_count("/t1") == 0`, etc. (all zero)
- Verify `has_underflow() == false`

This test will **fail** with current code if disconnect races between subscribe and update_subscriptions. In the single-threaded test it may pass, but the structural fix (incremental session update) prevents the race.

### Bug #2 вҖ” Ref-count underflow on partial resume
Test `ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession`:
- Add `/topic_a`, `/topic_b` to mock, subscribe, then pause
- Remove `/topic_b` from `MockTopicSource::topics_` (simulate topic disappearance)
- Resume вҖ” `/topic_b` subscribe should fail
- Verify `/topic_b` is no longer in session subscriptions
- Disconnect вҖ” verify `has_underflow() == false`

This test will **fail** with current code (underflow on cleanup).

### Bug #4 вҖ” Unsubscribe ignores object format
Test `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat`:
- Add `/topic_a` to mock, subscribe successfully
- Unsubscribe with `[{"name": "/topic_a"}]`
- Verify `/topic_a` is in `removed` array
- Verify `ref_count("/topic_a") == 0`

This test will **fail** with current code (object entry silently skipped).

### Bug #6 вҖ” Unbounded queue
Test goes in `tests/unit/test_websocket_middleware.cpp`:
Test `IncomingQueueBounded`:
- After initialize, push more than `kMaxIncomingQueueSize` messages via the IXWebSocket callback
- Verify only `kMaxIncomingQueueSize` are dequeued via `receive_request()`

This test will **fail** with current code (all messages enqueued).

### Bug #9 вҖ” Division by zero
Test `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate`:
- Create BridgeServer with `publish_rate = 0.0`
- Call `initialize()` вҖ” should return false

This test should already **pass** (the validation exists). But add it for documentation and verify entry points don't compute `1.0/rate` before calling initialize.

### Bug #15 вҖ” Dead code
No test needed. Just remove the field.

## Step 3: Implement fixes

### Bug #15 (simplest) вҖ” Remove dead code
**Files**: `app/include/pj_bridge/message_buffer.hpp`, `app/src/message_buffer.cpp`
- Remove `uint64_t last_read_timestamp_ns_` from header
- Remove all assignments in constructor, `move_messages()`, `clear()`

### Bug #4 вҖ” Fix unsubscribe parsing
**File**: `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, `handle_unsubscribe()` (line 378-384)
- Add `else if (topic_item.is_object() && topic_item.contains("name") && topic_item["name"].is_string())` branch mirroring subscribe's parsing

### Bug #9 вҖ” Move interval computation after initialize()
**Files**: `ros2/src/main.cpp`, `rti/src/main.cpp`
- Move `publish_period`/`publish_interval` computation and timer creation to after `server.initialize()` returns true

### Bug #6 вҖ” Bound the incoming queue
**Files**: `app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, `app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/websocket_middleware.hpp`
- Add `static constexpr size_t kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024;`
- In message callback: check `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, if so log warning and return (drop message)

### Bug #7 вҖ” Release clients_mutex_ before sending
**File**: `app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`
- `send_reply()`: copy `shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket>` under lock, release lock, then `ws->send()`
- `send_binary()`: same pattern
- `publish_data()`: copy all `shared_ptr`s into a local vector under lock, release, then iterate and send

### Bug #1 вҖ” Incremental session update during subscribe
**Files**: `app/include/pj_bridge/session_manager.hpp`, `app/src/session_manager.cpp`, `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`

Add to `SessionManager`:
```cpp
bool add_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic, double rate);
bool remove_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic);
```

In `handle_subscribe()`: after each successful `subscription_manager_->subscribe()`, immediately call `session_manager_->add_subscription()`. If `add_subscription` returns false (session gone), roll back with `subscription_manager_->unsubscribe()`.

Remove the batch `update_subscriptions()` call at end. Keep a separate loop for rate-only updates on pre-existing topics.

### Bug #2 вҖ” Remove failed topics from session on resume
**File**: `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, `handle_resume()` (lines 483-497)

Collect topics that fail to re-subscribe into `failed_topics`. After the loop, remove them from the session via `session_manager_->remove_subscription()` (or batch `update_subscriptions()`).

## Step 4: Fix existing tests that break due to mock changes

After enhancing MockSubscriptionManager, review all tests that call `is_subscribed()` вҖ” the semantics change from set-based to ref-count-based but `is_subscribed()` should still return the same boolean result (`ref_count > 0`).

## Verification

```bash
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
# Regular build + test
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
# TSAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
# ASAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
```

input.content:
# Implement FastDDS Backend for pj_bridge

## Context

The project has two DDS backends: ROS2 (`ros2/`) and RTI Connext (`rti/`). We want to add a third backend using eProsima Fast DDS 3.4.0 (acquired via Conan), and stop compiling the RTI backend (without deleting it). FastDDS produces OMG IDL schemas and CDR-encoded messages, same as RTI вҖ” so the core `app/` layer and PlotJuggler client need no changes.

## Architecture: Flattened (2 classes + main)

Unlike RTI's 4-class two-level design, we flatten into 2 classes that directly implement the abstract interfaces:

- **`FastDdsTopicSource`** вҖ” implements `TopicSourceInterface`, manages `DomainParticipant`s, discovers topics via `on_data_writer_discovery()`, resolves `DynamicType` from `TypeObjectRegistry`, generates IDL via `idl_serialize()`
- **`FastDdsSubscriptionManager`** вҖ” implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface`, creates `DataReader`s with `DynamicPubSubType`, ref-counted subscriptions, extracts CDR via deserialize+re-serialize
- **`main.cpp`** вҖ” CLI11 parsing + `std::chrono` event loop (same pattern as RTI)

## Files to Create

```
fastdds/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ include/pj_bridge_fastdds/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_topic_source.hpp
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ fastdds_subscription_manager.hpp
в””в”Җв”Җ src/
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_topic_source.cpp
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ fastdds_subscription_manager.cpp
    в””в”Җв”Җ main.cpp
```

Plus `conanfile.txt` at project root.

## Files to Modify

- **`CMakeLists.txt`** вҖ” add `ENABLE_FASTDDS` block, disable RTI block
- **`CLAUDE.md`** вҖ” add FastDDS backend to docs
- **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`** вҖ” add FastDDS to diagram

## Step 1: Build System

### `conanfile.txt` (new, project root)

```ini
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

### `CMakeLists.txt` changes

1. **Disable RTI**: Comment out the `if(ENABLE_RTI ...)` block (lines 168-205) with a note

2. **Add after the disabled RTI block** (before "Install common"):

```cmake
option(ENABLE_FASTDDS "Build eProsima FastDDS backend" OFF)

if(ENABLE_FASTDDS)
  find_package(fastcdr REQUIRED)
  find_package(fastdds REQUIRED)

  if(NOT TARGET CLI11::CLI11)
    FetchContent_Declare(cli11
      URL https://github.com/CLIUtils/CLI11/archive/refs/tags/v2.4.2.tar.gz)
    set(CLI11_PRECOMPILED OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cli11)
  endif()

  add_library(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib STATIC
    fastdds/src/fastdds_topic_source.cpp
    fastdds/src/fastdds_subscription_manager.cpp
  )
  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/fastdds/include)
  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app fastdds fastcdr)

  add_executable(pj_bridge_fastdds fastdds/src/main.cpp)
  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds pj_bridge_fastdds_lib CLI11::CLI11)
  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_fastdds DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME})
endif()
```

> Exact link target names (`fastdds` vs `fastdds::fastdds`) need verification from Conan-generated files.

### Build commands

```bash
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge
conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing -s build_type=Release
cd build_fastdds
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON \
         -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake
make -j$(nproc)
```

## Step 2: FastDdsTopicSource

Implements `TopicSourceInterface`. Merges RTI's `DdsTopicDiscovery` + `RtiTopicSource` into one class.

**Reference files**: `rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp`, `rti/src/rti_topic_source.cpp`

### Constructor: `FastDdsTopicSource(const std::vector<int32_t>& domain_ids)`

For each domain:
1. Create `DomainParticipant` via `DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->create_participant(domain_id, qos)` with TypeLookup enabled:
   ```cpp
   DomainParticipantQos pqos;
   pqos.wire_protocol().builtin.typelookup_config.use_client = true;
   pqos.wire_protocol().builtin.typelookup_config.use_server = true;
   ```
2. Attach a `ParticipantListener` (inner class) overriding `on_data_writer_discovery()`

### ParticipantListener::on_data_writer_discovery()

When `reason == DISCOVERED_WRITER`:
1. Get topic name from discovery info
2. Get `TypeObject` from `ITypeObjectRegistry` using type identifier from `info`
3. Build `DynamicType`: `DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::get_instance()->create_type_w_type_object(type_object)->build()`
4. Check `dynamic_type->get_kind() == TK_STRUCTURE`
5. Generate IDL: `idl_serialize(dynamic_type, oss)`
6. Cache in `topics_` map (deduplicated by topic name)

### Interface methods

- `get_topics()` вҶ’ snapshot `topics_` under shared lock, map to `TopicInfo`
- `get_schema(name)` вҶ’ return cached IDL string
- `schema_encoding()` вҶ’ return `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl`

### Non-interface methods (used by subscription manager)

- `get_dynamic_type(name)` вҶ’ return `DynamicType::_ref_type`
- `get_participant(domain_id)` вҶ’ return `DomainParticipant*`
- `get_domain_id(name)` вҶ’ return `optional<int32_t>`

### RTI вҶ’ FastDDS API mapping (discovery)

| RTI | FastDDS 3.4 |
|-----|-------------|
| `DomainParticipant(domain_id)` | `Factory->create_participant(domain_id, qos)` |
| `NoOpDataReaderListener<PublicationBuiltinTopicData>` | `DomainParticipantListener::on_data_writer_discovery()` |
| `sample.data().extensions().type()` | `TypeObjectRegistry` + `DynamicTypeBuilderFactory::create_type_w_type_object()` |
| `rti::core::xtypes::to_string(type, idl)` | `idl_serialize(dynamic_type, ostream)` |
| `participant.close()` | `Factory->delete_participant(p)` |

### Thread safety: `std::shared_mutex` (write in discovery callback, read in getters)

## Step 3: FastDdsSubscriptionManager

Implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface`. Merges RTI's `DdsSubscriptionManager` + `RtiSubscriptionManager`.

**Reference files**: `rti/src/dds_subscription_manager.cpp`, `rti/src/rti_subscription_manager.cpp`

### Constructor: `FastDdsSubscriptionManager(FastDdsTopicSource& topic_source)`

### subscribe(topic_name, topic_type)

1. If already subscribed вҶ’ increment ref count, return true
2. Get `DynamicType` from `topic_source_.get_dynamic_type(topic_name)`
3. Get `DomainParticipant*` and domain_id from topic_source
4. Register type: `TypeSupport ts(new DynamicPubSubType(dynamic_type)); ts.register_type(participant)`
5. Create Topic, Subscriber, DataReader with listener
6. Store in `subscriptions_` with ref count = 1

### InternalReaderListener::on_data_available(DataReader* reader)

CDR extraction via deserialize + re-serialize (cache `DynamicPubSubType` per subscription):
```cpp
DynamicData::_ref_type data = DynamicDataFactory::get_instance()->create_data(dynamic_type_);
SampleInfo info;
while (reader->take_next_sample(&data, &info) == RETCODE_OK) {
  if (!info.valid_data) continue;
  SerializedPayload_t payload;
  pub_sub_type_.serialize(&data, &payload);
  auto cdr = std::make_shared<std::vector<std::byte>>(payload.length);
  std::memcpy(cdr->data(), payload.data, payload.length);
  uint64_t ts_ns = uint64_t(info.source_timestamp.seconds()) * 1'000'000'000ULL
                 + info.source_timestamp.nanosec();
  callback_(topic_name_, std::move(cdr), ts_ns);
}
```

### unsubscribe / unsubscribe_all

Same ref-count pattern as RTI. Close DDS entities outside lock to avoid deadlock.

## Step 4: main.cpp

Copy from `rti/src/main.cpp`, replace RTI types:

- CLI11 flags: `--domains`, `--port`, `--publish-rate`, `--session-timeout`, `--log-level`, `--stats`
- Create `FastDdsTopicSource(domain_ids)` and `FastDdsSubscriptionManager(topic_source)`
- Create shared `WebSocketMiddleware` and `BridgeServer`
- Same `std::chrono` event loop (1ms sleep, process_requests, publish at rate, check timeouts)
- Same signal handling (`SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`)
- Same ordered shutdown: clear callback вҶ’ unsubscribe_all вҶ’ middleware shutdown

## Step 5: Documentation

- **`CLAUDE.md`**: Add FastDDS backend section, Conan build commands, update architecture diagram and target table
- **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`**: Add FastDDS to backend diagram

## Implementation Order

1. Build skeleton: `conanfile.txt`, dirs, stubs, CMake вҶ’ verify compile
2. Topic discovery: `FastDdsTopicSource` with participants + discovery + IDL
3. Subscriptions: `FastDdsSubscriptionManager` with DataReaders + CDR
4. Event loop: `main.cpp` with CLI + BridgeServer wiring
5. Integration test against a real FastDDS publisher

## Known Risks

1. **Conan target names** вҖ” `fastdds` vs `fastdds::fastdds`, verify from generated configs
2. **TypeObject timing** вҖ” discovery callback may fire before TypeObject is in registry; may need retry or `register_remote_type()` fallback
3. **CDR encoding** вҖ” FastDDS defaults to XCDR2; PlotJuggler's deserializer must handle the encapsulation header
4. **`on_data_writer_discovery` signature** вҖ” exact `info` struct fields need verification against 3.4.0 headers

## Verification

```bash
# FastDDS standalone build
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge
conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing -s build_type=Release
cd build_fastdds
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON \
         -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake
make -j$(nproc)

# Core tests still pass (RTI disabled, ROS2 unaffected)
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose

# Integration test
./build_fastdds/pj_bridge_fastdds --domains 0 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50
```

lines: -# Fix 7 Bugs Found in Code Review

-Code review revealed 7 bugs of varying severity. Two high-severity ref-counting issues (#1, #2) share a root cause: the session's subscription list can be out of sync with the subscription manager's ref counts. The plan fixes all 7, enhances the mock to detect ref-count bugs, and fixes two existing tests that test the wrong thing.

+The project has two DDS backends: ROS2 (`ros2/`) and RTI Connext (`rti/`). We want to add a third backend using eProsima Fast DDS 3.4.0 (acquired via Conan), and stop compiling the RTI backend (without deleting it). FastDDS produces OMG IDL schemas and CDR-encoded messages, same as RTI вҖ” so the core `app/` layer and PlotJuggler client need no changes.
-## Step 0: Enhance MockSubscriptionManager with ref counting
-The current mock uses `std::set` вҖ” it can't detect ref-count leaks or underflows. Replace with `std::unordered_map<std::string, int>` ref counting.
+Unlike RTI's 4-class two-level design, we flatten into 2 classes that directly implement the abstract interfaces:

+- **`FastDdsTopicSource`** вҖ” implements `TopicSourceInterface`, manages `DomainParticipant`s, discovers topics via `on_data_writer_discovery()`, resolves `DynamicType` from `TypeObjectRegistry`, generates IDL via `idl_serialize()`
+- **`FastDdsSubscriptionManager`** вҖ” implements `SubscriptionManagerInterface`, creates `DataReader`s with `DynamicPubSubType`, ref-counted subscriptions, extracts CDR via deserialize+re-serialize
+- **`main.cpp`** вҖ” CLI11 parsing + `std::chrono` event loop (same pattern as RTI)
-- `unsubscribe()`: decrement; if already 0, set `underflow_detected_ = true` and return false

-Also enhance `MockTopicSource` вҖ” add topics to both `topics_` AND `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` via a test helper so subscribe actually succeeds. Currently most subscribe tests fail because topics aren't in `known_topics_`.
-## Step 1: Fix two incorrect existing tests
+- **`CMakeLists.txt`** вҖ” add `ENABLE_FASTDDS` block, disable RTI block
+- **`CLAUDE.md`** вҖ” add FastDDS backend to docs

-Currently subscribes to `/topic_a` and `/topic_b` which don't exist in the mock, so subscribe fails. The unsubscribe then has nothing to remove. The test is functionally identical to `UnsubscribeIgnoresUnsubscribedTopics`.
-**Fix**: Add real topics to the mock before subscribing:
-Then verify the subscribe succeeds, and the unsubscribe of `/topic_a` actually removes it (the `removed` array should contain `/topic_a`). Verify `/topic_b` remains subscribed.
-Same problem вҖ” topics don't exist so subscribes fail. Cannot verify additivity.
+1. **Disable RTI**: Comment out the `if(ENABLE_RTI ...)` block (lines 168-205) with a note

-**Fix**: Add topics to mock, verify that after subscribing to `/topic_a` then `/topic_b`, both appear in the session's subscriptions (verify via a get_topics or subscribe response that shows schemas for both).
+2. **Add after the disabled RTI block** (before "Install common"):
-## Step 2: Write failing tests for each bug
-### Bug #1 вҖ” Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe

-This test will **fail** with current code if disconnect races between subscribe and update_subscriptions. In the single-threaded test it may pass, but the structural fix (incremental session update) prevents the race.
-### Bug #2 вҖ” Ref-count underflow on partial resume
-- Add `/topic_a`, `/topic_b` to mock, subscribe, then pause
-- Verify `/topic_b` is no longer in session subscriptions
-This test will **fail** with current code (underflow on cleanup).
+> Exact link target names (`fastdds` vs `fastdds::fastdds`) need verification from Conan-generated files.
-### Bug #4 вҖ” Unsubscribe ignores object format
-This test will **fail** with current code (object entry silently skipped).
-- After initialize, push more than `kMaxIncomingQueueSize` messages via the IXWebSocket callback
-This test will **fail** with current code (all messages enqueued).
-This test should already **pass** (the validation exists). But add it for documentation and verify entry points don't compute `1.0/rate` before calling initialize.
-No test needed. Just remove the field.
+1. Create `DomainParticipant` via `DomainParticipantFactory::get_instance()->create_participant(domain_id, qos)` with TypeLookup enabled:
+1. Get topic name from discovery info
+2. Get `TypeObject` from `ITypeObjectRegistry` using type identifier from `info`
+6. Cache in `topics_` map (deduplicated by topic name)
-- Add `else if (topic_item.is_object() && topic_item.contains("name") && topic_item["name"].is_string())` branch mirroring subscribe's parsing
-### Bug #9 вҖ” Move interval computation after initialize()
-- Move `publish_period`/`publish_interval` computation and timer creation to after `server.initialize()` returns true
+- `get_topics()` вҶ’ snapshot `topics_` under shared lock, map to `TopicInfo`
-### Bug #6 вҖ” Bound the incoming queue
-- In message callback: check `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, if so log warning and return (drop message)
-### Bug #7 вҖ” Release clients_mutex_ before sending
-- `send_reply()`: copy `shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket>` under lock, release lock, then `ws->send()`
-- `publish_data()`: copy all `shared_ptr`s into a local vector under lock, release, then iterate and send
-### Bug #1 вҖ” Incremental session update during subscribe
+### Thread safety: `std::shared_mutex` (write in discovery callback, read in getters)
+1. If already subscribed вҶ’ increment ref count, return true
+5. Create Topic, Subscriber, DataReader with listener
+CDR extraction via deserialize + re-serialize (cache `DynamicPubSubType` per subscription):
-bool add_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic, double rate);
-bool remove_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic);

-In `handle_subscribe()`: after each successful `subscription_manager_->subscribe()`, immediately call `session_manager_->add_subscription()`. If `add_subscription` returns false (session gone), roll back with `subscription_manager_->unsubscribe()`.
-Remove the batch `update_subscriptions()` call at end. Keep a separate loop for rate-only updates on pre-existing topics.
+Same ref-count pattern as RTI. Close DDS entities outside lock to avoid deadlock.
-### Bug #2 вҖ” Remove failed topics from session on resume
-Collect topics that fail to re-subscribe into `failed_topics`. After the loop, remove them from the session via `session_manager_->remove_subscription()` (or batch `update_subscriptions()`).
-## Step 4: Fix existing tests that break due to mock changes
+- Same `std::chrono` event loop (1ms sleep, process_requests, publish at rate, check timeouts)
+- Same ordered shutdown: clear callback вҶ’ unsubscribe_all вҶ’ middleware shutdown

-After enhancing MockSubscriptionManager, review all tests that call `is_subscribed()` вҖ” the semantics change from set-based to ref-count-based but `is_subscribed()` should still return the same boolean result (`ref_count > 0`).
+- **`CLAUDE.md`**: Add FastDDS backend section, Conan build commands, update architecture diagram and target table
+1. Build skeleton: `conanfile.txt`, dirs, stubs, CMake вҶ’ verify compile
+2. Topic discovery: `FastDdsTopicSource` with participants + discovery + IDL
+4. Event loop: `main.cpp` with CLI + BridgeServer wiring
+5. Integration test against a real FastDDS publisher
+1. **Conan target names** вҖ” `fastdds` vs `fastdds::fastdds`, verify from generated configs
+2. **TypeObject timing** вҖ” discovery callback may fire before TypeObject is in registry; may need retry or `register_remote_type()` fallback
+3. **CDR encoding** вҖ” FastDDS defaults to XCDR2; PlotJuggler's deserializer must handle the encapsulation header
+4. **`on_data_writer_discovery` signature** вҖ” exact `info` struct fields need verification against 3.4.0 headers
+# Core tests still pass (RTI disabled, ROS2 unaffected)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fix 7 Bugs Found in Code Review

## Context

Code review revealed 7 bugs of varying severity. Two high-severity ref-counting issues (#1, #2) share a root cause: the session's subscription list can be out of sync with the subscription manager's ref counts. The plan fixes all 7, enhances the mock to detect ref-count bugs, and fixes two existing tests that test the wrong thing.

## Step 0: Enhance MockSubscriptionManager with ref counting

The current mock uses `std::set` вҖ” it can't detect ref-count leaks or underflows. Replace with `std::unordered_map<std::string, int>` ref counting.

**File**: `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp` (lines 206-247)

```cpp
// Replace std::set<std::string> subscribed_topics_ with:
std::unordered_map<std::string, int> ref_counts_;
bool underflow_detected_{false};
```

- `subscribe()`: `ref_counts_[topic]++`
- `unsubscribe()`: decrement; if already 0, set `underflow_detected_ = true` and return false
- `unsubscribe_all()`: clear `ref_counts_`
- Add helpers: `int ref_count(topic)`, `bool has_underflow()`, `bool is_subscribed(topic)` (returns `ref_counts_[topic] > 0`)

Also enhance `MockTopicSource` вҖ” add topics to both `topics_` AND `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` via a test helper so subscribe actually succeeds. Currently most subscribe tests fail because topics aren't in `known_topics_`.

## Step 1: Fix two incorrect existing tests

**File**: `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`

### Fix `UnsubscribeRemovesTopics` (line 855)

Currently subscribes to `/topic_a` and `/topic_b` which don't exist in the mock, so subscribe fails. The unsubscribe then has nothing to remove. The test is functionally identical to `UnsubscribeIgnoresUnsubscribedTopics`.

**Fix**: Add real topics to the mock before subscribing:
```cpp
mock_topic_source_->set_topics({{"/topic_a", "std_msgs/msg/String"}, {"/topic_b", "std_msgs/msg/String"}});
mock_sub_manager_->add_known_topic("/topic_a");
mock_sub_manager_->add_known_topic("/topic_b");
```
Then verify the subscribe succeeds, and the unsubscribe of `/topic_a` actually removes it (the `removed` array should contain `/topic_a`). Verify `/topic_b` remains subscribed.

### Fix `SubscribeIsAdditive` (line 897)

Same problem вҖ” topics don't exist so subscribes fail. Cannot verify additivity.

**Fix**: Add topics to mock, verify that after subscribing to `/topic_a` then `/topic_b`, both appear in the session's subscriptions (verify via a get_topics or subscribe response that shows schemas for both).

## Step 2: Write failing tests for each bug

All tests go in `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp` unless noted.

### Bug #1 вҖ” Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe
Test `DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts`:
- Add topics `/t1`, `/t2`, `/t3` to mock
- Subscribe to all three
- Immediately simulate disconnect
- Verify `ref_count("/t1") == 0`, etc. (all zero)
- Verify `has_underflow() == false`

This test will **fail** with current code if disconnect races between subscribe and update_subscriptions. In the single-threaded test it may pass, but the structural fix (incremental session update) prevents the race.

### Bug #2 вҖ” Ref-count underflow on partial resume
Test `ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession`:
- Add `/topic_a`, `/topic_b` to mock, subscribe, then pause
- Remove `/topic_b` from `MockTopicSource::topics_` (simulate topic disappearance)
- Resume вҖ” `/topic_b` subscribe should fail
- Verify `/topic_b` is no longer in session subscriptions
- Disconnect вҖ” verify `has_underflow() == false`

This test will **fail** with current code (underflow on cleanup).

### Bug #4 вҖ” Unsubscribe ignores object format
Test `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat`:
- Add `/topic_a` to mock, subscribe successfully
- Unsubscribe with `[{"name": "/topic_a"}]`
- Verify `/topic_a` is in `removed` array
- Verify `ref_count("/topic_a") == 0`

This test will **fail** with current code (object entry silently skipped).

### Bug #6 вҖ” Unbounded queue
Test goes in `tests/unit/test_websocket_middleware.cpp`:
Test `IncomingQueueBounded`:
- After initialize, push more than `kMaxIncomingQueueSize` messages via the IXWebSocket callback
- Verify only `kMaxIncomingQueueSize` are dequeued via `receive_request()`

This test will **fail** with current code (all messages enqueued).

### Bug #9 вҖ” Division by zero
Test `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate`:
- Create BridgeServer with `publish_rate = 0.0`
- Call `initialize()` вҖ” should return false

This test should already **pass** (the validation exists). But add it for documentation and verify entry points don't compute `1.0/rate` before calling initialize.

### Bug #15 вҖ” Dead code
No test needed. Just remove the field.

## Step 3: Implement fixes

### Bug #15 (simplest) вҖ” Remove dead code
**Files**: `app/include/pj_bridge/message_buffer.hpp`, `app/src/message_buffer.cpp`
- Remove `uint64_t last_read_timestamp_ns_` from header
- Remove all assignments in constructor, `move_messages()`, `clear()`

### Bug #4 вҖ” Fix unsubscribe parsing
**File**: `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, `handle_unsubscribe()` (line 378-384)
- Add `else if (topic_item.is_object() && topic_item.contains("name") && topic_item["name"].is_string())` branch mirroring subscribe's parsing

### Bug #9 вҖ” Move interval computation after initialize()
**Files**: `ros2/src/main.cpp`, `rti/src/main.cpp`
- Move `publish_period`/`publish_interval` computation and timer creation to after `server.initialize()` returns true

### Bug #6 вҖ” Bound the incoming queue
**Files**: `app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, `app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/websocket_middleware.hpp`
- Add `static constexpr size_t kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024;`
- In message callback: check `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, if so log warning and return (drop message)

### Bug #7 вҖ” Release clients_mutex_ before sending
**File**: `app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`
- `send_reply()`: copy `shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket>` under lock, release lock, then `ws->send()`
- `send_binary()`: same pattern
- `publish_data()`: copy all `shared_ptr`s into a local vector under lock, release, then iterate and send

### Bug #1 вҖ” Incremental session update during subscribe
**Files**: `app/include/pj_bridge/session_manager.hpp`, `app/src/session_manager.cpp`, `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`

Add to `SessionManager`:
```cpp
bool add_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic, double rate);
bool remove_subscription(const std::string& client_id, const std::string& topic);
```

In `handle_subscribe()`: after each successful `subscription_manager_->subscribe()`, immediately call `session_manager_->add_subscription()`. If `add_subscription` returns false (session gone), roll back with `subscription_manager_->unsubscribe()`.

Remove the batch `update_subscriptions()` call at end. Keep a separate loop for rate-only updates on pre-existing topics.

### Bug #2 вҖ” Remove failed topics from session on resume
**File**: `app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, `handle_resume()` (lines 483-497)

Collect topics that fail to re-subscribe into `failed_topics`. After the loop, remove them from the session via `session_manager_->remove_subscription()` (or batch `update_subscriptions()`).

## Step 4: Fix existing tests that break due to mock changes

After enhancing MockSubscriptionManager, review all tests that call `is_subscribed()` вҖ” the semantics change from set-based to ref-count-based but `is_subscribed()` should still return the same boolean result (`ref_count > 0`).

## Verification

```bash
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
# Regular build + test
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
# TSAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
# ASAN
colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
```
